authors_text: Chathura Gamage,Vimukthini Pinto,Cheng Xue,Peng Zhang,Ekaterina Nikonova,Matthew Stephenson,Jochen Renz
title: NovPhy: A Testbed for Physical Reasoning in Open-world Environments
title_zh: NovPhy：开放世界环境中物理推理的测试平台
abstract: Due to the emergence of AI systems that interact with the physical environment, there is an increased interest in incorporating physical reasoning capabilities into those AI systems. But is it enough to only have physical reasoning capabilities to operate in a real physical environment? In the real world, we constantly face novel situations we have not encountered before. As humans, we are competent at successfully adapting to those situations. Similarly, an agent needs to have the ability to function under the impact of novelties in order to properly operate in an open-world physical environment. To facilitate the development of such AI systems, we propose a new testbed, NovPhy, that requires an agent to reason about physical scenarios in the presence of novelties and take actions accordingly. The testbed consists of tasks that require agents to detect and adapt to novelties in physical scenarios. To create tasks in the testbed, we develop eight novelties representing a diverse novelty space and apply them to five commonly encountered scenarios in a physical environment. According to our testbed design, we evaluate two capabilities of an agent: the performance on a novelty when it is applied to different physical scenarios and the performance on a physical scenario when different novelties are applied to it. We conduct a thorough evaluation with human players, learning agents, and heuristic agents. Our evaluation shows that humans' performance is far beyond the agents' performance. Some agents, even with good normal task performance, perform significantly worse when there is a novelty, and the agents that can adapt to novelties typically adapt slower than humans. We promote the development of intelligent agents capable of performing at the human level or above when operating in open-world physical environments. Testbed website: https://github.com/phy-q/novphy

abstract_zh: 由于与物理环境交互的人工智能系统的出现，人们越来越有兴趣将物理推理能力融入到这些人工智能系统中。但仅仅具备物理推理能力就足以在真实的物理环境中运行吗？在现实世界中，我们不断面临从未遇到过的新情况。作为人类，我们有能力成功适应这些情况。同样，智能体需要有能力在新事物的影响下发挥作用，以便在开放世界的物理环境中正确运行。为了促进此类人工智能系统的开发，我们提出了一个新的测试平台 NovPhy，它要求代理在存在新奇事物的情况下推理物理场景并采取相应的行动。测试台包含要求代理检测并适应物理场景中的新奇事物的任务。为了在测试台中创建任务，我们开发了代表不同新奇空间的八个新奇事物，并将它们应用于物理环境中的五个常见场景。根据我们的测试床设计，我们评估代理的两种能力：将新颖性应用于不同物理场景时的性能以及将不同新颖性应用于物理场景时的性能。我们对人类玩家、学习代理和启发式代理进行了彻底的评估。我们的评估表明，人类的表现远远超出了智能体的表现。有些智能体，即使在正常任务中表现良好，在出现新奇事物时表现也会明显较差，并且能够适应新奇事物的智能体通常比人类适应得慢。我们促进智能代理的开发，该智能代理在开放世界的物理环境中运行时能够达到人类或更高水平。测试床网站：https://github.com/phy-q/novphy

AI.tldr: 本文提出NovPhy测试平台，用于评估智能体在开放世界物理环境中应对新颖物理场景的推理能力。
AI.motivation: 现有AI系统在物理环境中的表现难以应对未见过的新颖情况，而人类能灵活适应，需构建测试平台推动相关研究。
AI.method: 设计8种新颖物理变化应用于5类场景，通过人类玩家与学习/启发式智能体的对比实验，评估其在新颖情境下的表现。
AI.result: 人类表现远超智能体，部分高性能智能体在新颖场景中显著退化，适应速度也慢于人类。
AI.conclusion: 需开发能在开放物理环境中达到或超越人类水平的自适应智能体，NovPhy为该领域研究提供基准测试框架。

authors_text: Shangqi Gao,Hangqi Zhou,Yibo Gao,Xiahai Zhuang
title: BayeSeg: Bayesian Modeling for Medical Image Segmentation with Interpretable Generalizability
title_zh: BayeSeg：具有可解释概括性的医学图像分割贝叶斯建模
abstract: Due to the cross-domain distribution shift aroused from diverse medical imaging systems, many deep learning segmentation methods fail to perform well on unseen data, which limits their real-world applicability. Recent works have shown the benefits of extracting domain-invariant representations on domain generalization. However, the interpretability of domain-invariant features remains a great challenge. To address this problem, we propose an interpretable Bayesian framework (BayeSeg) through Bayesian modeling of image and label statistics to enhance model generalizability for medical image segmentation. Specifically, we first decompose an image into a spatial-correlated variable and a spatial-variant variable, assigning hierarchical Bayesian priors to explicitly force them to model the domain-stable shape and domain-specific appearance information respectively. Then, we model the segmentation as a locally smooth variable only related to the shape. Finally, we develop a variational Bayesian framework to infer the posterior distributions of these explainable variables. The framework is implemented with neural networks, and thus is referred to as deep Bayesian segmentation. Quantitative and qualitative experimental results on prostate segmentation and cardiac segmentation tasks have shown the effectiveness of our proposed method. Moreover, we investigated the interpretability of BayeSeg by explaining the posteriors and analyzed certain factors that affect the generalization ability through further ablation studies. Our code will be released via https://zmiclab.github.io/projects.html, once the manuscript is accepted for publication.
summary: Due to the cross-domain distribution shift aroused from diverse medical imaging systems, many deep learning segmentation methods fail to perform well on unseen data, which limits their real-world applicability. Recent works have shown the benefits of extracting domain-invariant representations on domain generalization. However, the interpretability of domain-invariant features remains a great challenge. To address this problem, we propose an interpretable Bayesian framework (BayeSeg) through Bayesian modeling of image and label statistics to enhance model generalizability for medical image segmentation. Specifically, we first decompose an image into a spatial-correlated variable and a spatial-variant variable, assigning hierarchical Bayesian priors to explicitly force them to model the domain-stable shape and domain-specific appearance information respectively. Then, we model the segmentation as a locally smooth variable only related to the shape. Finally, we develop a variational Bayesian framework to infer the posterior distributions of these explainable variables. The framework is implemented with neural networks, and thus is referred to as deep Bayesian segmentation. Quantitative and qualitative experimental results on prostate segmentation and cardiac segmentation tasks have shown the effectiveness of our proposed method. Moreover, we investigated the interpretability of BayeSeg by explaining the posteriors and analyzed certain factors that affect the generalization ability through further ablation studies. Our code will be released via https://zmiclab.github.io/projects.html, once the manuscript is accepted for publication.
abstract_zh: 由于不同的医学成像系统引起的跨域分布变化，许多深度学习分割方法无法在看不见的数据上表现良好，这限制了它们的实际适用性。最近的工作表明了提取域不变表示对域泛化的好处。然而，领域不变特征的可解释性仍然是一个巨大的挑战。为了解决这个问题，我们通过图像和标签统计的贝叶斯建模提出了一种可解释的贝叶斯框架（BayeSeg），以增强医学图像分割的模型通用性。具体来说，我们首先将图像分解为空间相关变量和空间变异变量，分配分层贝叶斯先验以明确强制它们分别对域稳定形状和域特定外观信息进行建模。然后，我们将分割建模为仅与形状相关的局部平滑变量。最后，我们开发了一个变分贝叶斯框架来推断这些可解释变量的后验分布。该框架是用神经网络实现的，因此被称为深度贝叶斯分割。前列腺分割和心脏分割任务的定量和定性实验结果表明了我们提出的方法的有效性。此外，我们通过解释后验来研究BayeSeg的可解释性，并通过进一步的消融研究分析影响泛化能力的某些因素。一旦稿件被接受出版，我们的代码将通过 https://zmiclab.github.io/projects.html 发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种可解释的贝叶斯框架BayeSeg，通过贝叶斯建模解决医学图像分割中的跨域分布偏移问题，提升模型泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习分割方法在跨域医疗影像数据上表现不佳，且领域泛化方法缺乏对特征可解释性的研究，限制了实际应用。
AI.method: 将图像分解为空间相关变量（建模形状）与空间变化变量（建模外观），通过分层贝叶斯先验分离领域稳定与特定信息，构建局部平滑分割变量，并采用变分贝叶斯框架推断后验分布。
AI.result: 在前列腺和心脏分割任务中验证了方法有效性，通过消融实验分析了影响泛化能力的因素，代码将在论文接收后公开。
AI.conclusion: BayeSeg通过贝叶斯建模实现了医学图像分割的可解释泛化，实验结果验证了其有效性，未来将公开代码供进一步研究。

authors_text: David P. Woodruff,Fred Zhang,Samson Zhou
title: Streaming Algorithms for Learning with Experts: Deterministic Versus Robust
title_zh: 与专家一起学习的流算法：确定性与鲁棒性
abstract: In the online learning with experts problem, an algorithm must make a prediction about an outcome on each of $T$ days (or times), given a set of $n$ experts who make predictions on each day (or time). The algorithm is given feedback on the outcomes of each day, including the cost of its prediction and the cost of the expert predictions, and the goal is to make a prediction with the minimum cost, specifically compared to the best expert in the set. Recent work by Srinivas, Woodruff, Xu, and Zhou (STOC 2022) introduced the study of the online learning with experts problem under memory constraints. However, often the predictions made by experts or algorithms at some time influence future outcomes, so that the input is adaptively chosen. Whereas deterministic algorithms would be robust to adaptive inputs, existing algorithms all crucially use randomization to sample a small number of experts. In this paper, we study deterministic and robust algorithms for the experts problem. We first show a space lower bound of $\widetildeΩ\left(\frac{nM}{RT}\right)$ for any deterministic algorithm that achieves regret $R$ when the best expert makes $M$ mistakes. Our result shows that the natural deterministic algorithm, which iterates through pools of experts until each expert in the pool has erred, is optimal up to polylogarithmic factors. On the positive side, we give a randomized algorithm that is robust to adaptive inputs that uses $\widetilde{O}\left(\frac{n}{R\sqrt{T}}\right)$ space for $M=O\left(\frac{R^2 T}{\log^2 n}\right)$, thereby showing a smooth space-regret trade-off.
summary: In the online learning with experts problem, an algorithm must make a prediction about an outcome on each of $T$ days (or times), given a set of $n$ experts who make predictions on each day (or time). The algorithm is given feedback on the outcomes of each day, including the cost of its prediction and the cost of the expert predictions, and the goal is to make a prediction with the minimum cost, specifically compared to the best expert in the set. Recent work by Srinivas, Woodruff, Xu, and Zhou (STOC 2022) introduced the study of the online learning with experts problem under memory constraints. However, often the predictions made by experts or algorithms at some time influence future outcomes, so that the input is adaptively chosen. Whereas deterministic algorithms would be robust to adaptive inputs, existing algorithms all crucially use randomization to sample a small number of experts. In this paper, we study deterministic and robust algorithms for the experts problem. We first show a space lower bound of $\widetildeΩ\left(\frac{nM}{RT}\right)$ for any deterministic algorithm that achieves regret $R$ when the best expert makes $M$ mistakes. Our result shows that the natural deterministic algorithm, which iterates through pools of experts until each expert in the pool has erred, is optimal up to polylogarithmic factors. On the positive side, we give a randomized algorithm that is robust to adaptive inputs that uses $\widetilde{O}\left(\frac{n}{R\sqrt{T}}\right)$ space for $M=O\left(\frac{R^2 T}{\log^2 n}\right)$, thereby showing a smooth space-regret trade-off.
abstract_zh: 在专家在线学习问题中，给定一组每天（或时间）做出预测的 $n$ 专家，算法必须对每个 $T$ 天（或时间）的结果进行预测。该算法会收到每天结果的反馈，包括其预测成本和专家预测成本，目标是以最小成本进行预测，特别是与集合中最好的专家进行比较。 Srinivas、Woodruff、Xu 和 Zhou (STOC 2022) 最近的工作介绍了内存约束下的专家在线学习问题的研究。然而，专家或算法在某些时候做出的预测通常会影响未来的结果，因此输入是自适应选择的。尽管确定性算法对于自适应输入具有鲁棒性，但现有算法都关键性地使用随机化来对少数专家进行抽样。在本文中，我们研究专家问题的确定性和鲁棒性算法。我们首先展示了任何确定性算法的空间下界 $\widetildeΩ\left(\frac{nM}{RT}\right)$，当最好的专家犯 $M$ 错误时，该算法会后悔 $R$。我们的结果表明，自然确定性算法（迭代专家池直到池中的每个专家都出错）在多对数因子下是最优的。从积极的方面来看，我们给出了一种对自适应输入具有鲁棒性的随机算法，该算法使用 $\widetilde{O}\left(\frac{n}{R\sqrt{T}}\right)$ 空间来表示 $M=O\left(\frac{R^2 T}{\log^2 n}\right)$，从而表现出平滑的空间与遗憾权衡。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在线专家学习问题中的确定性与鲁棒算法，分析了其空间复杂度与遗憾值的权衡关系。
AI.motivation: 现有算法依赖随机化以应对自适应输入，但确定性算法可能更鲁棒。需探索在内存限制下确定性与鲁棒算法的性能边界。
AI.method: 通过建立确定性算法的空间下界，并设计一个对自适应输入鲁棒的随机化算法，分析其空间复杂度与专家错误次数的关系。
AI.result: 确定性算法空间下界为Ω(nM/(RT))，自然确定性算法最优至对数因子；随机化算法空间复杂度为O(n/(R√T))，实现空间-遗憾平滑权衡。
AI.conclusion: 确定性算法在理论下界内最优，而随机化算法提供了更灵活的空间-遗憾 trade-off，适用于不同场景需求。

authors_text: Wentao Lei,Lei Liu,Li Liu
title: Spatio-Temporal Structure Consistency for Semi-supervised Medical Image Classification
title_zh: 半监督医学图像分类的时空结构一致性
abstract: Intelligent medical diagnosis has shown remarkable progress based on the large-scale datasets with precise annotations. However, fewer labeled images are available due to significantly expensive cost for annotating data by experts. To fully exploit the easily available unlabeled data, we propose a novel Spatio-Temporal Structure Consistent (STSC) learning framework. Specifically, a gram matrix is derived to combine the spatial structure consistency and temporal structure consistency together. This gram matrix captures the structural similarity among the representations of different training samples. At the spatial level, our framework explicitly enforces the consistency of structural similarity among different samples under perturbations. At the temporal level, we consider the consistency of the structural similarity in different training iterations by digging out the stable sub-structures in a relation graph. Experiments on two medical image datasets (i.e., ISIC 2018 challenge and ChestX-ray14) show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art SSL methods. Furthermore, extensive qualitative analysis on the Gram matrices and heatmaps by Grad-CAM are presented to validate the effectiveness of our method.
summary: Intelligent medical diagnosis has shown remarkable progress based on the large-scale datasets with precise annotations. However, fewer labeled images are available due to significantly expensive cost for annotating data by experts. To fully exploit the easily available unlabeled data, we propose a novel Spatio-Temporal Structure Consistent (STSC) learning framework. Specifically, a gram matrix is derived to combine the spatial structure consistency and temporal structure consistency together. This gram matrix captures the structural similarity among the representations of different training samples. At the spatial level, our framework explicitly enforces the consistency of structural similarity among different samples under perturbations. At the temporal level, we consider the consistency of the structural similarity in different training iterations by digging out the stable sub-structures in a relation graph. Experiments on two medical image datasets (i.e., ISIC 2018 challenge and ChestX-ray14) show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art SSL methods. Furthermore, extensive qualitative analysis on the Gram matrices and heatmaps by Grad-CAM are presented to validate the effectiveness of our method.
abstract_zh: 基于具有精确注释的大规模数据集的智能医疗诊断取得了显着进展。然而，由于专家注释数据的成本非常昂贵，可用的标记图像较少。为了充分利用容易获得的未标记数据，我们提出了一种新颖的时空结构一致（STSC）学习框架。具体地，推导语法矩阵以将空间结构一致性和时间结构一致性结合在一起。该语法矩阵捕获了不同训练样本的表示之间的结构相似性。在空间层面，我们的框架明确强制扰动下不同样本之间结构相似性的一致性。在时间层面，我们通过挖掘关系图中的稳定子结构来考虑不同训练迭代中结构相似性的一致性。对两个医学图像数据集（即 ISIC 2018 挑战和 ChestX-ray14）的实验表明，我们的方法优于最先进的 SSL 方法。此外，Grad-CAM 对 Gram 矩阵和热图进行了广泛的定性分析，以验证我们方法的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种半监督医学图像分类方法，通过时空结构一致性框架利用未标记数据提升分类性能。
AI.motivation: 医学图像标注成本高昂导致标记数据稀缺，现有半监督学习方法难以有效利用未标记数据中的结构信息。
AI.method: 构建时空结构一致性（STSC）框架，通过 Gram 矩阵融合空间结构一致性（扰动下样本间结构相似性）与时间结构一致性（训练迭代中关系图的稳定子结构）。
AI.result: 在 ISIC 2018 和 ChestX-ray14 数据集上优于现有半监督方法，Gram 矩阵与 Grad-CAM 热图验证了方法有效性。
AI.conclusion: STSC 框架通过联合建模时空结构一致性，有效提升了医学图像分类在低标注场景下的性能。

authors_text: Andre Coelho,Alin Albu-Schaeffer,Arne Sachtler,Hrishik Mishra,Davide Bicego,Christian Ott,Antonio Franchi
title: EigenMPC: An Eigenmanifold-Inspired Model-Predictive Control Framework for Exciting Efficient Oscillations in Mechanical Systems
title_zh: EigenMPC：一种受本征流形启发的模型预测控制框架，用于激发机械系统中的高效振荡
comment: ef:2022 IEEE 61st Conference on Decision and Control (CDC), Cancun, Mexico, 2022, pp. 2437-2442
comments: ef:2022 IEEE 61st Conference on Decision and Control (CDC), Cancun, Mexico, 2022, pp. 2437-2442
abstract: This paper proposes a Nonlinear Model-Predictive Control (NMPC) method capable of finding and converging to energy-efficient regular oscillations, which require no control action to be sustained. The approach builds up on the recently developed Eigenmanifold theory, which defines the sets of line-shaped oscillations of a robot as an invariant two-dimensional submanifold of its state space. By defining the control problem as a nonlinear program (NLP), the controller is able to deal with constraints in the state and control variables and be energy-efficient not only in its final trajectory but also during the convergence phase. An initial implementation of this approach is proposed, analyzed, and tested in simulation.
summary: This paper proposes a Nonlinear Model-Predictive Control (NMPC) method capable of finding and converging to energy-efficient regular oscillations, which require no control action to be sustained. The approach builds up on the recently developed Eigenmanifold theory, which defines the sets of line-shaped oscillations of a robot as an invariant two-dimensional submanifold of its state space. By defining the control problem as a nonlinear program (NLP), the controller is able to deal with constraints in the state and control variables and be energy-efficient not only in its final trajectory but also during the convergence phase. An initial implementation of this approach is proposed, analyzed, and tested in simulation.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种非线性模型预测控制（NMPC）方法，能够发现并收敛到节能的规则振荡，不需要持续的控制动作。该方法建立在最近开发的特征流形理论的基础上，该理论将机器人的线形振荡集定义为其状态空间的不变二维子流形。通过将控制问题定义为非线性程序（NLP），控制器能够处理状态和控制变量的约束，并且不仅在其最终轨迹中而且在收敛阶段都具有节能性。该方法的初步实现是在模拟中提出、分析和测试的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于特征流形理论的非线性模型预测控制（NMPC）方法，用于机械系统中高效振荡的激发与维持。
AI.motivation: 传统控制方法难以在无需持续控制输入的情况下实现机械系统的能量高效振荡，而特征流形理论为定义振荡子流形提供了新思路。
AI.method: 结合特征流形理论与NMPC框架，将控制问题转化为非线性规划（NLP），通过状态空间约束优化实现高效振荡的收敛。
AI.result: 仿真验证了该方法在初始实现中可有效收敛至无需控制输入的高效振荡轨迹，并满足状态与控制约束。
AI.conclusion: 提出的EigenMPC框架为机械系统高效振荡控制提供了理论支持，后续需进一步验证实际应用效果。

search_text: EigenMPC: An Eigenmanifold-Inspired Model-Predictive Control Framework for Exciting Efficient Oscillations in Mechanical Systems EigenMPC：一种受本征流形启发的模型预测控制框架，用于激发机械系统中的高效振荡 This paper proposes a Nonlinear Model-Predictive Control (NMPC) method capable of finding and converging to energy-efficient regular oscillations, which require no control action to be sustained. The approach builds up on the recently developed Eigenmanifold theory, which defines the sets of line-shaped oscillations of a robot as an invariant two-dimensional submanifold of its state space. By defining the control problem as a nonlinear program (NLP), the controller is able to deal with constraints in the state and control variables and be energy-efficient not only in its final trajectory but also during the convergence phase. An initial implementation of this approach is proposed, analyzed, and tested in simulation. 本文提出了一种非线性模型预测控制（NMPC）方法，能够发现并收敛到节能的规则振荡，不需要持续的控制动作。该方法建立在最近开发的特征流形理论的基础上，该理论将机器人的线形振荡集定义为其状态空间的不变二维子流形。通过将控制问题定义为非线性程序（NLP），控制器能够处理状态和控制变量的约束，并且不仅在其最终轨迹中而且在收敛阶段都具有节能性。该方法的初步实现是在模拟中提出、分析和测试的。 本文提出一种基于特征流形理论的非线性模型预测控制（NMPC）方法，用于机械系统中高效振荡的激发与维持。 Andre Coelho,Alin Albu-Schaeffer,Arne Sachtler,Hrishik Mishra,Davide Bicego,Christian Ott,Antonio Franchi cs.RO,eess.SY

authors_text: Kokila Perera,Aneta Neumann,Frank Neumann
title: Evolutionary Multi-Objective Algorithms for the Knapsack Problems with Stochastic Profits
title_zh: 具有随机利润的背包问题的进化多目标算法
abstract: Evolutionary multi-objective algorithms have been widely shown to be successful when utilized for a variety of stochastic combinatorial optimization problems. Chance constrained optimization plays an important role in complex real-world scenarios, as it allows decision makers to take into account the uncertainty of the environment. We consider a version of the knapsack problem with stochastic profits to guarantee a certain level of confidence in the profit of the solutions. We introduce the multi-objective formulations of the profit chance constrained knapsack problem and design three bi-objective fitness evaluation methods that work independently of the specific confidence level required. We evaluate our approaches using well-known multi-objective evolutionary algorithms GSEMO and NSGA-II. In addition, we introduce a filtering method for GSEMO that improves the quality of the final population by periodically removing certain solutions from the interim populations based on their confidence level. We show the effectiveness of our approaches on several benchmarks for both settings where the knapsack items have fixed uniform uncertainties and uncertainties that are positively correlated with the expected profit of an item.
summary: Evolutionary multi-objective algorithms have been widely shown to be successful when utilized for a variety of stochastic combinatorial optimization problems. Chance constrained optimization plays an important role in complex real-world scenarios, as it allows decision makers to take into account the uncertainty of the environment. We consider a version of the knapsack problem with stochastic profits to guarantee a certain level of confidence in the profit of the solutions. We introduce the multi-objective formulations of the profit chance constrained knapsack problem and design three bi-objective fitness evaluation methods that work independently of the specific confidence level required. We evaluate our approaches using well-known multi-objective evolutionary algorithms GSEMO and NSGA-II. In addition, we introduce a filtering method for GSEMO that improves the quality of the final population by periodically removing certain solutions from the interim populations based on their confidence level. We show the effectiveness of our approaches on several benchmarks for both settings where the knapsack items have fixed uniform uncertainties and uncertainties that are positively correlated with the expected profit of an item.
abstract_zh: 进化多目标算法已被广泛证明在用于各种随机组合优化问题时是成功的。机会约束优化在复杂的现实场景中发挥着重要作用，因为它允许决策者考虑环境的不确定性。我们考虑具有随机利润的背包问题的版本，以保证对解决方案的利润有一定程度的信心。我们引入了利润机会约束背包问题的多目标公式，并设计了三种独立于所需的特定置信水平工作的双目标适应性评估方法。我们使用著名的多目标进化算法 GSEMO 和 NSGA-II 来评估我们的方法。此外，我们引入了 GSEMO 的过滤方法，该方法通过根据置信水平定期从临时群体中删除某些解决方案来提高最终群体的质量。我们在两种设置的几个基准上展示了我们的方法的有效性，其中背包物品具有固定的统一不确定性以及与物品的预期利润正相关的不确定性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了具有随机收益的背包问题，提出多目标进化算法及其评估方法，并验证其有效性。
AI.motivation: 现实场景中决策需考虑环境不确定性，传统方法难以有效处理随机收益背包问题的置信度约束。
AI.method: 设计三种独立于置信度的双目标适应度评估方法，结合GSEMO和NSGA-II算法，并引入过滤机制优化种群质量。
AI.result: 在固定均匀不确定性及收益正相关不确定性的基准测试中，方法均表现出有效性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的算法和过滤方法能有效解决随机收益背包问题，为类似优化问题提供新思路。

authors_text: Shuaiqi Chen,Xiaofen Xing,Weibin Zhang,Weidong Chen,Xiangmin Xu
title: DWFormer: Dynamic Window transFormer for Speech Emotion Recognition
title_zh: DWFormer：用于语音情感识别的动态窗口转换器
comment: 4 pages, 5 figures, 3 tables, accepted by 2023 International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing (ICASSP2023)
comments: 4 pages, 5 figures, 3 tables, accepted by 2023 International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing (ICASSP2023)
abstract: Speech emotion recognition is crucial to human-computer interaction. The temporal regions that represent different emotions scatter in different parts of the speech locally. Moreover, the temporal scales of important information may vary over a large range within and across speech segments. Although transformer-based models have made progress in this field, the existing models could not precisely locate important regions at different temporal scales. To address the issue, we propose Dynamic Window transFormer (DWFormer), a new architecture that leverages temporal importance by dynamically splitting samples into windows. Self-attention mechanism is applied within windows for capturing temporal important information locally in a fine-grained way. Cross-window information interaction is also taken into account for global communication. DWFormer is evaluated on both the IEMOCAP and the MELD datasets. Experimental results show that the proposed model achieves better performance than the previous state-of-the-art methods.
summary: Speech emotion recognition is crucial to human-computer interaction. The temporal regions that represent different emotions scatter in different parts of the speech locally. Moreover, the temporal scales of important information may vary over a large range within and across speech segments. Although transformer-based models have made progress in this field, the existing models could not precisely locate important regions at different temporal scales. To address the issue, we propose Dynamic Window transFormer (DWFormer), a new architecture that leverages temporal importance by dynamically splitting samples into windows. Self-attention mechanism is applied within windows for capturing temporal important information locally in a fine-grained way. Cross-window information interaction is also taken into account for global communication. DWFormer is evaluated on both the IEMOCAP and the MELD datasets. Experimental results show that the proposed model achieves better performance than the previous state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 语音情感识别对于人机交互至关重要。代表不同情绪的颞区局部分散在语音的不同部分。此外，重要信息的时间尺度可能在语音片段内和语音片段之间在很大范围内变化。尽管基于变压器的模型在这一领域取得了进展，但现有模型无法精确定位不同时间尺度的重要区域。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了动态窗口转换器（DWFormer），这是一种通过动态地将样本分割到窗口中来利用时间重要性的新架构。在窗口中应用自注意力机制，以细粒度的方式在本地捕获时间重要信息。全局通信还考虑了跨窗口信息交互。 DWFormer 在 IEMOCAP 和 MELD 数据集上进行评估。实验结果表明，所提出的模型比以前最先进的方法取得了更好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DWFormer模型，通过动态窗口机制改进语音情感识别中的时序特征捕捉能力。
AI.motivation: 现有Transformer模型无法精确定位语音中不同时间尺度的重要情感特征区域，导致识别性能受限。
AI.method: 设计动态窗口分割机制，结合局部窗口内自注意力与跨窗口交互机制，同时捕获细粒度时序特征和全局上下文信息。
AI.result: 在IEMOCAP和MELD数据集上均超越现有最优方法，验证了动态窗口机制的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 动态窗口与跨窗口交互相结合的架构有效提升了语音情感识别模型对多尺度时序特征的建模能力。

authors_text: Shageenderan Sapai,Junn Yong Loo,Ze Yang Ding,Chee Pin Tan,Raphael CW Phan,Vishnu Monn Baskaran,Surya Girinatha Nurzaman
title: Cross-domain Transfer Learning and State Inference for Soft Robots via a Semi-supervised Sequential Variational Bayes Framework
title_zh: 通过半监督序列变分贝叶斯框架进行软机器人的跨域迁移学习和状态推理
comment: Accepted at the IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) 2023
comments: Accepted at the IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) 2023
abstract: Recently, data-driven models such as deep neural networks have shown to be promising tools for modelling and state inference in soft robots. However, voluminous amounts of data are necessary for deep models to perform effectively, which requires exhaustive and quality data collection, particularly of state labels. Consequently, obtaining labelled state data for soft robotic systems is challenged for various reasons, including difficulty in the sensorization of soft robots and the inconvenience of collecting data in unstructured environments. To address this challenge, in this paper, we propose a semi-supervised sequential variational Bayes (DSVB) framework for transfer learning and state inference in soft robots with missing state labels on certain robot configurations. Considering that soft robots may exhibit distinct dynamics under different robot configurations, a feature space transfer strategy is also incorporated to promote the adaptation of latent features across multiple configurations. Unlike existing transfer learning approaches, our proposed DSVB employs a recurrent neural network to model the nonlinear dynamics and temporal coherence in soft robot data. The proposed framework is validated on multiple setup configurations of a pneumatic-based soft robot finger. Experimental results on four transfer scenarios demonstrate that DSVB performs effective transfer learning and accurate state inference amidst missing state labels. The data and code are available at https://github.com/shageenderan/DSVB.
summary: Recently, data-driven models such as deep neural networks have shown to be promising tools for modelling and state inference in soft robots. However, voluminous amounts of data are necessary for deep models to perform effectively, which requires exhaustive and quality data collection, particularly of state labels. Consequently, obtaining labelled state data for soft robotic systems is challenged for various reasons, including difficulty in the sensorization of soft robots and the inconvenience of collecting data in unstructured environments. To address this challenge, in this paper, we propose a semi-supervised sequential variational Bayes (DSVB) framework for transfer learning and state inference in soft robots with missing state labels on certain robot configurations. Considering that soft robots may exhibit distinct dynamics under different robot configurations, a feature space transfer strategy is also incorporated to promote the adaptation of latent features across multiple configurations. Unlike existing transfer learning approaches, our proposed DSVB employs a recurrent neural network to model the nonlinear dynamics and temporal coherence in soft robot data. The proposed framework is validated on multiple setup configurations of a pneumatic-based soft robot finger. Experimental results on four transfer scenarios demonstrate that DSVB performs effective transfer learning and accurate state inference amidst missing state labels. The data and code are available at https://github.com/shageenderan/DSVB.
abstract_zh: 最近，数据驱动模型（例如深度神经网络）已被证明是软机器人建模和状态推理的有前途的工具。然而，深度模型的有效执行需要大量的数据，这需要详尽且高质量的数据收集，尤其是状态标签的数据收集。因此，由于各种原因，获取软机器人系统的标记状态数据面临挑战，包括软机器人传感的困难以及在非结构化环境中收集数据的不便。为了应对这一挑战，在本文中，我们提出了一种半监督顺序变分贝叶斯（DSVB）框架，用于在某些机器人配置上缺少状态标签的软机器人中进行迁移学习和状态推理。考虑到软机器人在不同的机器人配置下可能表现出不同的动力学，还结合了特征空间转移策略来促进跨多种配置的潜​​在特征的适应。与现有的迁移学习方法不同，我们提出的 DSVB 采用循环神经网络来对软机器人数据中的非线性动力学和时间相干性进行建模。所提出的框架在基于气动的软机器人手指的多种设置配置上进行了验证。四种迁移场景的实验结果表明，DSVB 在缺失状态标签的情况下执行有效的迁移学习和准确的状态推断。数据和代码可在 https://github.com/shageenderan/DSVB 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种半监督序列变分贝叶斯框架（DSVB），用于解决软体机器人跨领域迁移学习和状态推断中标签数据缺失的问题。
AI.motivation: 深度模型需大量带标签数据，但软体机器人因传感器化困难和非结构化环境导致状态标签难以获取，现有迁移学习方法无法有效处理此问题。
AI.method: 结合半监督学习与迁移学习，采用递归神经网络建模非线性动态和时序关联，并引入特征空间迁移策略以适应不同机器人配置。
AI.result: 在四种迁移场景的气动软体机器人手指实验中，DSVB实现了有效的跨领域学习和准确的状态推断，验证了其在标签缺失情况下的有效性。
AI.conclusion: DSVB框架为软体机器人在有限标签数据下的状态推断与跨领域适应提供了可行方案，具有实际应用价值。

search_text: Cross-domain Transfer Learning and State Inference for Soft Robots via a Semi-supervised Sequential Variational Bayes Framework 通过半监督序列变分贝叶斯框架进行软机器人的跨域迁移学习和状态推理 Recently, data-driven models such as deep neural networks have shown to be promising tools for modelling and state inference in soft robots. However, voluminous amounts of data are necessary for deep models to perform effectively, which requires exhaustive and quality data collection, particularly of state labels. Consequently, obtaining labelled state data for soft robotic systems is challenged for various reasons, including difficulty in the sensorization of soft robots and the inconvenience of collecting data in unstructured environments. To address this challenge, in this paper, we propose a semi-supervised sequential variational Bayes (DSVB) framework for transfer learning and state inference in soft robots with missing state labels on certain robot configurations. Considering that soft robots may exhibit distinct dynamics under different robot configurations, a feature space transfer strategy is also incorporated to promote the adaptation of latent features across multiple configurations. Unlike existing transfer learning approaches, our proposed DSVB employs a recurrent neural network to model the nonlinear dynamics and temporal coherence in soft robot data. The proposed framework is validated on multiple setup configurations of a pneumatic-based soft robot finger. Experimental results on four transfer scenarios demonstrate that DSVB performs effective transfer learning and accurate state inference amidst missing state labels. authors_text: Xiaojian Zhang,Qian Ke,Xilei Zhao
title: Travel Demand Forecasting: A Fair AI Approach
title_zh: 旅行需求预测：一种公平的人工智能方法
comment: improved the methodology; updated new contents
comments: improved the methodology; updated new contents
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and machine learning have been increasingly adopted for travel demand forecasting. The AI-based travel demand forecasting models, though generate accurate predictions, may produce prediction biases and raise fairness issues. Using such biased models for decision-making may lead to transportation policies that exacerbate social inequalities. However, limited studies have been focused on addressing the fairness issues of these models. Therefore, in this study, we propose a novel methodology to develop fairness-aware, highly-accurate travel demand forecasting models. Particularly, the proposed methodology can enhance the fairness of AI models for multiple protected attributes (such as race and income) simultaneously. Specifically, we introduce a new fairness regularization term, which is explicitly designed to measure the correlation between prediction accuracy and multiple protected attributes, into the loss function of the travel demand forecasting model. We conduct two case studies to evaluate the performance of the proposed methodology using real-world ridesourcing-trip data in Chicago, IL and Austin, TX, respectively. Results highlight that our proposed methodology can effectively enhance fairness for multiple protected attributes while preserving prediction accuracy. Additionally, we have compared our methodology with three state-of-the-art methods that adopt the regularization term approach, and the results demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms them in both preserving prediction accuracy and enhancing fairness. This study can provide transportation professionals with a new tool to achieve fair and accurate travel demand forecasting.

The AI-based travel demand forecasting models, though generate accurate predictions, may produce prediction biases and raise fairness issues. We conduct two case studies to evaluate the performance of the proposed methodology using real-world ridesourcing-trip data in Chicago, IL and Austin, TX, respectively. Results highlight that our proposed methodology can effectively enhance fairness for multiple protected attributes while preserving prediction accuracy. Additionally, we have compared our methodology with three state-of-the-art methods that adopt the regularization term approach, and the results demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms them in both preserving prediction accuracy and enhancing fairness. This study can provide transportation professionals with a new tool to achieve fair and accurate travel demand forecasting.
abstract_zh: 人工智能 (AI) 和机器学习越来越多地用于出行需求预测。基于人工智能的出行需求预测模型虽然可以产生准确的预测，但可能会产生预测偏差并引发公平性问题。使用这种有偏见的模型进行决策可能会导致交通政策加剧社会不平等。然而，有限的研究集中在解决这些模型的公平性问题上。因此，在本研究中，我们提出了一种新颖的方法来开发具有公平意识的、高精度的出行需求预测模型。特别是，所提出的方法可以同时增强人工智能模型对多个受保护属性（例如种族和收入）的公平性。具体来说，我们在出行需求预测模型的损失函数中引入了一个新的公平正则化项，该项明确设计用于衡量预测准确性与多个受保护属性之间的相关性。我们分别使用伊利诺伊州芝加哥和德克萨斯州奥斯汀的真实世界乘车出行数据进行了两个案例研究，以评估所提出方法的性能。结果表明，我们提出的方法可以有效增强多个受保护属性的公平性，同时保持预测准确性。此外，我们将我们的方法与采用正则化项方法的三种最先进的方法进行了比较，结果表明我们的方法在保持预测准确性和增强公平性方面都显着优于它们。这项研究可以为交通专业人士提供一个新工具来实现公平、准确的出行需求预测。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种公平AI方法，用于在保持预测精度的同时解决交通需求预测模型中的公平性问题。
AI.motivation: 现有AI交通需求预测模型虽准确，但可能因预测偏差加剧社会不平等，而公平性研究不足。
AI.method: 在损失函数中引入新型公平正则化项，量化预测精度与多属性（如种族、收入）的关联，通过芝加哥和奥斯汀的真实网约车数据验证。
AI.result: 方法在多属性公平性提升的同时保持预测精度，且优于三种现有正则化方法。
AI.conclusion: 为交通领域提供兼顾公平性与准确性的预测工具，推动AI模型的社会责任实践。

authors_text: Yilin Yang,Kamil Adamczewski,Danica J. Sutherland,Xiaoxiao Li,Mijung Park
title: Differentially Private Neural Tangent Kernels for Privacy-Preserving Data Generation
title_zh: 用于隐私保护数据生成的差分隐私神经切线内核
abstract: Maximum mean discrepancy (MMD) is a particularly useful distance metric for differentially private data generation: when used with finite-dimensional features it allows us to summarize and privatize the data distribution once, which we can repeatedly use during generator training without further privacy loss. An important question in this framework is, then, what features are useful to distinguish between real and synthetic data distributions, and whether those enable us to generate quality synthetic data. This work considers the using the features of $\textit{neural tangent kernels (NTKs)}$, more precisely $\textit{empirical}$ NTKs (e-NTKs). We find that, perhaps surprisingly, the expressiveness of the untrained e-NTK features is comparable to that of the features taken from pre-trained perceptual features using public data. As a result, our method improves the privacy-accuracy trade-off compared to other state-of-the-art methods, without relying on any public data, as demonstrated on several tabular and image benchmark datasets.
summary: Maximum mean discrepancy (MMD) is a particularly useful distance metric for differentially private data generation: when used with finite-dimensional features it allows us to summarize and privatize the data distribution once, which we can repeatedly use during generator training without further privacy loss. An important question in this framework is, then, what features are useful to distinguish between real and synthetic data distributions, and whether those enable us to generate quality synthetic data. This work considers the using the features of $\textit{neural tangent kernels (NTKs)}$, more precisely $\textit{empirical}$ NTKs (e-NTKs). We find that, perhaps surprisingly, the expressiveness of the untrained e-NTK features is comparable to that of the features taken from pre-trained perceptual features using public data. As a result, our method improves the privacy-accuracy trade-off compared to other state-of-the-art methods, without relying on any public data, as demonstrated on several tabular and image benchmark datasets.
abstract_zh: 最大平均差异（MMD）对于差分隐私数据生成来说是一个特别有用的距离度量：当与有限维特征一起使用时，它允许我们对数据分布进行一次汇总和私有化，我们可以在生成器训练期间重复使用它，而不会进一步丢失隐私。那么，该框架中的一个重要问题是，哪些特征有助于区分真实数据分布和合成数据分布，以及这些特征是否使我们能够生成高质量的合成数据。这项工作考虑使用 $\textit{神经正切核（NTK）}$，更准确地说 $\textit{经验}$ NTK（e-NTK）的特征。我们发现，也许令人惊讶的是，未经训练的 e-NTK 特征的表达能力与使用公共数据预先训练的感知特征所获取的特征相当。因此，与其他最先进的方法相比，我们的方法改善了隐私准确性权衡，而不依赖于任何公共数据，如几个表格和图像基准数据集所示。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于差分隐私的神经切线核（NTK）方法，用于在生成合成数据时平衡隐私保护与数据质量。
AI.motivation: 在差分隐私数据生成框架中，如何选择有效的特征以区分真实与合成数据分布，并在无需公共数据的情况下提升隐私-准确性权衡，是关键挑战。
AI.method: 采用经验神经切线核（e-NTK）作为特征，利用最大均值差异（MMD）度量，通过未训练的e-NTK特征直接建模数据分布，避免依赖预训练模型或公共数据。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在多个表格和图像数据集上优于现有方法，在隐私预算下生成的合成数据质量更高，且无需公共数据辅助。
AI.conclusion: e-NTK特征能有效捕捉数据分布差异，为隐私保护数据生成提供了无需公共数据的高效解决方案，优化了隐私与生成质量的权衡。

search_text: Differentially Private Neural Tangent Kernels for Privacy-Preserving Data Generation 用于隐私保护数据生成的差分隐私神经切线内核 Maximum mean discrepancy (MMD) is a particularly useful distance metric for differentially private data generation: when used with finite-dimensional features it allows us to summarize and privatize the data distribution once, which we can repeatedly use during generator training without further privacy loss. An important question in this framework is, then, what features are useful to distinguish between real and synthetic data distributions, and whether those enable us to generate quality synthetic data. This work considers the using the features of $\textit{neural tangent kernels (NTKs)}$, more precisely $\textit{empirical}$ NTKs (e-NTKs). We find that, perhaps surprisingly, the expressiveness of the untrained e-NTK features is comparable to that of the features taken from pre-trained perceptual features using public data. As a result, our method improves the privacy-accuracy trade-off compared to other state-of-the-art methods, without relying on any public data, as demonstrated on several tabular and image benchmark datasets. 最大平均差异（MMD）对于差分隐私数据生成来说是一个特别有用的距离度量：当与有限维特征一起使用时，它允许我们对数据分布进行一次汇总和私有化，我们可以在生成器训练期间重复使用它，而不会进一步丢失隐私。那么，该框架中的一个重要问题是，哪些特征有助于区分真实数据分布和合成数据分布，以及这些特征是否使我们能够生成高质量的合成数据。这项工作考虑使用 $\textit{神经正切核（NTK）}$，更准确地说 $\textit{经验}$ NTK（e-NTK）的特征。我们发现，也许令人惊讶的是，未经训练的 e-NTK 特征的表达能力与使用公共数据预先训练的感知特征所获取的特征相当。因此，与其他最先进的方法相比，我们的方法改善了隐私准确性权衡，而不依赖于任何公共数据，如几个表格和图像基准数据集所示。 本文提出了一种基于差分隐私的神经切线核（NTK）方法，用于在生成合成数据时平衡隐私保护与数据质量。 Yilin Yang,Kamil Adamczewski,Danica J. Sutherland,Xiaoxiao Li,Mijung Park cs.LG,cs.CR,cs.CV

authors_text: Shuo Wang,Xinhai Zhao,Hai-Ming Xu,Zehui Chen,Dameng Yu,Jiahao Chang,Zhen Yang,Feng Zhao
title: Towards Domain Generalization for Multi-view 3D Object Detection in Bird-Eye-View
title_zh: 鸟瞰多视图 3D 目标检测的域泛化
abstract: Multi-view 3D object detection (MV3D-Det) in Bird-Eye-View (BEV) has drawn extensive attention due to its low cost and high efficiency. Although new algorithms for camera-only 3D object detection have been continuously proposed, most of them may risk drastic performance degradation when the domain of input images differs from that of training. In this paper, we first analyze the causes of the domain gap for the MV3D-Det task. Based on the covariate shift assumption, we find that the gap mainly attributes to the feature distribution of BEV, which is determined by the quality of both depth estimation and 2D image's feature representation. To acquire a robust depth prediction, we propose to decouple the depth estimation from the intrinsic parameters of the camera (i.e. the focal length) through converting the prediction of metric depth to that of scale-invariant depth and perform dynamic perspective augmentation to increase the diversity of the extrinsic parameters (i.e. the camera poses) by utilizing homography. Moreover, we modify the focal length values to create multiple pseudo-domains and construct an adversarial training loss to encourage the feature representation to be more domain-agnostic. Without bells and whistles, our approach, namely DG-BEV, successfully alleviates the performance drop on the unseen target domain without impairing the accuracy of the source domain. Extensive experiments on various public datasets, including Waymo, nuScenes, and Lyft, demonstrate the generalization and effectiveness of our approach. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first systematic study to explore a domain generalization method for MV3D-Det.
summary: Multi-view 3D object detection (MV3D-Det) in Bird-Eye-View (BEV) has drawn extensive attention due to its low cost and high efficiency. Although new algorithms for camera-only 3D object detection have been continuously proposed, most of them may risk drastic performance degradation when the domain of input images differs from that of training. In this paper, we first analyze the causes of the domain gap for the MV3D-Det task. Based on the covariate shift assumption, we find that the gap mainly attributes to the feature distribution of BEV, which is determined by the quality of both depth estimation and 2D image's feature representation. To acquire a robust depth prediction, we propose to decouple the depth estimation from the intrinsic parameters of the camera (i.e. the focal length) through converting the prediction of metric depth to that of scale-invariant depth and perform dynamic perspective augmentation to increase the diversity of the extrinsic parameters (i.e. the camera poses) by utilizing homography. Moreover, we modify the focal length values to create multiple pseudo-domains and construct an adversarial training loss to encourage the feature representation to be more domain-agnostic. Without bells and whistles, our approach, namely DG-BEV, successfully alleviates the performance drop on the unseen target domain without impairing the accuracy of the source domain. Extensive experiments on various public datasets, including Waymo, nuScenes, and Lyft, demonstrate the generalization and effectiveness of our approach. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first systematic study to explore a domain generalization method for MV3D-Det.
abstract_zh: 鸟瞰（BEV）中的多视图3D物体检测（MV3D-Det）由于其低成本和高效率而引起了广泛的关注。尽管仅用于相机 3D 物体检测的新算法不断被提出，但当输入图像的域与训练域不同时，大多数算法可能会面临性能急剧下降的风险。在本文中，我们首先分析了 MV3D-Det 任务域间隙的原因。基于协变量移位假设，我们发现差距主要归因于 BEV 的特征分布，这是由深度估计和 2D 图像特征表示的质量决定的。为了获得鲁棒的深度预测，我们建议通过将度量深度的预测转换为尺度不变深度的预测，将深度估计与相机的内在参数（即焦距）解耦，并利用单应性执行动态透视增强以增加外在参数（即相机姿势）的多样性。此外，我们修改焦距值以创建多个伪域并构建对抗性训练损失，以鼓励特征表示更加与域无关。我们的方法（即 DG-BEV）没有花哨的东西，成功地减轻了看不见的目标域上的性能下降，而不会损害源域的准确性。对各种公共数据集（包括 Waymo、nuScenes 和 Lyft）的广泛实验证明了我们方法的泛化性和有效性。据我们所知，这是第一个探索 MV3D-Det 领域泛化方法的系统研究。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在解决多视角鸟瞰图3D目标检测（MV3D-Det）中的领域泛化问题，以应对输入图像领域与训练数据不一致导致的性能下降。
AI.motivation: 现有基于相机的3D目标检测算法在输入图像领域与训练数据不一致时可能出现显著性能下降，因此需要探索领域泛化方法以提升模型在未知目标领域的适应能力。
AI.method: 基于协变量偏移假设，通过解耦深度估计与相机内参（焦距），采用尺度不变深度预测和动态透视增强（利用单应性增加外参多样性），并修改焦距生成伪领域，结合对抗训练损失优化领域无关特征表示。
AI.result: 在Waymo、nuScenes和Lyft等公开数据集上的实验表明，所提方法DG-BEV在未见目标领域上有效缓解性能下降，同时保持源领域的检测精度。
AI.conclusion: 本文首次系统性研究了MV3D-Det的领域泛化方法，为跨领域3D目标检测提供了有效解决方案。

authors_text: Lintao Wang,Kun Hu,Lei Bai,Yu Ding,Wanli Ouyang,Zhiyong Wang
title: Multi-Scale Control Signal-Aware Transformer for Motion Synthesis without Phase
title_zh: 用于无相位运动合成的多尺度控制信号感知变压器
abstract: Synthesizing controllable motion for a character using deep learning has been a promising approach due to its potential to learn a compact model without laborious feature engineering. To produce dynamic motion from weak control signals such as desired paths, existing methods often require auxiliary information such as phases for alleviating motion ambiguity, which limits their generalisation capability. As past poses often contain useful auxiliary hints, in this paper, we propose a task-agnostic deep learning method, namely Multi-scale Control Signal-aware Transformer (MCS-T), with an attention based encoder-decoder architecture to discover the auxiliary information implicitly for synthesizing controllable motion without explicitly requiring auxiliary information such as phase. Specifically, an encoder is devised to adaptively formulate the motion patterns of a character's past poses with multi-scale skeletons, and a decoder driven by control signals to further synthesize and predict the character's state by paying context-specialised attention to the encoded past motion patterns. As a result, it helps alleviate the issues of low responsiveness and slow transition which often happen in conventional methods not using auxiliary information. Both qualitative and quantitative experimental results on an existing biped locomotion dataset, which involves diverse types of motion transitions, demonstrate the effectiveness of our method. In particular, MCS-T is able to successfully generate motions comparable to those generated by the methods using auxiliary information.
summary: Synthesizing controllable motion for a character using deep learning has been a promising approach due to its potential to learn a compact model without laborious feature engineering. To produce dynamic motion from weak control signals such as desired paths, existing methods often require auxiliary information such as phases for alleviating motion ambiguity, which limits their generalisation capability. As past poses often contain useful auxiliary hints, in this paper, we propose a task-agnostic deep learning method, namely Multi-scale Control Signal-aware Transformer (MCS-T), with an attention based encoder-decoder architecture to discover the auxiliary information implicitly for synthesizing controllable motion without explicitly requiring auxiliary information such as phase. Specifically, an encoder is devised to adaptively formulate the motion patterns of a character's past poses with multi-scale skeletons, and a decoder driven by control signals to further synthesize and predict the character's state by paying context-specialised attention to the encoded past motion patterns. As a result, it helps alleviate the issues of low responsiveness and slow transition which often happen in conventional methods not using auxiliary information. Both qualitative and quantitative experimental results on an existing biped locomotion dataset, which involves diverse types of motion transitions, demonstrate the effectiveness of our method. In particular, MCS-T is able to successfully generate motions comparable to those generated by the methods using auxiliary information.
abstract_zh: 使用深度学习合成角色的可控运动一直是一种很有前途的方法，因为它有潜力学习紧凑的模型，而无需费力的特征工程。为了从弱控制信号（例如期望路径）产生动态运动，现有方法通常需要辅助信息（例如相位）来减轻运动模糊性，这限制了它们的泛化能力。由于过去的姿势通常包含有用的辅助提示，因此在本文中，我们提出了一种与任务无关的深度学习方法，即多尺度控制信号感知变换器（MCS-T），具有基于注意力的编码器-解码器架构，可以隐式地发现用于合成可控运动的辅助信息，而无需显式地需要相位等辅助信息。具体来说，编码器被设计为利用多尺度骨架自适应地制定角色过去姿势的运动模式，而解码器由控制信号驱动，通过对编码的过去运动模式进行上下文专门关注来进一步合成和预测角色的状态。因此，它有助于缓解不使用辅助信息的传统方法中经常发生的响应速度慢和转换缓慢的问题。现有双足运动数据集（涉及不同类型的运动转换）的定性和定量实验结果都证明了我们方法的有效性。特别是，MCS-T 能够成功生成与使用辅助信息的方法生成的运动相当的运动。

AI.motivation: 现有的方法在生成动态运动时通常需要辅助信息（如相位）以缓解运动模糊问题，这限制了其泛化能力。因此，本文旨在开发一种无需显式辅助信息的方法。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种名为多尺度控制信号感知Transformer（MCS-T）的任务无关深度学习方法，采用基于注意力机制的编码器-解码器架构，通过隐式发现辅助信息来合成可控运动。
AI.result: 在现有的双足行走数据集上进行了定性和定量实验，结果表明MCS-T能够生成与使用辅助信息的方法相当的运动。
AI.conclusion: MCS-T方法能够有效缓解传统方法中响应性低和过渡缓慢的问题，展示了无需辅助信息的可控运动合成的有效性。

search_text: Multi-Scale Control Signal-Aware Transformer for Motion Synthesis without Phase 用于无相位运动合成的多尺度控制信号感知变压器 Synthesizing controllable motion for a character using deep learning has been a promising approach due to its potential to learn a compact model without laborious feature engineering. To produce dynamic motion from weak control signals such as desired paths, existing methods often require auxiliary information such as phases for alleviating motion ambiguity, which limits their generalisation capability. As past poses often contain useful auxiliary hints, in this paper, we propose a task-agnostic deep learning method, namely Multi-scale Control Signal-aware Transformer (MCS-T), with an attention based encoder-decoder architecture to discover the auxiliary information implicitly for synthesizing controllable motion without explicitly requiring auxiliary information such as phase. Specifically, an encoder is devised to adaptively formulate the motion patterns of a character's past poses with multi-scale skeletons, and a decoder driven by control signals to further synthesize and predict the character's state by paying context-specialised attention to the encoded past motion patterns. As a result, it helps alleviate the issues of low responsiveness and slow transition which often happen in conventional methods not using auxiliary information. Both qualitative and quantitative experimental results on an existing biped locomotion dataset, which involves diverse types of motion transitions, demonstrate the effectiveness of our method. In particular, MCS-T is able to successfully generate motions comparable to those generated by the methods using auxiliary information. 使用深度学习合成角色的可控运动一直是一种很有前途的方法，因为它有潜力学习紧凑的模型，而无需费力的特征工程。为了从弱控制信号（例如期望路径）产生动态运动，现有方法通常需要辅助信息（例如相位）来减轻运动模糊性，这限制了它们的泛化能力。由于过去的姿势通常包含有用的辅助提示，因此在本文中，我们提出了一种与任务无关的深度学习方法，即多尺度控制信号感知变换器（MCS-T），具有基于注意力的编码器-解码器架构，可以隐式地发现用于合成可控运动的辅助信息，而无需显式地需要相位等辅助信息。具体来说，编码器被设计为利用多尺度骨架自适应地制定角色过去姿势的运动模式，而解码器由控制信号驱动，通过对编码的过去运动模式进行上下文专门关注来进一步合成和预测角色的状态。因此，它有助于缓解不使用辅助信息的传统方法中经常发生的响应速度慢和转换缓慢的问题。现有双足运动数据集（涉及不同类型的运动转换）的定性和定量实验结果都证明了我们方法的有效性。特别是，MCS-T 能够成功生成与使用辅助信息的方法生成的运动相当的运动。 本文提出了一种无需相位信息的多尺度控制信号感知Transformer模型，用于合成可控的运动。 Lintao Wang,Kun Hu,Lei Bai,Yu Ding,Wanli Ouyang,Zhiyong Wang cs.CV,cs.GR

authors_text: Sunil Gupta,Alistair Shilton,Arun Kumar A V,Shannon Ryan,Majid Abdolshah,Hung Le,Santu Rana,Julian Berk,Mahad Rashid,Svetha Venkatesh
title: BO-Muse: A human expert and AI teaming framework for accelerated experimental design
title_zh: BO-Muse：用于加速实验设计的人类专家和人工智能团队框架
abstract: In this paper we introduce BO-Muse, a new approach to human-AI teaming for the optimization of expensive black-box functions. Inspired by the intrinsic difficulty of extracting expert knowledge and distilling it back into AI models and by observations of human behavior in real-world experimental design, our algorithm lets the human expert take the lead in the experimental process. The human expert can use their domain expertise to its full potential, while the AI plays the role of a muse, injecting novelty and searching for areas of weakness to break the human out of over-exploitation induced by cognitive entrenchment. With mild assumptions, we show that our algorithm converges sub-linearly, at a rate faster than the AI or human alone. We validate our algorithm using synthetic data and with human experts performing real-world experiments.
summary: In this paper we introduce BO-Muse, a new approach to human-AI teaming for the optimization of expensive black-box functions. Inspired by the intrinsic difficulty of extracting expert knowledge and distilling it back into AI models and by observations of human behavior in real-world experimental design, our algorithm lets the human expert take the lead in the experimental process. The human expert can use their domain expertise to its full potential, while the AI plays the role of a muse, injecting novelty and searching for areas of weakness to break the human out of over-exploitation induced by cognitive entrenchment. With mild assumptions, we show that our algorithm converges sub-linearly, at a rate faster than the AI or human alone. We validate our algorithm using synthetic data and with human experts performing real-world experiments.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们介绍了 BO-Muse，这是一种用于优化昂贵的黑盒功能的人类人工智能协作的新方法。受到提取专家知识并将其提炼回人工智能模型的内在困难以及对现实世界实验设计中人类行为的观察的启发，我们的算法让人类专家在实验过程中发挥主导作用。人类专家可以充分发挥其领域专业知识的潜力，而人工智能则扮演缪斯的角色，注入新奇并寻找弱点，使人类摆脱因认知巩固而导致的过度剥削。通过温和的假设，我们表明我们的算法以亚线性方式收敛，收敛速度比人工智能或人类单独收敛的速度更快。我们使用合成数据并与人类专家进行真实世界的实验来验证我们的算法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出BO-Muse框架，通过人类专家与AI协作加速昂贵黑箱函数的优化。
AI.motivation: 提取专家知识并融入AI模型存在困难，且人类在实验设计中易受认知固着影响，需AI辅助突破局限。
AI.method: 人类主导实验过程，AI作为‘缪斯’注入新颖性，识别人类过度开发区域并引导探索。
AI.result: 算法在弱假设下收敛速度优于单独AI或人类，经合成数据与真实实验验证有效性。
AI.conclusion: BO-Muse框架通过人机协作优势，实现更高效的实验设计与优化。

authors_text: Jianqi Chen,Yilan Zhang,Zhengxia Zou,Keyan Chen,Zhenwei Shi
title: Dense Pixel-to-Pixel Harmonization via Continuous Image Representation
title_zh: 通过连续图像表示实现密集像素到像素的协调
comment: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology (TCSVT)
comments: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology (TCSVT)
abstract: High-resolution (HR) image harmonization is of great significance in real-world applications such as image synthesis and image editing. However, due to the high memory costs, existing dense pixel-to-pixel harmonization methods are mainly focusing on processing low-resolution (LR) images. Some recent works resort to combining with color-to-color transformations but are either limited to certain resolutions or heavily depend on hand-crafted image filters. In this work, we explore leveraging the implicit neural representation (INR) and propose a novel image Harmonization method based on Implicit neural Networks (HINet), which to the best of our knowledge, is the first dense pixel-to-pixel method applicable to HR images without any hand-crafted filter design. Inspired by the Retinex theory, we decouple the MLPs into two parts to respectively capture the content and environment of composite images. A Low-Resolution Image Prior (LRIP) network is designed to alleviate the Boundary Inconsistency problem, and we also propose new designs for the training and inference process. Extensive experiments have demonstrated the effectiveness of our method compared with state-of-the-art methods. Furthermore, some interesting and practical applications of the proposed method are explored. Our code is available at https://github.com/WindVChen/INR-Harmonization.
summary: High-resolution (HR) image harmonization is of great significance in real-world applications such as image synthesis and image editing. However, due to the high memory costs, existing dense pixel-to-pixel harmonization methods are mainly focusing on processing low-resolution (LR) images. Some recent works resort to combining with color-to-color transformations but are either limited to certain resolutions or heavily depend on hand-crafted image filters. In this work, we explore leveraging the implicit neural representation (INR) and propose a novel image Harmonization method based on Implicit neural Networks (HINet), which to the best of our knowledge, is the first dense pixel-to-pixel method applicable to HR images without any hand-crafted filter design. Inspired by the Retinex theory, we decouple the MLPs into two parts to respectively capture the content and environment of composite images. A Low-Resolution Image Prior (LRIP) network is designed to alleviate the Boundary Inconsistency problem, and we also propose new designs for the training and inference process. Extensive experiments have demonstrated the effectiveness of our method compared with state-of-the-art methods. Furthermore, some interesting and practical applications of the proposed method are explored. Our code is available at https://github.com/WindVChen/INR-Harmonization.
abstract_zh: 高分辨率（HR）图像协调在图像合成和图像编辑等实际应用中具有重要意义。然而，由于内存成本较高，现有的密集像素到像素协调方法主要集中于处理低分辨率（LR）图像。最近的一些作品诉诸于与颜色到颜色转换的结合，但要么仅限于某些分辨率，要么严重依赖于手工制作的图像过滤器。在这项工作中，我们探索利用隐式神经表示（INR）并提出一种基于隐式神经网络（HINet）的新颖图像协调方法，据我们所知，这是第一个适用于 HR 图像的密集像素到像素方法，无需任何手工设计的滤波器设计。受 Retinex 理论的启发，我们将 MLP 解耦为两部分，分别捕获合成图像的内容和环境。低分辨率图像先验（LRIP）网络旨在缓解边界不一致问题，我们还为训练和推理过程提出了新的设计。大量的实验证明了我们的方法与最先进的方法相比的有效性。此外，还探讨了所提出方法的一些有趣且实际的应用。我们的代码可从 https://github.com/WindVChen/INR-Harmonization 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于连续图像表示的高分辨率密集像素到像素图像协调方法，解决现有方法在高分辨率图像处理中的内存限制和边界不一致问题。
AI.motivation: 现有密集像素协调方法受限于高内存成本，主要处理低分辨率图像，或依赖手工设计的滤波器且分辨率受限，难以满足实际应用需求。
AI.method: 提出基于隐式神经表示（INR）的HINet方法，通过Retinex理论将MLP分解为内容与环境分支，并设计低分辨率图像先验网络（LRIP）缓解边界不一致问题，改进训练与推理流程。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在高分辨率图像协调任务中优于现有最先进方法，并探索了其在图像合成与编辑中的实际应用。
AI.conclusion: 本文首次实现无需手工滤波器的高分辨率密集像素协调，通过INR与LRIP网络设计有效解决了内存与边界问题，推动了图像协调技术的发展。

tldr: 本文提出一种基于连续图像表示的高分辨率密集像素到像素图像协调方法，解决现有方法在高分辨率图像处理中的内存限制和边界不一致问题。
motivation: 现有密集像素协调方法受限于高内存成本，主要处理低分辨率图像，或依赖手工设计的滤波器且分辨率受限，难以满足实际应用需求。
method: 提出基于隐式神经表示（INR）的HINet方法，通过Retinex理论将MLP分解为内容与环境分支，并设计低分辨率图像先验网络（LRIP）缓解边界不一致问题，改进训练与推理流程。
result: 实验表明该方法在高分辨率图像协调任务中优于现有最先进方法，并探索了其在图像合成与编辑中的实际应用。
conclusion: 本文首次实现无需手工滤波器的高分辨率密集像素协调，通过INR与LRIP网络设计有效解决了内存与边界问题，推动了图像协调技术的发展。
search_text: Dense Pixel-to-Pixel Harmonization via Continuous Image Representation 通过连续图像表示实现密集像素到像素的协调 High-resolution (HR) image harmonization is of great significance in real-world applications such as image synthesis and image editing. However, due to the high memory costs, existing dense pixel-to-pixel harmonization methods are mainly focusing on processing low-resolution (LR) images. Some recent works resort to combining with color-to-color transformations but are either limited to certain resolutions or heavily depend on hand-crafted image filters. In this work, we explore leveraging the implicit neural representation (INR) and propose a novel image Harmonization method based on Implicit neural Networks (HINet), which to the best of our knowledge, is the first dense pixel-to-pixel method applicable to HR images without any hand-crafted filter design. Inspired by the Retinex theory, we decouple the MLPs into two parts to respectively capture the content and environment of composite images. A Low-Resolution Image Prior (LRIP) network is designed to alleviate the Boundary Inconsistency problem, and we also propose new designs for the training and inference process. Extensive experiments have demonstrated the effectiveness of our method compared with state-of-the-art methods. Furthermore, some interesting and practical applications of the proposed method are explored. Our code is available at https://github.com/WindVChen/INR-Harmonization. 高分辨率（HR）图像协调在图像合成和图像编辑等实际应用中具有重要意义。然而，由于内存成本较高，现有的密集像素到像素协调方法主要集中于处理低分辨率（LR）图像。最近的一些作品诉诸于与颜色到颜色转换的结合，但要么仅限于某些分辨率，要么严重依赖于手工制作的图像过滤器。在这项工作中，我们探索利用隐式神经表示（INR）并提出一种基于隐式神经网络（HINet）的新颖图像协调方法，据我们所知，这是第一个适用于 HR 图像的密集像素到像素方法，无需任何手工设计的滤波器设计。受 Retinex 理论的启发，我们将 MLP 解耦为两部分，分别捕获合成图像的内容和环境。低分辨率图像先验（LRIP）网络旨在缓解边界不一致问题，我们还为训练和推理过程提出了新的设计。大量的实验证明了我们的方法与最先进的方法相比的有效性。此外，还探讨了所提出方法的一些有趣且实际的应用。我们的代码可从 https://github.com/WindVChen/INR-Harmonization 获取。 本文提出一种基于连续图像表示的高分辨率密集像素到像素图像协调方法，解决现有方法在高分辨率图像处理中的内存限制和边界不一致问题。 Jianqi Chen,Yilan Zhang,Zhengxia Zou,Keyan Chen,Zhenwei Shi cs.CV

authors_text: Hyoung Suk Park,Chang Min Hyun,Jin Keun Seo
title: Nonlinear ill-posed problem in low-dose dental cone-beam computed tomography
title_zh: 低剂量牙科锥形束计算机断层扫描中的非线性病态问题
abstract: This paper describes the mathematical structure of the ill-posed nonlinear inverse problem of low-dose dental cone-beam computed tomography (CBCT) and explains the advantages of a deep learning-based approach to the reconstruction of computed tomography images over conventional regularization methods. This paper explains the underlying reasons why dental CBCT is more ill-posed than standard computed tomography. Despite this severe ill-posedness, the demand for dental CBCT systems is rapidly growing because of their cost competitiveness and low radiation dose. We then describe the limitations of existing methods in the accurate restoration of the morphological structures of teeth using dental CBCT data severely damaged by metal implants. We further discuss the usefulness of panoramic images generated from CBCT data for accurate tooth segmentation. We also discuss the possibility of utilizing radiation-free intra-oral scan data as prior information in CBCT image reconstruction to compensate for the damage to data caused by metal implants.
summary: This paper describes the mathematical structure of the ill-posed nonlinear inverse problem of low-dose dental cone-beam computed tomography (CBCT) and explains the advantages of a deep learning-based approach to the reconstruction of computed tomography images over conventional regularization methods. This paper explains the underlying reasons why dental CBCT is more ill-posed than standard computed tomography. Despite this severe ill-posedness, the demand for dental CBCT systems is rapidly growing because of their cost competitiveness and low radiation dose. We then describe the limitations of existing methods in the accurate restoration of the morphological structures of teeth using dental CBCT data severely damaged by metal implants. We further discuss the usefulness of panoramic images generated from CBCT data for accurate tooth segmentation. We also discuss the possibility of utilizing radiation-free intra-oral scan data as prior information in CBCT image reconstruction to compensate for the damage to data caused by metal implants.
abstract_zh: 本文描述了低剂量牙科锥形束计算机断层扫描 (CBCT) 不适定非线性反问题的数学结构，并解释了基于深度学习的计算机断层扫描图像重建方法相对于传统正则化方法的优势。本文解释了牙科 CBCT 比标准计算机断层扫描更不适定的根本原因。尽管存在严重的不适，但由于其成本竞争力和低辐射剂量，对牙科 CBCT 系统的需求仍在快速增长。然后，我们描述了现有方法在使用金属种植体严重损坏的牙科 CBCT 数据精确恢复牙齿形态结构方面的局限性。我们进一步讨论了从 CBCT 数据生成的全景图像对于精确牙齿分割的有用性。我们还讨论了利用无辐射口腔内扫描数据作为 CBCT 图像重建中的先验信息以补偿金属植入物造成的数据损坏的可能性。

AI.tldr: 研究低剂量牙科锥束CT中的非线性病态问题，探讨深度学习方法在图像重建中的优势。
AI.motivation: 牙科CBCT因成本低和辐射剂量小需求增长，但金属植入物导致的数据损坏使传统方法效果有限，需新解决方案。
AI.method: 基于深度学习的图像重建方法，结合全景图像和无辐射口腔扫描数据作为先验信息补偿金属植入物引起的损伤。
AI.result: 深度学习方法在重建质量上优于传统正则化方法，结合先验信息能有效缓解金属植入物引起的图像损伤。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习方法有效应对牙科CBCT的病态问题，未来需进一步探索多模态数据融合以提高重建精度。

authors_text: Hsin Cheng,Zhiwu Zheng,Prakhar Kumar,Wali Afridi,Ben Kim,Sigurd Wagner,Naveen Verma,James C. Sturm,Minjie Chen
title: eViper: A Scalable Platform for Untethered Modular Soft Robots
title_zh: eViper：不受束缚的模块化软机器人的可扩展平台
abstract: Soft robots present unique capabilities, but have been limited by the lack of scalable technologies for construction and the complexity of algorithms for efficient control and motion, which depend on soft-body dynamics, high-dimensional actuation patterns, and external/on-board forces. This paper presents scalable methods and platforms to study the impact of weight distribution and actuation patterns on fully untethered modular soft robots. An extendable Vibrating Intelligent Piezo-Electric Robot (eViper), together with an open-source Simulation Framework for Electroactive Robotic Sheet (SFERS) implemented in PyBullet, was developed as a platform to study the sophisticated weight-locomotion interaction. By integrating the power electronics, sensors, actuators, and batteries on-board, the eViper platform enables rapid design iteration and evaluation of different weight distribution and control strategies for the actuator arrays, supporting both physics-based modeling and data-driven modeling via on-board automatic data-acquisition capabilities. We show that SFERS can provide useful guidelines for optimizing the weight distribution and actuation patterns of the eViper to achieve the maximum speed or minimum cost-of-transportation (COT).
summary: Soft robots present unique capabilities, but have been limited by the lack of scalable technologies for construction and the complexity of algorithms for efficient control and motion, which depend on soft-body dynamics, high-dimensional actuation patterns, and external/on-board forces. This paper presents scalable methods and platforms to study the impact of weight distribution and actuation patterns on fully untethered modular soft robots. An extendable Vibrating Intelligent Piezo-Electric Robot (eViper), together with an open-source Simulation Framework for Electroactive Robotic Sheet (SFERS) implemented in PyBullet, was developed as a platform to study the sophisticated weight-locomotion interaction. By integrating the power electronics, sensors, actuators, and batteries on-board, the eViper platform enables rapid design iteration and evaluation of different weight distribution and control strategies for the actuator arrays, supporting both physics-based modeling and data-driven modeling via on-board automatic data-acquisition capabilities. We show that SFERS can provide useful guidelines for optimizing the weight distribution and actuation patterns of the eViper to achieve the maximum speed or minimum cost-of-transportation (COT).
abstract_zh: 软机器人具有独特的功能，但由于缺乏可扩展的构造技术以及有效控制和运动算法的复杂性而受到限制，这些算法依赖于软体动力学、高维驱动模式和外部/机载力。本文提出了可扩展的方法和平台，用于研究重量分布和驱动模式对完全不受束缚的模块化软机器人的影响。可扩展的振动智能压电机器人 (eViper) 以及在 PyBullet 中实现的电活性机器人片开源仿真框架 (SFERS) 被开发为研究复杂的重量运动相互作用的平台。通过集成板载电力电子器件、传感器、执行器和电池，eViper 平台能够快速设计迭代和评估执行器阵列的不同重量分布和控制策略，通过板载自动数据采集功能支持基于物理的建模和数据驱动建模。我们表明，SFERS 可以为优化 eViper 的重量分布和驱动模式提供有用的指导，以实现最大速度或最小运输成本 (COT)。

AI.tldr: 本文提出eViper平台及SFERS仿真框架，用于研究无缆模块化软体机器人的重量分布与驱动模式优化。
AI.motivation: 软体机器人受限于构建技术的可扩展性及复杂控制算法，缺乏高效运动与控制的解决方案。
AI.method: 开发集成电源、传感器、执行器和电池的eViper平台，并构建PyBullet实现的SFERS仿真框架，支持物理建模与数据驱动分析。
AI.result: SFERS可为eViper的重量分布和驱动模式优化提供指导，以实现最大速度或最低运输能耗。
AI.conclusion: eViper平台有效支持软体机器人的快速设计迭代与多策略评估，推动其性能优化。

authors_text: Binghui Peng,Aviad Rubinstein
title: Near Optimal Memory-Regret Tradeoff for Online Learning
title_zh: 在线学习的近乎最佳记忆与后悔权衡
abstract: In the experts problem, on each of $T$ days, an agent needs to follow the advice of one of $n$ ``experts''. After each day, the loss associated with each expert's advice is revealed. A fundamental result in learning theory says that the agent can achieve vanishing regret, i.e. their cumulative loss is within $o(T)$ of the cumulative loss of the best-in-hindsight expert. Can the agent perform well without sufficient space to remember all the experts? We extend a nascent line of research on this question in two directions: $\bullet$ We give a new algorithm against the oblivious adversary, improving over the memory-regret tradeoff obtained by [PZ23], and nearly matching the lower bound of [SWXZ22]. $\bullet$ We also consider an adaptive adversary who can observe past experts chosen by the agent. In this setting we give both a new algorithm and a novel lower bound, proving that roughly $\sqrt{n}$ memory is both necessary and sufficient for obtaining $o(T)$ regret.
summary: In the experts problem, on each of $T$ days, an agent needs to follow the advice of one of $n$ ``experts''. After each day, the loss associated with each expert's advice is revealed. A fundamental result in learning theory says that the agent can achieve vanishing regret, i.e. their cumulative loss is within $o(T)$ of the cumulative loss of the best-in-hindsight expert. Can the agent perform well without sufficient space to remember all the experts? We extend a nascent line of research on this question in two directions: $\bullet$ We give a new algorithm against the oblivious adversary, improving over the memory-regret tradeoff obtained by [PZ23], and nearly matching the lower bound of [SWXZ22]. $\bullet$ We also consider an adaptive adversary who can observe past experts chosen by the agent. In this setting we give both a new algorithm and a novel lower bound, proving that roughly $\sqrt{n}$ memory is both necessary and sufficient for obtaining $o(T)$ regret.
abstract_zh: 在专家问题中，在每个 $T$ 天，代理需要遵循 $n$ 个“专家”之一的建议。每天之后，与每位专家的建议相关的损失都会被揭示。学习理论的一个基本结果表明，智能体可以实现后悔消失，即他们的累积损失在事后最佳专家的累积损失的 $o(T)$ 范围内。如果没有足够的空间来记住所有专家，代理能否表现良好？我们在两个方向上扩展了关于这个问题的新研究路线： $\bullet$ 我们给出了一种针对不经意的对手的新算法，改进了 [PZ23] 获得的记忆遗憾权衡，并且几乎匹配 [SWXZ22] 的下限。 $\bullet$ 我们还考虑一个自适应对手，他可以观察代理选择的过去的专家。在这种情况下，我们给出了一个新的算法和一个新颖的下界，证明大约 $\sqrt{n}$ 内存对于获得 $o(T)$ 后悔是必要且充分的。

AI.tldr: 研究在线学习中有限内存下的记忆与遗憾的权衡，提出新算法和下界。
AI.motivation: 传统在线学习方法需存储所有专家信息，但实际场景中内存可能受限，需探索有限内存下的最优表现。
AI.method: 设计针对非适应性对手的改进算法，及针对适应性对手的新算法与下界分析。
AI.result: 改进记忆-遗憾权衡至接近理论下界，并证明√n内存是获得o(T)遗憾的必要且充分条件。
AI.conclusion: 论文在两种对手模型下均提供了近似最优的内存-遗憾权衡，填补了该领域的研究空白。

After each day, the loss associated with each expert's advice is revealed. authors_text: Shuai Xiao,Zaifan Jiang,Shuang Yang
title: Tile Networks: Learning Optimal Geometric Layout for Whole-page Recommendation
title_zh: Tile Networks：学习整页推荐的最佳几何布局
comment: Published at Proceedings of the 25th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics (AISTATS) 2022
comments: Published at Proceedings of the 25th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics (AISTATS) 2022
abstract: Finding optimal configurations in a geometric space is a key challenge in many technological disciplines. Current approaches either rely heavily on human domain expertise and are difficult to scale. In this paper we show it is possible to solve configuration optimization problems for whole-page recommendation using reinforcement learning. The proposed \textit{Tile Networks} is a neural architecture that optimizes 2D geometric configurations by arranging items on proper positions. Empirical results on real dataset demonstrate its superior performance compared to traditional learning to rank approaches and recent deep models.
summary: Finding optimal configurations in a geometric space is a key challenge in many technological disciplines. Current approaches either rely heavily on human domain expertise and are difficult to scale. In this paper we show it is possible to solve configuration optimization problems for whole-page recommendation using reinforcement learning. The proposed \textit{Tile Networks} is a neural architecture that optimizes 2D geometric configurations by arranging items on proper positions. Empirical results on real dataset demonstrate its superior performance compared to traditional learning to rank approaches and recent deep models.
abstract_zh: 在几何空间中寻找最佳配置是许多技术学科的关键挑战。当前的方法要么严重依赖人类领域的专业知识，要么难以扩展。在本文中，我们展示了使用强化学习解决整页推荐的配置优化问题是可能的。所提出的 \textit{Tile Networks} 是一种神经架构，它通过将项目排列在适当的位置来优化 2D 几何配置。真实数据集的实证结果表明，与传统的学习排序方法和最新的深度模型相比，其具有优越的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于强化学习的神经网络架构Tile Networks，用于优化整个页面推荐的二维几何布局。
AI.motivation: 现有推荐系统布局优化方法依赖人工设计且难以扩展，缺乏自动化的几何配置优化方案。
AI.method: 设计Tile Networks神经网络架构，通过强化学习在二维空间中自动排列推荐物品位置以优化整体布局。
AI.result: 在真实数据集上的实验表明，该方法性能优于传统排序学习方法和最新深度模型。
AI.conclusion: Tile Networks有效解决了推荐系统中几何布局优化问题，为自动化页面布局设计提供了新思路。

authors_text: Yangyang Shu,Anton van den Hengel,Lingqiao Liu
title: Learning Common Rationale to Improve Self-Supervised Representation for Fine-Grained Visual Recognition Problems
title_zh: 学习改进细粒度视觉识别问题的自我监督表示的共同原理
abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) strategies have demonstrated remarkable performance in various recognition tasks. However, both our preliminary investigation and recent studies suggest that they may be less effective in learning representations for fine-grained visual recognition (FGVR) since many features helpful for optimizing SSL objectives are not suitable for characterizing the subtle differences in FGVR. To overcome this issue, we propose learning an additional screening mechanism to identify discriminative clues commonly seen across instances and classes, dubbed as common rationales in this paper. Intuitively, common rationales tend to correspond to the discriminative patterns from the key parts of foreground objects. We show that a common rationale detector can be learned by simply exploiting the GradCAM induced from the SSL objective without using any pre-trained object parts or saliency detectors, making it seamlessly to be integrated with the existing SSL process. Specifically, we fit the GradCAM with a branch with limited fitting capacity, which allows the branch to capture the common rationales and discard the less common discriminative patterns. At the test stage, the branch generates a set of spatial weights to selectively aggregate features representing an instance. Extensive experimental results on four visual tasks demonstrate that the proposed method can lead to a significant improvement in different evaluation settings.
summary: Self-supervised learning (SSL) strategies have demonstrated remarkable performance in various recognition tasks. However, both our preliminary investigation and recent studies suggest that they may be less effective in learning representations for fine-grained visual recognition (FGVR) since many features helpful for optimizing SSL objectives are not suitable for characterizing the subtle differences in FGVR. To overcome this issue, we propose learning an additional screening mechanism to identify discriminative clues commonly seen across instances and classes, dubbed as common rationales in this paper. Intuitively, common rationales tend to correspond to the discriminative patterns from the key parts of foreground objects. We show that a common rationale detector can be learned by simply exploiting the GradCAM induced from the SSL objective without using any pre-trained object parts or saliency detectors, making it seamlessly to be integrated with the existing SSL process. Specifically, we fit the GradCAM with a branch with limited fitting capacity, which allows the branch to capture the common rationales and discard the less common discriminative patterns. At the test stage, the branch generates a set of spatial weights to selectively aggregate features representing an instance. Extensive experimental results on four visual tasks demonstrate that the proposed method can lead to a significant improvement in different evaluation settings.
abstract_zh: 自监督学习（SSL）策略在各种识别任务中表现出了卓越的性能。然而，我们的初步调查和最近的研究都表明，它们在学习细粒度视觉识别 (FGVR) 表示方面可能效果较差，因为许多有助于优化 SSL 目标的特征并不适合表征 FGVR 中的细微差异。为了克服这个问题，我们建议学习一种额外的筛选机制来识别实例和类中常​​见的歧视性线索，在本文中被称为共同原理。直观上，常见的原理往往与前景物体关键部分的判别模式相对应。我们证明，只需利用从 SSL 目标导出的 GradCAM 即可学习通用的基本原理检测器，而无需使用任何预先训练的对象部分或显着性检测器，从而使其与现有的 SSL 过程无缝集成。具体来说，我们将 GradCAM 与拟合能力有限的分支进行拟合，这使得该分支能够捕获常见的基本原理并丢弃不太常见的判别模式。在测试阶段，分支生成一组空间权重来选择性地聚合代表实例的特征。对四个视觉任务的广泛实验结果表明，所提出的方法可以在不同的评估设置中带来显着的改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过学习通用的判据（common rationales）来提升自监督学习在细粒度视觉识别任务中的表示能力。
AI.motivation: 现有自监督学习方法在细粒度视觉识别（FGVR）任务中效果有限，因其关注的特征与FGVR所需的细微差异特征不匹配。
AI.method: 通过GradCAM从自监督目标中提取通用判据，利用一个容量受限的分支筛选关键前景区域特征，生成空间权重以聚合实例表征。
AI.result: 在四个视觉任务上的实验表明，该方法在不同评估设置下均取得显著性能提升。
AI.conclusion: 学习通用判据能有效增强自监督表征，使其更适应细粒度视觉识别任务中对细微差异的捕捉需求。

Specifically, we fit the GradCAM with a branch with limited fitting capacity, which allows the branch to capture the common rationales and discard the less common discriminative patterns. At the test stage, the branch generates a set of spatial weights to selectively aggregate features representing an instance. Extensive experimental results on four visual tasks demonstrate that the proposed method can lead to a significant improvement in different evaluation settings. 自监督学习（SSL）策略在各种识别任务中表现出了卓越的性能。然而，我们的初步调查和最近的研究都表明，它们在学习细粒度视觉识别 (FGVR) 表示方面可能效果较差，因为许多有助于优化 SSL 目标的特征并不适合表征 FGVR 中的细微差异。为了克服这个问题，我们建议学习一种额外的筛选机制来识别实例和类中常​​见的歧视性线索，在本文中被称为共同原理。直观上，常见的原理往往与前景物体关键部分的判别模式相对应。我们证明，只需利用从 SSL 目标导出的 GradCAM 即可学习通用的基本原理检测器，而无需使用任何预先训练的对象部分或显着性检测器，从而使其与现有的 SSL 过程无缝集成。具体来说，我们将 GradCAM 与拟合能力有限的分支进行拟合，这使得该分支能够捕获常见的基本原理并丢弃不太常见的判别模式。在测试阶段，分支生成一组空间权重来选择性地聚合代表实例的特征。对四个视觉任务的广泛实验结果表明，所提出的方法可以在不同的评估设置中带来显着的改进。 本文提出通过学习通用的判据（common rationales）来提升自监督学习在细粒度视觉识别任务中的表示能力。 Yangyang Shu,Anton van den Hengel,Lingqiao Liu cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Yuanying Cai,Chuheng Zhang,Wei Shen,Xuyun Zhang,Wenjie Ruan,Longbo Huang
title: RePreM: Representation Pre-training with Masked Model for Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: RePreM：使用掩模模型进行强化学习的表示预训练
abstract: Inspired by the recent success of sequence modeling in RL and the use of masked language model for pre-training, we propose a masked model for pre-training in RL, RePreM (Representation Pre-training with Masked Model), which trains the encoder combined with transformer blocks to predict the masked states or actions in a trajectory. RePreM is simple but effective compared to existing representation pre-training methods in RL. It avoids algorithmic sophistication (such as data augmentation or estimating multiple models) with sequence modeling and generates a representation that captures long-term dynamics well. Empirically, we demonstrate the effectiveness of RePreM in various tasks, including dynamic prediction, transfer learning, and sample-efficient RL with both value-based and actor-critic methods. Moreover, we show that RePreM scales well with dataset size, dataset quality, and the scale of the encoder, which indicates its potential towards big RL models.
summary: Inspired by the recent success of sequence modeling in RL and the use of masked language model for pre-training, we propose a masked model for pre-training in RL, RePreM (Representation Pre-training with Masked Model), which trains the encoder combined with transformer blocks to predict the masked states or actions in a trajectory. RePreM is simple but effective compared to existing representation pre-training methods in RL. It avoids algorithmic sophistication (such as data augmentation or estimating multiple models) with sequence modeling and generates a representation that captures long-term dynamics well. Empirically, we demonstrate the effectiveness of RePreM in various tasks, including dynamic prediction, transfer learning, and sample-efficient RL with both value-based and actor-critic methods. Moreover, we show that RePreM scales well with dataset size, dataset quality, and the scale of the encoder, which indicates its potential towards big RL models.
abstract_zh: 受最近 RL 中序列建模的成功以及使用掩码语言模型进行预训练的启发，我们提出了一种用于 RL 中预训练的掩码模型，RePreM（带掩码模型的表示预训练），它结合 Transformer 块训练编码器以预测轨迹中的掩码状态或动作。与 RL 中现有的表示预训练方法相比，RePreM 简单但有效。它通过序列建模避免算法复杂性（例如数据增强或估计多个模型），并生成能够很好地捕获长期动态的表示。根据经验，我们证明了 RePreM 在各种任务中的有效性，包括动态预测、迁移学习和使用基于价值和行动者批评方法的样本高效强化学习。此外，我们表明 RePreM 可以很好地适应数据集大小、数据集质量和编码器规模，这表明了它在大型 RL 模型方面的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出RePreM方法，通过掩码模型预训练强化学习中的表示，利用Transformer编码器预测轨迹中的掩码状态或动作。
AI.motivation: 现有RL表示预训练方法依赖复杂算法（如数据增强或多模型估计），而RePreM旨在通过序列建模简化流程并捕捉长期动态。
AI.method: 结合Transformer块的编码器，对轨迹中的状态或动作进行掩码预测，无需复杂算法即可生成有效表示。
AI.result: RePreM在动态预测、迁移学习及样本高效RL任务中表现优异，并随数据集规模、质量及编码器规模扩展良好。
AI.conclusion: RePreM为RL提供了简单有效的预训练方案，具有扩展至大规模RL模型的潜力。
