ai_content_json.result: 在16个分类和10个检测数据集上，PPL方法分别提升性能估计37%和33%，并减少数据需求高估76%（分类）和91%（检测）。
ai_content_json.conclusion: PPL方法通过分段建模和元学习显著提升性能预测准确性，结合置信区间有效降低数据需求的高估风险。

tldr: 本文提出一种基于元学习的分段幂定律（PPL）方法，用于更准确地预测模型性能和数据需求，改进传统幂定律在小数据集上的误差问题。
motivation: 传统幂定律在小样本场景下预测模型性能时存在较大误差，因数据量与性能的对数关系在低样本量时呈非线性，高样本量时呈线性，需分段处理。
method: 引入分段幂定律（PPL）区分低样本量和高样本量两种数据场景，并通过元学习训练的随机森林回归器估计参数，进一步扩展置信区间以限制预测范围。
result: 在16个分类和10个检测数据集上，PPL方法分别提升性能估计37%和33%，并减少数据需求高估76%（分类）和91%（检测）。
conclusion: PPL方法通过分段建模和元学习显著提升性能预测准确性，结合置信区间有效降低数据需求的高估风险。
search_text: A Meta-Learning Approach to Predicting Performance and Data Requirements 预测性能和数据需求的元学习方法 We propose an approach to estimate the number of samples required for a model to reach a target performance. We find that the power law, the de facto principle to estimate model performance, leads to large error when using a small dataset (e.g., 5 samples per class) for extrapolation. This is because the log-performance error against the log-dataset size follows a nonlinear progression in the few-shot regime followed by a linear progression in the high-shot regime. We introduce a novel piecewise power law (PPL) that handles the two data regimes differently. To estimate the parameters of the PPL, we introduce a random forest regressor trained via meta learning that generalizes across classification/detection tasks, ResNet/ViT based architectures, and random/pre-trained initializations. The PPL improves the performance estimation on average by 37% across 16 classification and 33% across 10 detection datasets, compared to the power law. We further extend the PPL to provide a confidence bound and use it to limit the prediction horizon that reduces over-estimation of data by 76% on classification and 91% on detection datasets. 我们提出了一种方法来估计模型达到目标性能所需的样本数量。我们发现，幂律（估计模型性能的事实上的原理）在使用小数据集（例如，每类 5 个样本）进行外推时会导致较大误差。这是因为对数数据集大小的对数性能误差在少样本情况下遵循非线性进展，然后在高样本情况下遵循线性进展。我们引入了一种新颖的分段幂律（PPL），它以不同的方式处理两种数据机制。为了估计 PPL 的参数，我们引入了通过元学习训练的随机森林回归器，该回归器可以泛化分类/检测任务、基于 ResNet/ViT 的架构以及随机/预训练的初始化。与幂律相比，PPL 将 16 个分类的性能估计平均提高了 37%，将 10 个检测数据集的性能估计平均提高了 33%。我们进一步扩展 PPL 以提供置信区间，并使用它来限制预测范围，从而将分类数据的高估减少 76%，在检测数据集上减少 91%。 本文提出一种基于元学习的分段幂定律（PPL）方法，用于更准确地预测模型性能和数据需求，改进传统幂定律在小数据集上的误差问题。 Achin Jain,Gurumurthy Swaminathan,Paolo Favaro,Hao Yang,Avinash Ravichandran,Hrayr Harutyunyan,Alessandro Achille,Onkar Dabeer,Bernt Schiele,Ashwin Swaminathan,Stefano Soatto cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Asaf Yehudai,Matan Vetzler,Yosi Mass,Koren Lazar,Doron Cohen,Boaz Carmeli
title: QAID: Question Answering Inspired Few-shot Intent Detection
title_zh: QAID：问答启发的小样本意图检测
abstract: Intent detection with semantically similar fine-grained intents is a challenging task. To address it, we reformulate intent detection as a question-answering retrieval task by treating utterances and intent names as questions and answers. To that end, we utilize a question-answering retrieval architecture and adopt a two stages training schema with batch contrastive loss. In the pre-training stage, we improve query representations through self-supervised training. Then, in the fine-tuning stage, we increase contextualized token-level similarity scores between queries and answers from the same intent. Our results on three few-shot intent detection benchmarks achieve state-of-the-art performance.
summary: Intent detection with semantically similar fine-grained intents is a challenging task. To address it, we reformulate intent detection as a question-answering retrieval task by treating utterances and intent names as questions and answers. To that end, we utilize a question-answering retrieval architecture and adopt a two stages training schema with batch contrastive loss. In the pre-training stage, we improve query representations through self-supervised training. Then, in the fine-tuning stage, we increase contextualized token-level similarity scores between queries and answers from the same intent. Our results on three few-shot intent detection benchmarks achieve state-of-the-art performance.
abstract_zh: 具有语义相似的细粒度意图的意图检测是一项具有挑战性的任务。为了解决这个问题，我们通过将话语和意图名称视为问题和答案，将意图检测重新定义为问答检索任务。为此，我们利用问答检索架构，并采用具有批量对比损失的两阶段训练模式。在预训练阶段，我们通过自监督训练改进查询表示。然后，在微调阶段，我们增加来自同一意图的查询和答案之间的上下文标记级相似度得分。我们在三个少量意图检测基准上的结果实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 将意图检测任务转化为问答检索任务，通过自监督预训练和对比学习提升少样本场景下的意图识别性能。
AI.motivation: 传统意图检测方法在处理语义相似的细粒度意图时面临挑战，尤其在数据稀缺的少样本场景下难以有效区分相似意图。
AI.method: 构建问答检索框架，采用两阶段训练方案：预训练阶段通过自监督学习优化查询表示，微调阶段利用批次对比损失增强同类意图的语义相似性。
AI.result: 在三个少样本意图检测基准数据集上取得最先进性能，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 基于问答启发的检索框架结合两阶段训练策略，能有效提升少样本意图检测的准确率，为细粒度意图识别提供新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 将意图检测任务转化为问答检索任务，通过自监督预训练和对比学习提升少样本场景下的意图识别性能。

authors_text: Jian Li,Greta Tuckute,Evelina Fedorenko,Brian L. Edlow,Bruce Fischl,Adrian V. Dalca
title: Joint cortical registration of geometry and function using semi-supervised learning
title_zh: 使用半监督学习进行几何和功能的联合皮层配准
comment: B. Fischl and A. V. Dalca are co-senior authors with equal contribution. This work has been published in MIDL 2023 (https://openreview.net/forum?id=n9v_BuIcY7G) Medical Imaging with Deep Learning, Nashville, TN, Jul. 2023
comments: B. Fischl and A. V. Dalca are co-senior authors with equal contribution. This work has been published in MIDL 2023 (https://openreview.net/forum?id=n9v_BuIcY7G) Medical Imaging with Deep Learning, Nashville, TN, Jul. 2023
abstract: Brain surface-based image registration, an important component of brain image analysis, establishes spatial correspondence between cortical surfaces. Existing iterative and learning-based approaches focus on accurate registration of folding patterns of the cerebral cortex, and assume that geometry predicts function and thus functional areas will also be well aligned. However, structure/functional variability of anatomically corresponding areas across subjects has been widely reported. In this work, we introduce a learning-based cortical registration framework, JOSA, which jointly aligns folding patterns and functional maps while simultaneously learning an optimal atlas. We demonstrate that JOSA can substantially improve registration performance in both anatomical and functional domains over existing methods. By employing a semi-supervised training strategy, the proposed framework obviates the need for functional data during inference, enabling its use in broad neuroscientific domains where functional data may not be observed. The source code of JOSA will be released to the public at https://voxelmorph.net.
summary: Brain surface-based image registration, an important component of brain image analysis, establishes spatial correspondence between cortical surfaces. Existing iterative and learning-based approaches focus on accurate registration of folding patterns of the cerebral cortex, and assume that geometry predicts function and thus functional areas will also be well aligned. However, structure/functional variability of anatomically corresponding areas across subjects has been widely reported. In this work, we introduce a learning-based cortical registration framework, JOSA, which jointly aligns folding patterns and functional maps while simultaneously learning an optimal atlas. We demonstrate that JOSA can substantially improve registration performance in both anatomical and functional domains over existing methods. By employing a semi-supervised training strategy, the proposed framework obviates the need for functional data during inference, enabling its use in broad neuroscientific domains where functional data may not be observed. The source code of JOSA will be released to the public at https://voxelmorph.net.
abstract_zh: 基于大脑表面的图像配准是大脑图像分析的重要组成部分，它建立了皮质表面之间的空间对应关系。现有的迭代和基于学习的方法侧重于大脑皮层折叠模式的准确注册，并假设几何形状预测功能，因此功能区域也将很好地对齐。然而，不同受试者解剖学上相应区域的结构/功能变异性已被广泛报道。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种基于学习的皮质配准框架 JOSA，它联合对齐折叠模式和功能图，同时学习最佳图谱。我们证明，与现有方法相比，JOSA 可以显着提高解剖和功能领域的配准性能。通过采用半监督训练策略，所提出的框架消除了推理过程中对功能数据的需求，使其能够在可能无法观察到功能数据的广泛神经科学领域中使用。 JOSA的源代码将在https://voxelmorph.net上向公众发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于半监督学习的皮层配准框架JOSA，可同时对齐脑皮层的几何结构和功能图谱。
AI.motivation: 现有方法假设几何结构可预测功能区域，但跨被试的解剖对应区域存在结构/功能变异性，需联合优化几何与功能配准。
AI.method: 开发了JOSA框架，通过半监督学习联合对齐皮层褶皱模式和功能图谱，并学习最优图谱模板，推理时无需功能数据。
AI.result: 在解剖和功能领域均显著优于现有方法，且无需功能数据即可进行推理，适用于缺乏功能数据的神经科学领域。
AI.conclusion: JOSA通过联合优化几何与功能配准，解决了结构功能变异性问题，半监督策略提升了方法的通用性和实用性。

authors_text: Xijun Wang,Ruiqi Xian,Tianrui Guan,Celso M. de Melo,Stephen M. Nogar,Aniket Bera,Dinesh Manocha
title: AZTR: Aerial Video Action Recognition with Auto Zoom and Temporal Reasoning
title_zh: AZTR：具有自动缩放和时间推理的航空视频动作识别
abstract: We propose a novel approach for aerial video action recognition. Our method is designed for videos captured using UAVs and can run on edge or mobile devices. We present a learning-based approach that uses customized auto zoom to automatically identify the human target and scale it appropriately. This makes it easier to extract the key features and reduces the computational overhead. We also present an efficient temporal reasoning algorithm to capture the action information along the spatial and temporal domains within a controllable computational cost. Our approach has been implemented and evaluated both on the desktop with high-end GPUs and on the low power Robotics RB5 Platform for robots and drones. In practice, we achieve 6.1-7.4% improvement over SOTA in Top-1 accuracy on the RoCoG-v2 dataset, 8.3-10.4% improvement on the UAV-Human dataset and 3.2% improvement on the Drone Action dataset.
summary: We propose a novel approach for aerial video action recognition. Our method is designed for videos captured using UAVs and can run on edge or mobile devices. We present a learning-based approach that uses customized auto zoom to automatically identify the human target and scale it appropriately. This makes it easier to extract the key features and reduces the computational overhead. We also present an efficient temporal reasoning algorithm to capture the action information along the spatial and temporal domains within a controllable computational cost. Our approach has been implemented and evaluated both on the desktop with high-end GPUs and on the low power Robotics RB5 Platform for robots and drones. In practice, we achieve 6.1-7.4% improvement over SOTA in Top-1 accuracy on the RoCoG-v2 dataset, 8.3-10.4% improvement on the UAV-Human dataset and 3.2% improvement on the Drone Action dataset.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种用于航空视频动作识别的新颖方法。我们的方法专为使用无人机捕获的视频而设计，可以在边缘或移动设备上运行。我们提出了一种基于学习的方法，该方法使用定制的自动缩放来自动识别人类目标并适当地缩放它。这使得提取关键特征变得更容易并减少计算开销。我们还提出了一种有效的时间推理算法，可以在可控的计算成本内捕获沿空间和时间域的动作信息。我们的方法已在具有高端 GPU 的桌面以及用于机器人和无人机的低功耗机器人 RB5 平台上实施和评估。在实践中，我们在 RoCoG-v2 数据集上的 Top-1 准确度上比 SOTA 提高了 6.1-7.4%，在 UAV-Human 数据集上提高了 8.3-10.4%，在 Drone Action 数据集上提高了 3.2%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于无人机拍摄视频的人类动作识别方法AZTR，结合自动变焦和时序推理技术。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在处理无人机拍摄的空中视频时存在计算开销大、目标定位困难等问题，亟需轻量化且高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 采用基于学习的自动变焦技术定位人体目标并进行尺度适配，配合可控计算成本的时序推理算法，实现时空域动作信息提取。
AI.result: 在RoCoG-v2数据集上Top-1准确率提升6.1-7.4%，UAV-Human数据集提升8.3-10.4%，Drone Action数据集提升3.2%。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在保证精度的同时显著降低计算成本，验证了其在高端GPU和低功耗机器人平台上的有效性与实用性。

search_text: AZTR: Aerial Video Action Recognition with Auto Zoom and Temporal Reasoning AZTR：具有自动缩放和时间推理的航空视频动作识别 We propose a novel approach for aerial video action recognition. Our method is designed for videos captured using UAVs and can run on edge or mobile devices. We present a learning-based approach that uses customized auto zoom to automatically identify the human target and scale it appropriately. This makes it easier to extract the key features and reduces the computational overhead. We also present an efficient temporal reasoning algorithm to capture the action information along the spatial and temporal domains within a controllable computational cost. Our approach has been implemented and evaluated both on the desktop with high-end GPUs and on the low power Robotics RB5 Platform for robots and drones. In practice, we achieve 6.1-7.4% improvement over SOTA in Top-1 accuracy on the RoCoG-v2 dataset, 8.3-10.4% improvement on the UAV-Human dataset and 3.2% improvement on the Drone Action dataset. 我们提出了一种用于航空视频动作识别的新颖方法。我们的方法专为使用无人机捕获的视频而设计，可以在边缘或移动设备上运行。我们提出了一种基于学习的方法，该方法使用定制的自动缩放来自动识别人类目标并适当地缩放它。这使得提取关键特征变得更容易并减少计算开销。我们还提出了一种有效的时间推理算法，可以在可控的计算成本内捕获沿空间和时间域的动作信息。我们的方法已在具有高端 GPU 的桌面以及用于机器人和无人机的低功耗机器人 RB5 平台上实施和评估。在实践中，我们在 RoCoG-v2 数据集上的 Top-1 准确度上比 SOTA 提高了 6.1-7.4%，在 UAV-Human 数据集上提高了 8.3-10.4%，在 Drone Action 数据集上提高了 3.2%。 本文提出了一种用于无人机拍摄视频的人类动作识别方法AZTR，结合自动变焦和时序推理技术。 Xijun Wang,Ruiqi Xian,Tianrui Guan,Celso M. de Melo,Stephen M. authors_text: Qiaozi Gao,Govind Thattai,Suhaila Shakiah,Xiaofeng Gao,Shreyas Pansare,Vasu Sharma,Gaurav Sukhatme,Hangjie Shi,Bofei Yang,Desheng Zheng,Lucy Hu,Karthika Arumugam,Shui Hu,Matthew Wen,Dinakar Guthy,Cadence Chung,Rohan Khanna,Osman Ipek,Leslie Ball,Kate Bland,Heather Rocker,Yadunandana Rao,Michael Johnston,Reza Ghanadan,Arindam Mandal, et al. (2 additional authors not shown)
title: Alexa Arena: A User-Centric Interactive Platform for Embodied AI
title_zh: Alexa Arena：以用户为中心的嵌入式人工智能交互平台
abstract: We introduce Alexa Arena, a user-centric simulation platform for Embodied AI (EAI) research. Alexa Arena provides a variety of multi-room layouts and interactable objects, for the creation of human-robot interaction (HRI) missions. With user-friendly graphics and control mechanisms, Alexa Arena supports the development of gamified robotic tasks readily accessible to general human users, thus opening a new venue for high-efficiency HRI data collection and EAI system evaluation. Along with the platform, we introduce a dialog-enabled instruction-following benchmark and provide baseline results for it. We make Alexa Arena publicly available to facilitate research in building generalizable and assistive embodied agents.
summary: We introduce Alexa Arena, a user-centric simulation platform for Embodied AI (EAI) research. Alexa Arena provides a variety of multi-room layouts and interactable objects, for the creation of human-robot interaction (HRI) missions. With user-friendly graphics and control mechanisms, Alexa Arena supports the development of gamified robotic tasks readily accessible to general human users, thus opening a new venue for high-efficiency HRI data collection and EAI system evaluation. Along with the platform, we introduce a dialog-enabled instruction-following benchmark and provide baseline results for it. We make Alexa Arena publicly available to facilitate research in building generalizable and assistive embodied agents.
abstract_zh: 我们推出 Alexa Arena，这是一个以用户为中心的模拟平台，用于 Embodied AI (EAI) 研究。 Alexa Arena 提供各种多房间布局和可交互对象，用于创建人机交互 (HRI) 任务。凭借用户友好的图形和控制机制，Alexa Arena 支持开发普通人类用户可以轻松访问的游戏化机器人任务，从而为高效 HRI 数据收集和 EAI 系统评估开辟了新场所。除了该平台之外，我们还引入了支持对话的指令跟踪基准测试，并为其提供基准结果。我们公开 Alexa Arena，以促进构建可推广和辅助性具体代理的研究。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一个用户为中心的模拟平台Alexa Arena，用于具身人工智能研究，支持创建人机交互任务并高效收集数据。
AI.motivation: 现有具身AI研究缺乏用户友好且高效的交互平台，难以支持大规模人机交互数据的收集与系统评估。
AI.method: 构建多房间布局与可交互物体，设计用户友好界面，开发对话驱动的指令遵循基准测试，并提供基线结果。
AI.result: 实现了一个公开可用的平台，支持游戏化机器人任务开发，并提供了指令遵循基准测试的初始性能数据。
AI.conclusion: Alexa Arena为具身AI研究提供了新途径，推动通用化、助人型智能体的开发与评估。

authors_text: Noah Barrett,Zahra Sadeghi,Stan Matwin
title: Evolutionary Augmentation Policy Optimization for Self-supervised Learning
title_zh: 自监督学习的进化增强策略优化
abstract: Self-supervised Learning (SSL) is a machine learning algorithm for pretraining Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) without requiring manually labeled data. The central idea of this learning technique is based on an auxiliary stage aka pretext task in which labeled data are created automatically through data augmentation and exploited for pretraining the DNN. However, the effect of each pretext task is not well studied or compared in the literature. In this paper, we study the contribution of augmentation operators on the performance of self supervised learning algorithms in a constrained settings. We propose an evolutionary search method for optimization of data augmentation pipeline in pretext tasks and measure the impact of augmentation operators in several SOTA SSL algorithms. By encoding different combination of augmentation operators in chromosomes we seek the optimal augmentation policies through an evolutionary optimization mechanism. We further introduce methods for analyzing and explaining the performance of optimized SSL algorithms. Our results indicate that our proposed method can find solutions that outperform the accuracy of classification of SSL algorithms which confirms the influence of augmentation policy choice on the overall performance of SSL algorithms. We also compare optimal SSL solutions found by our evolutionary search mechanism and show the effect of batch size in the pretext task on two visual datasets.
summary: Self-supervised Learning (SSL) is a machine learning algorithm for pretraining Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) without requiring manually labeled data. The central idea of this learning technique is based on an auxiliary stage aka pretext task in which labeled data are created automatically through data augmentation and exploited for pretraining the DNN. However, the effect of each pretext task is not well studied or compared in the literature. In this paper, we study the contribution of augmentation operators on the performance of self supervised learning algorithms in a constrained settings. We propose an evolutionary search method for optimization of data augmentation pipeline in pretext tasks and measure the impact of augmentation operators in several SOTA SSL algorithms. By encoding different combination of augmentation operators in chromosomes we seek the optimal augmentation policies through an evolutionary optimization mechanism. We further introduce methods for analyzing and explaining the performance of optimized SSL algorithms. Our results indicate that our proposed method can find solutions that outperform the accuracy of classification of SSL algorithms which confirms the influence of augmentation policy choice on the overall performance of SSL algorithms. We also compare optimal SSL solutions found by our evolutionary search mechanism and show the effect of batch size in the pretext task on two visual datasets.
abstract_zh: 自监督学习 (SSL) 是一种机器学习算法，用于预训练深度神经网络 (DNN)，无需手动标记数据。这种学习技术的中心思想基于辅助阶段（又名借口任务），其中通过数据增强自动创建标记数据并用于预训练 DNN。然而，文献中并没有很好地研究或比较每个借口任务的效果。在本文中，我们研究了增强算子在受限环境下对自监督学习算法性能的贡献。我们提出了一种进化搜索方法，用于优化借口任务中的数据增强管道，并测量增强算子在几种 SOTA SSL 算法中的影响。通过在染色体中编码增强算子的不同组合，我们通过进化优化机制寻求最佳增强策略。我们进一步介绍了分析和解释优化 SSL 算法性能的方法。我们的结果表明，我们提出的方法可以找到优于 SSL 算法分类准确性的解决方案，这证实了增强策略选择对 SSL 算法整体性能的影响。我们还比较了通过进化搜索机制找到的最佳 SSL 解决方案，并展示了借口任务中批量大小对两个视觉数据集的影响。

AI.tldr: 研究数据增强策略对自监督学习算法性能的影响，并提出进化方法优化增强策略。
AI.motivation: 现有自监督学习方法中数据增强策略的选择缺乏系统研究，影响模型性能，需优化策略以提高效果。
AI.method: 采用进化搜索方法，将数据增强操作符组合编码为染色体，通过进化机制优化增强策略，并分析优化后算法的性能。
AI.result: 实验表明，优化后的增强策略显著提升SSL分类准确率，并验证了批量大小对预训练任务效果的影响。
AI.conclusion: 数据增强策略的选择显著影响SSL性能，进化优化方法能有效提升模型表现。

We further introduce methods for analyzing and explaining the performance of optimized SSL algorithms. Our results indicate that our proposed method can find solutions that outperform the accuracy of classification of SSL algorithms which confirms the influence of augmentation policy choice on the overall performance of SSL algorithms. We also compare optimal SSL solutions found by our evolutionary search mechanism and show the effect of batch size in the pretext task on two visual datasets. 自监督学习 (SSL) 是一种机器学习算法，用于预训练深度神经网络 (DNN)，无需手动标记数据。这种学习技术的中心思想基于辅助阶段（又名借口任务），其中通过数据增强自动创建标记数据并用于预训练 DNN。然而，文献中并没有很好地研究或比较每个借口任务的效果。在本文中，我们研究了增强算子在受限环境下对自监督学习算法性能的贡献。我们提出了一种进化搜索方法，用于优化借口任务中的数据增强管道，并测量增强算子在几种 SOTA SSL 算法中的影响。通过在染色体中编码增强算子的不同组合，我们通过进化优化机制寻求最佳增强策略。我们进一步介绍了分析和解释优化 SSL 算法性能的方法。我们的结果表明，我们提出的方法可以找到优于 SSL 算法分类准确性的解决方案，这证实了增强策略选择对 SSL 算法整体性能的影响。我们还比较了通过进化搜索机制找到的最佳 SSL 解决方案，并展示了借口任务中批量大小对两个视觉数据集的影响。 研究数据增强策略对自监督学习算法性能的影响，并提出进化方法优化增强策略。 Noah Barrett,Zahra Sadeghi,Stan Matwin cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.NE

authors_text: Iason Katsamenis,Eftychios Protopapadakis,Nikolaos Bakalos,Anastasios Doulamis,Nikolaos Doulamis,Athanasios Voulodimos
title: A Few-Shot Attention Recurrent Residual U-Net for Crack Segmentation
title_zh: 用于裂纹分割的少样本注意力循环残差 U-Net
abstract: Recent studies indicate that deep learning plays a crucial role in the automated visual inspection of road infrastructures. However, current learning schemes are static, implying no dynamic adaptation to users' feedback. To address this drawback, we present a few-shot learning paradigm for the automated segmentation of road cracks, which is based on a U-Net architecture with recurrent residual and attention modules (R2AU-Net). The retraining strategy dynamically fine-tunes the weights of the U-Net as a few new rectified samples are being fed into the classifier. Extensive experiments show that the proposed few-shot R2AU-Net framework outperforms other state-of-the-art networks in terms of Dice and IoU metrics, on a new dataset, named CrackMap, which is made publicly available at https://github.com/ikatsamenis/CrackMap.
summary: Recent studies indicate that deep learning plays a crucial role in the automated visual inspection of road infrastructures. However, current learning schemes are static, implying no dynamic adaptation to users' feedback. To address this drawback, we present a few-shot learning paradigm for the automated segmentation of road cracks, which is based on a U-Net architecture with recurrent residual and attention modules (R2AU-Net). The retraining strategy dynamically fine-tunes the weights of the U-Net as a few new rectified samples are being fed into the classifier. Extensive experiments show that the proposed few-shot R2AU-Net framework outperforms other state-of-the-art networks in terms of Dice and IoU metrics, on a new dataset, named CrackMap, which is made publicly available at https://github.com/ikatsamenis/CrackMap.
abstract_zh: 最近的研究表明，深度学习在道路基础设施的自动视觉检查中发挥着至关重要的作用。然而，当前的学习方案是静态的，这意味着不能动态适应用户的反馈。为了解决这个缺点，我们提出了一种用于道路裂缝自动分割的小样本学习范例，该范例基于具有循环残差和注意模块的 U-Net 架构（R2AU-Net）。当一些新的校正样本被输入到分类器中时，再训练策略会动态地微调 U-Net 的权重。大量实验表明，在名为 CrackMap 的新数据集上，所提出的少样本 R2AU-Net 框架在 Dice 和 IoU 指标方面优于其他最先进的网络，该数据集已在 https://github.com/ikatsamenis/CrackMap 上公开发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于少样本学习的R2AU-Net网络，用于道路裂缝分割任务。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习方法在道路裂缝检测中存在静态学习缺陷，无法动态适应用户反馈且需要大量标注数据。
AI.method: 构建包含递归残差模块和注意力机制的R2AU-Net网络，通过动态微调策略实现少量修正样本的增量学习。
AI.result: 在自建CrackMap数据集上，Dice系数和IoU指标均优于现有SOTA方法，且支持实时模型更新。
AI.conclusion: 提出的少样本学习框架有效提升了裂缝分割的适应性，公开数据集为后续研究提供基准支持。

authors_text: Da Song,Zhijie Wang,Yuheng Huang,Lei Ma,Tianyi Zhang
title: DeepLens: Interactive Out-of-distribution Data Detection in NLP Models
title_zh: DeepLens：NLP 模型中的交互式分布外数据检测
comment: The first two authors contributed equally. To appear in the 2023 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI '23), April 23--28, 2023, Hamburg, Germany
comments: The first two authors contributed equally. To appear in the 2023 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI '23), April 23--28, 2023, Hamburg, Germany
abstract: Machine Learning (ML) has been widely used in Natural Language Processing (NLP) applications. A fundamental assumption in ML is that training data and real-world data should follow a similar distribution. However, a deployed ML model may suffer from out-of-distribution (OOD) issues due to distribution shifts in the real-world data. Though many algorithms have been proposed to detect OOD data from text corpora, there is still a lack of interactive tool support for ML developers. In this work, we propose DeepLens, an interactive system that helps users detect and explore OOD issues in massive text corpora. Users can efficiently explore different OOD types in DeepLens with the help of a text clustering method. Users can also dig into a specific text by inspecting salient words highlighted through neuron activation analysis. In a within-subjects user study with 24 participants, participants using DeepLens were able to find nearly twice more types of OOD issues accurately with 22% more confidence compared with a variant of DeepLens that has no interaction or visualization support.
summary: Machine Learning (ML) has been widely used in Natural Language Processing (NLP) applications. A fundamental assumption in ML is that training data and real-world data should follow a similar distribution. However, a deployed ML model may suffer from out-of-distribution (OOD) issues due to distribution shifts in the real-world data. Though many algorithms have been proposed to detect OOD data from text corpora, there is still a lack of interactive tool support for ML developers. In this work, we propose DeepLens, an interactive system that helps users detect and explore OOD issues in massive text corpora. Users can efficiently explore different OOD types in DeepLens with the help of a text clustering method. Users can also dig into a specific text by inspecting salient words highlighted through neuron activation analysis. In a within-subjects user study with 24 participants, participants using DeepLens were able to find nearly twice more types of OOD issues accurately with 22% more confidence compared with a variant of DeepLens that has no interaction or visualization support.
abstract_zh: 机器学习（ML）已广泛应用于自然语言处理（NLP）应用中。机器学习的一个基本假设是训练数据和现实世界数据应该遵循相似的分布。然而，由于现实世界数据的分布变化，部署的机器学习模型可能会遇到分布外 (OOD) 问题。尽管已经提出了许多算法来从文本语料库中检测 OOD 数据，但仍然缺乏对 ML 开发人员的交互式工具支持。在这项工作中，我们提出了 DeepLens，这是一个交互式系统，可以帮助用户检测和探索海量文本语料库中的 OOD 问题。用户可以借助文本聚类方法有效地探索 DeepLens 中的不同 OOD 类型。用户还可以通过检查神经元激活分析突出显示的显着单词来深入了解特定文本。在一项有 24 名参与者参与的受试者内用户研究中，与没有交互或可视化支持的 DeepLens 变体相比，使用 DeepLens 的参与者能够准确地发现近两倍的 OOD 问题，置信度提高 22%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DeepLens系统，通过交互式工具帮助用户检测和探索自然语言处理模型中的分布外数据问题。
AI.motivation: 尽管已有许多检测分布外数据的算法，但缺乏交互式工具支持，导致开发者难以高效发现和解决实际应用中的分布偏移问题。
AI.method: 结合文本聚类方法与神经元激活分析，通过交互式可视化界面帮助用户探索不同类型的分布外数据，并深入分析关键文本特征。
AI.result: 用户实验表明，使用DeepLens的参与者相比无交互版本，能准确发现近两倍的分布外问题类型，且置信度提升22%。
AI.conclusion: 交互式可视化工具显著提升分布外数据检测效率和准确性，验证了DeepLens在实际应用中的有效性。

authors_text: Zhijie Wang,Yuheng Huang,Da Song,Lei Ma,Tianyi Zhang
title: DeepSeer: Interactive RNN Explanation and Debugging via State Abstraction
title_zh: DeepSeer：通过状态抽象进行交互式 RNN 解释和调试
comment: To appear in the 2023 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI '23), April 23--28, 2023, Hamburg, Germany
comments: To appear in the 2023 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI '23), April 23--28, 2023, Hamburg, Germany
abstract: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) have been widely used in Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks given its superior performance on processing sequential data. However, it is challenging to interpret and debug RNNs due to the inherent complexity and the lack of transparency of RNNs. While many explainable AI (XAI) techniques have been proposed for RNNs, most of them only support local explanations rather than global explanations. In this paper, we present DeepSeer, an interactive system that provides both global and local explanations of RNN behavior in multiple tightly-coordinated views for model understanding and debugging. The core of DeepSeer is a state abstraction method that bundles semantically similar hidden states in an RNN model and abstracts the model as a finite state machine. Users can explore the global model behavior by inspecting text patterns associated with each state and the transitions between states. Users can also dive into individual predictions by inspecting the state trace and intermediate prediction results of a given input. A between-subjects user study with 28 participants shows that, compared with a popular XAI technique, LIME, participants using DeepSeer made a deeper and more comprehensive assessment of RNN model behavior, identified the root causes of incorrect predictions more accurately, and came up with more actionable plans to improve the model performance.
summary: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) have been widely used in Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks given its superior performance on processing sequential data. However, it is challenging to interpret and debug RNNs due to the inherent complexity and the lack of transparency of RNNs. While many explainable AI (XAI) techniques have been proposed for RNNs, most of them only support local explanations rather than global explanations. In this paper, we present DeepSeer, an interactive system that provides both global and local explanations of RNN behavior in multiple tightly-coordinated views for model understanding and debugging. The core of DeepSeer is a state abstraction method that bundles semantically similar hidden states in an RNN model and abstracts the model as a finite state machine. Users can explore the global model behavior by inspecting text patterns associated with each state and the transitions between states. Users can also dive into individual predictions by inspecting the state trace and intermediate prediction results of a given input. A between-subjects user study with 28 participants shows that, compared with a popular XAI technique, LIME, participants using DeepSeer made a deeper and more comprehensive assessment of RNN model behavior, identified the root causes of incorrect predictions more accurately, and came up with more actionable plans to improve the model performance.
abstract_zh: 递归神经网络（RNN）因其在处理顺序数据方面的卓越性能而被广泛应用于自然语言处理（NLP）任务中。然而，由于 RNN 固有的复杂性和缺乏透明度，解释和调试 RNN 具有挑战性。虽然已经针对 RNN 提出了许多可解释的人工智能（XAI）技术，但其中大多数仅支持局部解释而不是全局解释。在本文中，我们提出了 DeepSeer，这是一个交互式系统，它在多个紧密协调的视图中提供 RNN 行为的全局和局部解释，用于模型理解和调试。 DeepSeer的核心是一种状态抽象方法，它将语义相似的隐藏状态捆绑在RNN模型中，并将模型抽象为有限状态机。用户可以通过检查与每个状态相关的文本模式以及状态之间的转换来探索全局模型行为。用户还可以通过检查给定输入的状态轨迹和中间预测结果来深入进行单独的预测。一项包含 28 名参与者的受试者间用户研究表明，与流行的 XAI 技术 LIME 相比，使用 DeepSeer 的参与者对 RNN 模型行为进行了更深入、更全面的评估，更准确地识别了错误预测的根本原因，并提出了更可行的计划来提高模型性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DeepSeer系统，通过状态抽象方法实现对循环神经网络（RNN）的全局与局部解释，辅助模型理解和调试。
AI.motivation: RNN模型因复杂性和不透明性难以解释和调试，现有可解释AI技术多仅支持局部解释，缺乏全局视角。
AI.method: 核心方法为状态抽象，将语义相似的隐藏状态聚类为有限状态机，并通过多视图交互系统展示全局行为模式及个体预测轨迹。
AI.result: 28名参与者的对比实验表明，与LIME等方法相比，使用DeepSeer的用户能更深入分析模型行为、更准确定位预测错误根源并提出改进方案。

search_text: DeepSeer: Interactive RNN Explanation and Debugging via State Abstraction DeepSeer：通过状态抽象进行交互式 RNN 解释和调试 Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) have been widely used in Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks given its superior performance on processing sequential data. However, it is challenging to interpret and debug RNNs due to the inherent complexity and the lack of transparency of RNNs. While many explainable AI (XAI) techniques have been proposed for RNNs, most of them only support local explanations rather than global explanations. In this paper, we present DeepSeer, an interactive system that provides both global and local explanations of RNN behavior in multiple tightly-coordinated views for model understanding and debugging. The core of DeepSeer is a state abstraction method that bundles semantically similar hidden states in an RNN model and abstracts the model as a finite state machine. Users can explore the global model behavior by inspecting text patterns associated with each state and the transitions between states. Users can also dive into individual predictions by inspecting the state trace and intermediate prediction results of a given input. A between-subjects user study with 28 participants shows that, compared with a popular XAI technique, LIME, participants using DeepSeer made a deeper and more comprehensive assessment of RNN model behavior, identified the root causes of incorrect predictions more accurately, and came up with more actionable plans to improve the model performance. 递归神经网络（RNN）因其在处理顺序数据方面的卓越性能而被广泛应用于自然语言处理（NLP）任务中。然而，由于 RNN 固有的复杂性和缺乏透明度，解释和调试 RNN 具有挑战性。虽然已经针对 RNN 提出了许多可解释的人工智能（XAI）技术，但其中大多数仅支持局部解释而不是全局解释。在本文中，我们提出了 DeepSeer，这是一个交互式系统，它在多个紧密协调的视图中提供 RNN 行为的全局和局部解释，用于模型理解和调试。 DeepSeer的核心是一种状态抽象方法，它将语义相似的隐藏状态捆绑在RNN模型中，并将模型抽象为有限状态机。用户可以通过检查与每个状态相关的文本模式以及状态之间的转换来探索全局模型行为。用户还可以通过检查给定输入的状态轨迹和中间预测结果来深入进行单独的预测。一项包含 28 名参与者的受试者间用户研究表明，与流行的 XAI 技术 LIME 相比，使用 DeepSeer 的参与者对 RNN 模型行为进行了更深入、更全面的评估，更准确地识别了错误预测的根本原因，并提出了更可行的计划来提高模型性能。 本文提出DeepSeer系统，通过状态抽象方法实现对循环神经网络（RNN）的全局与局部解释，辅助模型理解和调试。 Zhijie Wang,Yuheng Huang,Da Song,Lei Ma,Tianyi Zhang cs.HC,cs.LG,cs.SE

authors_text: Arpit Narechania,Fan Du,Atanu R Sinha,Ryan A. Rossi,Jane Hoffswell,Shunan Guo,Eunyee Koh,Shamkant B. Navathe,Alex Endert
title: DataPilot: Utilizing Quality and Usage Information for Subset Selection during Visual Data Preparation
title_zh: DataPilot：在可视化数据准备过程中利用质量和使用信息进行子集选择
abstract: Selecting relevant data subsets from large, unfamiliar datasets can be difficult. We address this challenge by modeling and visualizing two kinds of auxiliary information: (1) quality - the validity and appropriateness of data required to perform certain analytical tasks; and (2) usage - the historical utilization characteristics of data across multiple users. Through a design study with 14 data workers, we integrate this information into a visual data preparation and analysis tool, DataPilot. DataPilot presents visual cues about "the good, the bad, and the ugly" aspects of data and provides graphical user interface controls as interaction affordances, guiding users to perform subset selection. Through a study with 36 participants, we investigate how DataPilot helps users navigate a large, unfamiliar tabular dataset, prepare a relevant subset, and build a visualization dashboard. We find that users selected smaller, effective subsets with higher quality and usage, and with greater success and confidence.
summary: Selecting relevant data subsets from large, unfamiliar datasets can be difficult. We address this challenge by modeling and visualizing two kinds of auxiliary information: (1) quality - the validity and appropriateness of data required to perform certain analytical tasks; and (2) usage - the historical utilization characteristics of data across multiple users. Through a design study with 14 data workers, we integrate this information into a visual data preparation and analysis tool, DataPilot. DataPilot presents visual cues about "the good, the bad, and the ugly" aspects of data and provides graphical user interface controls as interaction affordances, guiding users to perform subset selection. Through a study with 36 participants, we investigate how DataPilot helps users navigate a large, unfamiliar tabular dataset, prepare a relevant subset, and build a visualization dashboard. We find that users selected smaller, effective subsets with higher quality and usage, and with greater success and confidence.
abstract_zh: 从大型、不熟悉的数据集中选择相关数据子集可能很困难。我们通过建模和可视化两种辅助信息来应对这一挑战：（1）质量——执行某些分析任务所需数据的有效性和适当性； (2) 使用情况——跨多个用户的数据的历史使用特征。通过与 14 名数据工作者的设计研究，我们将这些信息集成到可视化数据准备和分析工具 DataPilot 中。 DataPilot 呈现有关数据“好、坏、丑”方面的视觉提示，并提供图形用户界面控件作为交互可供性，指导用户执行子集选择。通过对 36 名参与者的研究，我们调查了 DataPilot 如何帮助用户导航大型、不熟悉的表格数据集、准备相关子集并构建可视化仪表板。我们发现，用户选择了更小、更有效、质量更高、使用率更高的子集，并且获得了更大的成功和信心。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DataPilot工具，通过整合数据质量与使用信息辅助用户从大型陌生数据集中选择有效子集。
AI.motivation: 在处理大型陌生数据集时，用户难以快速筛选出高质量且符合分析需求的数据子集，传统方法缺乏对数据质量与历史使用情况的综合考量。
AI.method: 设计并实现可视化工具DataPilot，通过建模数据质量（有效性与适用性）和使用特征（多用户历史行为），结合视觉提示与交互控件引导用户选择子集。
AI.result: 36名参与者实验表明，用户能更高效地筛选出高质量、高使用频率的子集，且完成任务的成功率与置信度显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 将数据质量与使用信息可视化集成于数据准备流程，可有效提升用户选择子集的效率与准确性，验证了DataPilot设计的有效性。

authors_text: Shubhankar Borse,Debasmit Das,Hyojin Park,Hong Cai,Risheek Garrepalli,Fatih Porikli
title: DejaVu: Conditional Regenerative Learning to Enhance Dense Prediction
title_zh: DejaVu：条件再生学习增强密集预测
abstract: We present DejaVu, a novel framework which leverages conditional image regeneration as additional supervision during training to improve deep networks for dense prediction tasks such as segmentation, depth estimation, and surface normal prediction. First, we apply redaction to the input image, which removes certain structural information by sparse sampling or selective frequency removal. Next, we use a conditional regenerator, which takes the redacted image and the dense predictions as inputs, and reconstructs the original image by filling in the missing structural information. In the redacted image, structural attributes like boundaries are broken while semantic context is largely preserved. In order to make the regeneration feasible, the conditional generator will then require the structure information from the other input source, i.e., the dense predictions. As such, by including this conditional regeneration objective during training, DejaVu encourages the base network to learn to embed accurate scene structure in its dense prediction. This leads to more accurate predictions with clearer boundaries and better spatial consistency. When it is feasible to leverage additional computation, DejaVu can be extended to incorporate an attention-based regeneration module within the dense prediction network, which further improves accuracy. Through extensive experiments on multiple dense prediction benchmarks such as Cityscapes, COCO, ADE20K, NYUD-v2, and KITTI, we demonstrate the efficacy of employing DejaVu during training, as it outperforms SOTA methods at no added computation cost.
summary: We present DejaVu, a novel framework which leverages conditional image regeneration as additional supervision during training to improve deep networks for dense prediction tasks such as segmentation, depth estimation, and surface normal prediction. First, we apply redaction to the input image, which removes certain structural information by sparse sampling or selective frequency removal. Next, we use a conditional regenerator, which takes the redacted image and the dense predictions as inputs, and reconstructs the original image by filling in the missing structural information. In the redacted image, structural attributes like boundaries are broken while semantic context is largely preserved. In order to make the regeneration feasible, the conditional generator will then require the structure information from the other input source, i.e., the dense predictions. As such, by including this conditional regeneration objective during training, DejaVu encourages the base network to learn to embed accurate scene structure in its dense prediction. This leads to more accurate predictions with clearer boundaries and better spatial consistency. When it is feasible to leverage additional computation, DejaVu can be extended to incorporate an attention-based regeneration module within the dense prediction network, which further improves accuracy. Through extensive experiments on multiple dense prediction benchmarks such as Cityscapes, COCO, ADE20K, NYUD-v2, and KITTI, we demonstrate the efficacy of employing DejaVu during training, as it outperforms SOTA methods at no added computation cost.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 DejaVu，这是一种新颖的框架，它利用条件图像再生作为训练过程中的额外监督，以改进深度网络以执行分割、深度估计和表面法线预测等密集预测任务。首先，我们对输入图像应用编辑，通过稀疏采样或选择性频率去除来去除某些结构信息。接下来，我们使用条件再生器，它将编辑图像和密集预测作为输入，并通过填充缺失的结构信息来重建原始图像。在经过编辑的图像中，边界等结构属性被打破，而语义上下文在很大程度上得以保留。为了使再生可行，条件生成器将需要来自其他输入源的结构信息，即密集预测。因此，通过在训练期间包含此条件再生目标，DejaVu 鼓励基础网络学习在其密集预测中嵌入准确的场景结构。这可以带来更准确的预测、更清晰的边界和更好的空间一致性。当可以利用额外的计算时，DejaVu 可以进行扩展，将基于注意力的再生模块纳入密集预测网络中，从而进一步提高准确性。通过对 Cityscapes、COCO、ADE20K、NYUD-v2 和 KITTI 等多个密集预测基准进行大量实验，我们证明了在训练期间使用 DejaVu 的功效，因为它在不增加计算成本的情况下优于 SOTA 方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DejaVu框架，通过条件图像再生作为额外监督，提升密集预测任务（如分割、深度估计）的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有密集预测模型在结构信息（如边界）的学习上存在不足，导致预测结果边界模糊、空间一致性差。
AI.method: 通过红action（稀疏采样或频率移除）破坏输入图像结构，利用条件再生器结合密集预测结果重建原始图像，迫使模型学习更精确的场景结构。
AI.result: 在Cityscapes、ADE20K等基准测试中，DejaVu在无额外计算成本下超越现有最先进方法，边界清晰度和空间一致性显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 条件再生学习能有效增强密集预测模型的结构感知能力，为后续研究提供新思路。

search_text: DejaVu: Conditional Regenerative Learning to Enhance Dense Prediction DejaVu：条件再生学习增强密集预测 We present DejaVu, a novel framework which leverages conditional image regeneration as additional supervision during training to improve deep networks for dense prediction tasks such as segmentation, depth estimation, and surface normal prediction. First, we apply redaction to the input image, which removes certain structural information by sparse sampling or selective frequency removal. Next, we use a conditional regenerator, which takes the redacted image and the dense predictions as inputs, and reconstructs the original image by filling in the missing structural information. In the redacted image, structural attributes like boundaries are broken while semantic context is largely preserved. In order to make the regeneration feasible, the conditional generator will then require the structure information from the other input source, i.e., the dense predictions. As such, by including this conditional regeneration objective during training, DejaVu encourages the base network to learn to embed accurate scene structure in its dense prediction. This leads to more accurate predictions with clearer boundaries and better spatial consistency. When it is feasible to leverage additional computation, DejaVu can be extended to incorporate an attention-based regeneration module within the dense prediction network, which further improves accuracy. Through extensive experiments on multiple dense prediction benchmarks such as Cityscapes, COCO, ADE20K, NYUD-v2, and KITTI, we demonstrate the efficacy of employing DejaVu during training, as it outperforms SOTA methods at no added computation cost. 我们提出了 DejaVu，这是一种新颖的框架，它利用条件图像再生作为训练过程中的额外监督，以改进深度网络以执行分割、深度估计和表面法线预测等密集预测任务。首先，我们对输入图像应用编辑，通过稀疏采样或选择性频率去除来去除某些结构信息。接下来，我们使用条件再生器，它将编辑图像和密集预测作为输入，并通过填充缺失的结构信息来重建原始图像。在经过编辑的图像中，边界等结构属性被打破，而语义上下文在很大程度上得以保留。为了使再生可行，条件生成器将需要来自其他输入源的结构信息，即密集预测。因此，通过在训练期间包含此条件再生目标，DejaVu 鼓励基础网络学习在其密集预测中嵌入准确的场景结构。这可以带来更准确的预测、更清晰的边界和更好的空间一致性。当可以利用额外的计算时，DejaVu 可以进行扩展，将基于注意力的再生模块纳入密集预测网络中，从而进一步提高准确性。通过对 Cityscapes、COCO、ADE20K、NYUD-v2 和 KITTI 等多个密集预测基准进行大量实验，我们证明了在训练期间使用 DejaVu 的功效，因为它在不增加计算成本的情况下优于 SOTA 方法。 本文提出DejaVu框架，通过条件图像再生作为额外监督，提升密集预测任务（如分割、深度估计）的性能。 Shubhankar Borse,Debasmit Das,Hyojin Park,Hong Cai,Risheek Garrepalli,Fatih Porikli cs.CV

authors_text: Matthias Rath,Alexandru Paul Condurache
title: Deep Neural Networks with Efficient Guaranteed Invariances
title_zh: 具有有效保证不变性的深度神经网络
abstract: We address the problem of improving the performance and in particular the sample complexity of deep neural networks by enforcing and guaranteeing invariances to symmetry transformations rather than learning them from data. Group-equivariant convolutions are a popular approach to obtain equivariant representations. The desired corresponding invariance is then imposed using pooling operations. For rotations, it has been shown that using invariant integration instead of pooling further improves the sample complexity. In this contribution, we first expand invariant integration beyond rotations to flips and scale transformations. We then address the problem of incorporating multiple desired invariances into a single network. For this purpose, we propose a multi-stream architecture, where each stream is invariant to a different transformation such that the network can simultaneously benefit from multiple invariances. We demonstrate our approach with successful experiments on Scaled-MNIST, SVHN, CIFAR-10 and STL-10.
summary: We address the problem of improving the performance and in particular the sample complexity of deep neural networks by enforcing and guaranteeing invariances to symmetry transformations rather than learning them from data. Group-equivariant convolutions are a popular approach to obtain equivariant representations. The desired corresponding invariance is then imposed using pooling operations. For rotations, it has been shown that using invariant integration instead of pooling further improves the sample complexity. In this contribution, we first expand invariant integration beyond rotations to flips and scale transformations. We then address the problem of incorporating multiple desired invariances into a single network. For this purpose, we propose a multi-stream architecture, where each stream is invariant to a different transformation such that the network can simultaneously benefit from multiple invariances. We demonstrate our approach with successful experiments on Scaled-MNIST, SVHN, CIFAR-10 and STL-10.
abstract_zh: 我们通过强制和保证对称变换的不变性而不是从数据中学习它们来解决提高深度神经网络的性能，特别是样本复杂性的问题。组等变卷积是获得等变表示的流行方法。然后使用池化操作来施加所需的相应不变性。对于旋转，事实证明，使用不变积分而不是合并可以进一步提高样本复杂性。在这篇文章中，我们首先将不变积分扩展到旋转之外的翻转和缩放变换。然后，我们解决将多个所需的不变性合并到单个网络中的问题。为此，我们提出了一种多流架构，其中每个流对于不同的变换都是不变的，以便网络可以同时受益于多个不变性。我们通过在 Scaled-MNIST、SVHN、CIFAR-10 和 STL-10 上的成功实验展示了我们的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过强制保证对称变换的不变性来提升深度神经网络的性能和样本复杂度。
AI.motivation: 传统方法依赖数据学习不变性，导致样本复杂度高，而强制保证不变性可提升效率。
AI.method: 扩展不变积分至翻转和缩放变换，并设计多流架构以同时整合多种不变性。
AI.result: 在Scaled-MNIST、SVHN等数据集上验证了方法有效性，性能优于现有技术。
AI.conclusion: 多流架构能有效结合多种不变性，显著提升网络效率与泛化能力。

authors_text: Achkan Salehi,Stephane Doncieux
title: Data-efficient, Explainable and Safe Box Manipulation: Illustrating the Advantages of Physical Priors in Model-Predictive Control
title_zh: 数据高效、可解释且安全的盒子操作：说明物理先验在模型预测控制中的优势
comment: accepted for publication by l4dc 2024, 12 pages (with references), 4 figures, 2 tables
comments: accepted for publication by l4dc 2024, 12 pages (with references), 4 figures, 2 tables
abstract: Model-based RL/control have gained significant traction in robotics. Yet, these approaches often remain data-inefficient and lack the explainability of hand-engineered solutions. This makes them difficult to debug/integrate in safety-critical settings. However, in many systems, prior knowledge of environment kinematics/dynamics is available. Incorporating such priors can help address the aforementioned problems by reducing problem complexity and the need for exploration, while also facilitating the expression of the decisions taken by the agent in terms of physically meaningful entities. Our aim with this paper is to illustrate and support this point of view via a case-study. We model a payload manipulation problem based on a real robotic system, and show that leveraging prior knowledge about the dynamics of the environment in an MPC framework can lead to improvements in explainability, safety and data-efficiency, leading to satisfying generalization properties with less data.
summary: Model-based RL/control have gained significant traction in robotics. Yet, these approaches often remain data-inefficient and lack the explainability of hand-engineered solutions. This makes them difficult to debug/integrate in safety-critical settings. However, in many systems, prior knowledge of environment kinematics/dynamics is available. Incorporating such priors can help address the aforementioned problems by reducing problem complexity and the need for exploration, while also facilitating the expression of the decisions taken by the agent in terms of physically meaningful entities. Our aim with this paper is to illustrate and support this point of view via a case-study. We model a payload manipulation problem based on a real robotic system, and show that leveraging prior knowledge about the dynamics of the environment in an MPC framework can lead to improvements in explainability, safety and data-efficiency, leading to satisfying generalization properties with less data.
abstract_zh: 基于模型的强化学习/控制在机器人技术领域获得了巨大的关注。然而，这些方法通常数据效率低下，并且缺乏手工设计解决方案的可解释性。这使得它们难以在安全关键设置中进行调试/集成。然而，在许多系统中，可以获得环境运动学/动力学的先验知识。结合此类先验可以通过降低问题复杂性和探索需求来帮助解决上述问题，同时还有助于以物理上有意义的实体的形式表达代理所做出的决策。我们撰写本文的目的是通过案例研究来说明和支持这一观点。我们基于真实的机器人系统对有效负载操纵问题进行建模，并表明利用 MPC 框架中有关环境动态的先验知识可以提高可解释性、安全性和数据效率，从而用更少的数据满足泛化属性。

AI.tldr: 本文通过案例研究展示了在模型预测控制（MPC）框架中结合物理先验知识，如何提升机器人抓取任务的数据效率、可解释性和安全性。
AI.motivation: 基于模型的强化学习/控制方法在机器人领域虽受关注，但存在数据效率低、可解释性差的问题，难以满足安全关键场景的需求。利用环境动力学先验知识可缓解这些问题。
AI.method: 在真实机器人系统中建模负载操作问题，通过MPC框架整合环境动力学的物理先验知识，减少探索需求并增强决策的物理可解释性。
AI.result: 实验表明，结合物理先验的MPC方法在数据效率、安全性、可解释性及泛化能力方面均优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: 物理先验知识的引入能有效提升模型预测控制在机器人操作任务中的性能，为安全关键场景提供可行解决方案。

authors_text: Haozhe Liu,Wentian Zhang,Bing Li,Haoqian Wu,Nanjun He,Yawen Huang,Yuexiang Li,Bernard Ghanem,Yefeng Zheng
title: Improving GAN Training via Feature Space Shrinkage
title_zh: 通过特征空间收缩改进 GAN 训练
comment: Accepted by CVPR'2023. Code and Demo are available at https://github.com/WentianZhang-ML/AdaptiveMix
comments: Accepted by CVPR'2023. Code and Demo are available at https://github.com/WentianZhang-ML/AdaptiveMix
abstract: Due to the outstanding capability for data generation, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have attracted considerable attention in unsupervised learning. However, training GANs is difficult, since the training distribution is dynamic for the discriminator, leading to unstable image representation. In this paper, we address the problem of training GANs from a novel perspective, \emph{i.e.,} robust image classification. Motivated by studies on robust image representation, we propose a simple yet effective module, namely AdaptiveMix, for GANs, which shrinks the regions of training data in the image representation space of the discriminator. Considering it is intractable to directly bound feature space, we propose to construct hard samples and narrow down the feature distance between hard and easy samples. The hard samples are constructed by mixing a pair of training images. We evaluate the effectiveness of our AdaptiveMix with widely-used and state-of-the-art GAN architectures. The evaluation results demonstrate that our AdaptiveMix can facilitate the training of GANs and effectively improve the image quality of generated samples. We also show that our AdaptiveMix can be further applied to image classification and Out-Of-Distribution (OOD) detection tasks, by equipping it with state-of-the-art methods. Extensive experiments on seven publicly available datasets show that our method effectively boosts the performance of baselines. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/WentianZhang-ML/AdaptiveMix.
summary: Due to the outstanding capability for data generation, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have attracted considerable attention in unsupervised learning. However, training GANs is difficult, since the training distribution is dynamic for the discriminator, leading to unstable image representation. In this paper, we address the problem of training GANs from a novel perspective, \emph{i.e.,} robust image classification. Motivated by studies on robust image representation, we propose a simple yet effective module, namely AdaptiveMix, for GANs, which shrinks the regions of training data in the image representation space of the discriminator. Considering it is intractable to directly bound feature space, we propose to construct hard samples and narrow down the feature distance between hard and easy samples. The hard samples are constructed by mixing a pair of training images. We evaluate the effectiveness of our AdaptiveMix with widely-used and state-of-the-art GAN architectures. The evaluation results demonstrate that our AdaptiveMix can facilitate the training of GANs and effectively improve the image quality of generated samples. We also show that our AdaptiveMix can be further applied to image classification and Out-Of-Distribution (OOD) detection tasks, by equipping it with state-of-the-art methods. Extensive experiments on seven publicly available datasets show that our method effectively boosts the performance of baselines. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/WentianZhang-ML/AdaptiveMix.
abstract_zh: 由于出色的数据生成能力，生成对抗网络（GAN）在无监督学习领域引起了广泛关注。然而，训练 GAN 很困难，因为判别器的训练分布是动态的，导致图像表示不稳定。在本文中，我们从一个新的角度解决了训练 GAN 的问题，即鲁棒图像分类。受鲁棒图像表示研究的启发，我们为 GAN 提出了一个简单而有效的模块，即 AdaptiveMix，它缩小了鉴别器图像表示空间中训练数据的区域。考虑到直接绑定特征空间的困难，我们建议构造困难样本并缩小困难样本和简单样本之间的特征距离。硬样本是通过混合一对训练图像来构造的。我们使用广泛使用且最先进的 GAN 架构来评估 AdaptiveMix 的有效性。评估结果表明，我们的AdaptiveMix可以促进GAN的训练并有效提高生成样本的图像质量。我们还表明，通过配备最先进的方法，我们的 AdaptiveMix 可以进一步应用于图像分类和分布外 (OOD) 检测任务。对七个公开数据集的广泛实验表明，我们的方法有效地提高了基线的性能。该代码可在 https://github.com/WentianZhang-ML/AdaptiveMix 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为AdaptiveMix的模块，通过特征空间收缩来改进生成对抗网络（GAN）的训练过程。
AI.motivation: 由于GAN训练过程中判别器的动态训练分布导致图像表示不稳定，训练困难。受鲁棒图像表示研究的启发，本文从稳健图像分类的角度出发，提出改进方法。
AI.method: 提出AdaptiveMix模块，通过混合训练图像生成硬样本，并缩小硬样本与易样本之间的特征距离，从而在判别器的图像表示空间中收缩训练数据区域。
AI.result: 实验表明，AdaptiveMix有效提升了GAN的训练稳定性与生成图像质量，并可扩展应用于图像分类和分布外检测任务，在七个公开数据集上均优于基线方法。
AI.conclusion: AdaptiveMix通过特征空间收缩机制显著改善了GAN训练效果，同时具备跨任务应用潜力，为生成模型优化提供了新思路。

search_text: Improving GAN Training via Feature Space Shrinkage 通过特征空间收缩改进 GAN 训练 Due to the outstanding capability for data generation, Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have attracted considerable attention in unsupervised learning. However, training GANs is difficult, since the training distribution is dynamic for the discriminator, leading to unstable image representation. In this paper, we address the problem of training GANs from a novel perspective, \emph{i.e.,} robust image classification. Motivated by studies on robust image representation, we propose a simple yet effective module, namely AdaptiveMix, for GANs, which shrinks the regions of training data in the image representation space of the discriminator. Considering it is intractable to directly bound feature space, we propose to construct hard samples and narrow down the feature distance between hard and easy samples. The hard samples are constructed by mixing a pair of training images. We evaluate the effectiveness of our AdaptiveMix with widely-used and state-of-the-art GAN architectures. The evaluation results demonstrate that our AdaptiveMix can facilitate the training of GANs and effectively improve the image quality of generated samples. We also show that our AdaptiveMix can be further applied to image classification and Out-Of-Distribution (OOD) detection tasks, by equipping it with state-of-the-art methods. Extensive experiments on seven publicly available datasets show that our method effectively boosts the performance of baselines. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/WentianZhang-ML/AdaptiveMix. 由于出色的数据生成能力，生成对抗网络（GAN）在无监督学习领域引起了广泛关注。然而，训练 GAN 很困难，因为判别器的训练分布是动态的，导致图像表示不稳定。在本文中，我们从一个新的角度解决了训练 GAN 的问题，即鲁棒图像分类。受鲁棒图像表示研究的启发，我们为 GAN 提出了一个简单而有效的模块，即 AdaptiveMix，它缩小了鉴别器图像表示空间中训练数据的区域。考虑到直接绑定特征空间的困难，我们建议构造困难样本并缩小困难样本和简单样本之间的特征距离。硬样本是通过混合一对训练图像来构造的。我们使用广泛使用且最先进的 GAN 架构来评估 AdaptiveMix 的有效性。评估结果表明，我们的AdaptiveMix可以促进GAN的训练并有效提高生成样本的图像质量。我们还表明，通过配备最先进的方法，我们的 AdaptiveMix 可以进一步应用于图像分类和分布外 (OOD) 检测任务。对七个公开数据集的广泛实验表明，我们的方法有效地提高了基线的性能。该代码可在 https://github.com/WentianZhang-ML/AdaptiveMix 上公开获取。 本文提出了一种名为AdaptiveMix的模块，通过特征空间收缩来改进生成对抗网络（GAN）的训练过程。 Haozhe Liu,Wentian Zhang,Bing Li,Haoqian Wu,Nanjun He,Yawen Huang,Yuexiang Li,Bernard Ghanem,Yefeng Zheng cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Foivos Tsimpourlas,Pavlos Petoumenos,Min Xu,Chris Cummins,Kim Hazelwood,Ajitha Rajan,Hugh Leather
title: BenchDirect: A Directed Language Model for Compiler Benchmarks
title_zh: BenchDirect：编译器基准的定向语言模型
comment: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2208.06555
comments: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2208.06555
abstract: The exponential increase of hardware-software complexity has made it impossible for compiler engineers to find the right optimization heuristics manually. Predictive models have been shown to find near optimal heuristics with little human effort but they are limited by a severe lack of diverse benchmarks to train on. Generative AI has been used by researchers to synthesize benchmarks into existing datasets. However, the synthetic programs are short, exceedingly simple and lacking diversity in their features. We develop BenchPress, the first ML compiler benchmark generator that can be directed within source code feature representations. BenchPress synthesizes executable functions by infilling code that conditions on the program's left and right context. BenchPress uses active learning to introduce new benchmarks with unseen features into the dataset of Grewe's et al. CPU vs GPU heuristic, improving its acquired performance by 50%. BenchPress targets features that has been impossible for other synthesizers to reach. In 3 feature spaces, we outperform human-written code from GitHub, CLgen, CLSmith and the SRCIROR mutator in targeting the features of Rodinia benchmarks. BenchPress steers generation with beam search over a feature-agnostic language model. We improve this with BenchDirect which utilizes a directed LM that infills programs by jointly observing source code context and the compiler features that are targeted. BenchDirect achieves up to 36% better accuracy in targeting the features of Rodinia benchmarks, it is 1.8x more likely to give an exact match and it speeds up execution time by up to 72% compared to BenchPress. Both our models produce code that is difficult to distinguish from human-written code. We conduct a Turing test which shows our models' synthetic benchmarks are labelled as 'human-written' as often as human-written code from GitHub.

Predictive models have been shown to find near optimal heuristics with little human effort but they are limited by a severe lack of diverse benchmarks to train on. Generative AI has been used by researchers to synthesize benchmarks into existing datasets. However, the synthetic programs are short, exceedingly simple and lacking diversity in their features. We develop BenchPress, the first ML compiler benchmark generator that can be directed within source code feature representations. BenchPress synthesizes executable functions by infilling code that conditions on the program's left and right context. BenchPress uses active learning to introduce new benchmarks with unseen features into the dataset of Grewe's et al. CPU vs GPU heuristic, improving its acquired performance by 50%. BenchPress targets features that has been impossible for other synthesizers to reach. In 3 feature spaces, we outperform human-written code from GitHub, CLgen, CLSmith and the SRCIROR mutator in targeting the features of Rodinia benchmarks. BenchPress steers generation with beam search over a feature-agnostic language model. We improve this with BenchDirect which utilizes a directed LM that infills programs by jointly observing source code context and the compiler features that are targeted. BenchDirect achieves up to 36% better accuracy in targeting the features of Rodinia benchmarks, it is 1.8x more likely to give an exact match and it speeds up execution time by up to 72% compared to BenchPress. Both our models produce code that is difficult to distinguish from human-written code. We conduct a Turing test which shows our models' synthetic benchmarks are labelled as 'human-written' as often as human-written code from GitHub.
abstract_zh: 硬件软件复杂性的指数级增长使得编译器工程师无法手动找到正确的优化启发式方法。预测模型已被证明可以在几乎不需要人类努力的情况下找到接近最佳的启发式方法，但由于严重缺乏多样化的训练基准而受到限制。研究人员已使用生成式人工智能将基准综合到现有数据集中。然而，合成程序很短，极其简单，并且功能缺乏多样性。我们开发了 BenchPress，这是第一个可以在源代码特征表示中定向的 ML 编译器基准生成器。 BenchPress 通过填充以程序左右上下文为条件的代码来合成可执行函数。 BenchPress 使用主动学习将具有未见特征的新基准引入 Grewe 等人的数据集中。 CPU vs GPU 启发式，将其获得的性能提高了 50%。 BenchPress 的目标是其他合成器无法达到的功能。在 3 个特征空间中，我们在针对 Rodinia 基准测试的特征方面优于来自 GitHub、CLgen、CLSmith 和 SRCIROR 变异器的人类编写的代码。 BenchPress 通过在与特征无关的语言模型上进行波束搜索来引导生成。我们通过 BenchDirect 改进了这一点，它利用定向 LM，通过联合观察源代码上下文和目标编译器功能来填充程序。与 BenchPress 相比，BenchDirect 在针对 Rodinia 基准测试功能方面的准确度提高了 36%，提供精确匹配的可能性提高了 1.8 倍，并且执行时间加快了 72%。我们的两种模型生成的代码都很难与人类编写的代码区分开来。我们进行了图灵测试，结果显示我们模型的综合基准被标记为“人类编写”的频率与来自 GitHub 的人类编写的代码一样频繁。

AI.tldr: 本文提出BenchDirect，一种定向语言模型，用于生成具有特定编译器特征的高质量合成基准测试程序。
AI.motivation: 传统编译器优化启发式方法难以手动确定，现有预测模型因缺乏多样化基准数据集效果受限，而现有合成基准存在代码简单、特征单一的问题。
AI.method: 开发BenchPress作为基础模型，通过主动学习扩展基准数据集；进一步提出BenchDirect，结合源代码上下文与目标编译器特征，使用定向语言模型进行代码填充生成基准。
AI.result: BenchDirect在Rodinia基准特征目标准确率提升36%，精确匹配概率提高1.8倍，执行速度加快72%，合成代码通过图灵测试难以与人类代码区分。
AI.conclusion: BenchDirect有效生成具有复杂特征的高质量合成基准，显著提升编译器优化模型性能，为自动化基准生成提供新方法。

authors_text: Maria Lymperaiou,Giorgos Filandrianos,Konstantinos Thomas,Giorgos Stamou
title: Counterfactual Edits for Generative Evaluation
title_zh: 生成评估的反事实编辑
abstract: Evaluation of generative models has been an underrepresented field despite the surge of generative architectures. Most recent models are evaluated upon rather obsolete metrics which suffer from robustness issues, while being unable to assess more aspects of visual quality, such as compositionality and logic of synthesis. At the same time, the explainability of generative models remains a limited, though important, research direction with several current attempts requiring access to the inner functionalities of generative models. Contrary to prior literature, we view generative models as a black box, and we propose a framework for the evaluation and explanation of synthesized results based on concepts instead of pixels. Our framework exploits knowledge-based counterfactual edits that underline which objects or attributes should be inserted, removed, or replaced from generated images to approach their ground truth conditioning. Moreover, global explanations produced by accumulating local edits can also reveal what concepts a model cannot generate in total. The application of our framework on various models designed for the challenging tasks of Story Visualization and Scene Synthesis verifies the power of our approach in the model-agnostic setting.
summary: Evaluation of generative models has been an underrepresented field despite the surge of generative architectures. Most recent models are evaluated upon rather obsolete metrics which suffer from robustness issues, while being unable to assess more aspects of visual quality, such as compositionality and logic of synthesis. At the same time, the explainability of generative models remains a limited, though important, research direction with several current attempts requiring access to the inner functionalities of generative models. Contrary to prior literature, we view generative models as a black box, and we propose a framework for the evaluation and explanation of synthesized results based on concepts instead of pixels. Our framework exploits knowledge-based counterfactual edits that underline which objects or attributes should be inserted, removed, or replaced from generated images to approach their ground truth conditioning. Moreover, global explanations produced by accumulating local edits can also reveal what concepts a model cannot generate in total. The application of our framework on various models designed for the challenging tasks of Story Visualization and Scene Synthesis verifies the power of our approach in the model-agnostic setting.
abstract_zh: 尽管生成架构激增，但生成模型的评估一直是一个代表性不足的领域。最近的模型是根据相当过时的指标进行评估的，这些指标存在鲁棒性问题，同时无法评估视觉质量的更多方面，例如组合性和合成逻辑。与此同时，生成模型的可解释性仍然是一个有限但重要的研究方向，当前的一些尝试需要访问生成模型的内部功能。与之前的文献相反，我们将生成模型视为黑匣子，并且我们提出了一个基于概念而不是像素来评估和解释合成结果的框架。我们的框架利用基于知识的反事实编辑，强调应从生成的图像中插入、删除或替换哪些对象或属性，以接近其地面实况条件。此外，通过累积局部编辑产生的全局解释还可以揭示模型无法总共生成哪些概念。我们的框架在专为故事可视化和场景合成的挑战性任务而设计的各种模型上的应用验证了我们的方法在与模型无关的环境中的威力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于反事实编辑的生成模型评估框架，通过概念而非像素评估生成图像的质量与逻辑性。
AI.motivation: 现有生成模型评估指标过时且不稳健，无法全面评估视觉质量（如组合性与逻辑性），且可解释性方法多依赖模型内部结构。
AI.method: 将生成模型视为黑箱，利用知识驱动的反事实编辑对生成图像进行对象/属性的增删改，通过局部编辑积累生成全局解释以揭示模型缺陷。
AI.result: 框架在故事可视化和场景合成任务中验证有效，证明其在模型无关场景下的评估与解释能力。
AI.conclusion: 该方法无需模型内部信息即可全面评估生成质量并揭示概念生成限制，适用于多种生成任务。

search_text: Counterfactual Edits for Generative Evaluation 生成评估的反事实编辑 Evaluation of generative models has been an underrepresented field despite the surge of generative architectures. Most recent models are evaluated upon rather obsolete metrics which suffer from robustness issues, while being unable to assess more aspects of visual quality, such as compositionality and logic of synthesis. At the same time, the explainability of generative models remains a limited, though important, research direction with several current attempts requiring access to the inner functionalities of generative models. Contrary to prior literature, we view generative models as a black box, and we propose a framework for the evaluation and explanation of synthesized results based on concepts instead of pixels. Our framework exploits knowledge-based counterfactual edits that underline which objects or attributes should be inserted, removed, or replaced from generated images to approach their ground truth conditioning. Moreover, global explanations produced by accumulating local edits can also reveal what concepts a model cannot generate in total. The application of our framework on various models designed for the challenging tasks of Story Visualization and Scene Synthesis verifies the power of our approach in the model-agnostic setting. 尽管生成架构激增，但生成模型的评估一直是一个代表性不足的领域。最近的模型是根据相当过时的指标进行评估的，这些指标存在鲁棒性问题，同时无法评估视觉质量的更多方面，例如组合性和合成逻辑。与此同时，生成模型的可解释性仍然是一个有限但重要的研究方向，当前的一些尝试需要访问生成模型的内部功能。与之前的文献相反，我们将生成模型视为黑匣子，并且我们提出了一个基于概念而不是像素来评估和解释合成结果的框架。我们的框架利用基于知识的反事实编辑，强调应从生成的图像中插入、删除或替换哪些对象或属性，以接近其地面实况条件。此外，通过累积局部编辑产生的全局解释还可以揭示模型无法总共生成哪些概念。我们的框架在专为故事可视化和场景合成的挑战性任务而设计的各种模型上的应用验证了我们的方法在与模型无关的环境中的威力。 本文提出一种基于反事实编辑的生成模型评估框架，通过概念而非像素评估生成图像的质量与逻辑性。 Maria Lymperaiou,Giorgos Filandrianos,Konstantinos Thomas,Giorgos Stamou cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Pengcheng Wu,Sonia Martinez,Jun Chen
title: Fine-Tuned Convex Approximations of Probabilistic Reachable Sets under Data-driven Uncertainties
title_zh: 数据驱动不确定性下概率可达集的微调凸逼近
abstract: This paper proposes a mechanism to fine-tune convex approximations of probabilistic reachable sets (PRS) of uncertain dynamic systems. We consider the case of unbounded uncertainties, for which it may be impossible to find a bounded reachable set of the system. Instead, we turn to find a PRS that bounds system states with high confidence. Our data-driven approach builds on a kernel density estimator (KDE) accelerated by a fast Fourier transform (FFT), which is customized to model the uncertainties and obtain the PRS efficiently. However, the non-convex shape of the PRS can make it impractical for subsequent optimal designs. Motivated by this, we formulate a mixed integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem whose solution result is an optimal $n$ sided convex polygon that approximates the PRS. Leveraging this formulation, we propose a heuristic algorithm to find this convex set efficiently while ensuring accuracy. The algorithm is tested on comprehensive case studies that demonstrate its near-optimality, accuracy, efficiency, and robustness. The benefits of this work pave the way for promising applications to safety-critical, real-time motion planning of uncertain dynamic systems.
summary: This paper proposes a mechanism to fine-tune convex approximations of probabilistic reachable sets (PRS) of uncertain dynamic systems. We consider the case of unbounded uncertainties, for which it may be impossible to find a bounded reachable set of the system. Instead, we turn to find a PRS that bounds system states with high confidence. Our data-driven approach builds on a kernel density estimator (KDE) accelerated by a fast Fourier transform (FFT), which is customized to model the uncertainties and obtain the PRS efficiently. However, the non-convex shape of the PRS can make it impractical for subsequent optimal designs. Motivated by this, we formulate a mixed integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem whose solution result is an optimal $n$ sided convex polygon that approximates the PRS. Leveraging this formulation, we propose a heuristic algorithm to find this convex set efficiently while ensuring accuracy. The algorithm is tested on comprehensive case studies that demonstrate its near-optimality, accuracy, efficiency, and robustness. The benefits of this work pave the way for promising applications to safety-critical, real-time motion planning of uncertain dynamic systems.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种微调不确定动态系统的概率可达集（PRS）凸近似的机制。我们考虑无限不确定性的情况，对于这种情况，可能不可能找到系统的有界可达集。相反，我们转而寻找一个以高置信度限制系统状态的 PRS。我们的数据驱动方法建立在通过快速傅立叶变换 (FFT) 加速的核密度估计器 (KDE) 的基础上，该估计器经过定制以对不确定性进行建模并有效地获得 PRS。然而，PRS 的非凸形状可能使其对于后续的优化设计来说不切实际。受此启发，我们制定了一个混合整数非线性规划（MINLP）问题，其解决方案结果是一个近似于 PRS 的最优 $n$ 边凸多边形。利用这个公式，我们提出了一种启发式算法来有效地找到这个凸集，同时确保准确性。该算法经过全面的案例研究测试，证明其接近最优、准确性、效率和鲁棒性。这项工作的好处为不确定动态系统的安全关键型实时运动规划的有前途的应用铺平了道路。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种机制，用于精细调整不确定动态系统的概率可达集（PRS）的凸近似。
AI.motivation: 由于非凸形状的PRS可能不适用于后续的最优设计，因此需要找到一种方法将其近似为凸多边形。
AI.method: 本文采用混合整数非线性规划（MINLP）问题，并提出了一种启发式算法来高效找到近似PRS的凸集。
AI.result: 该算法在多个案例研究中展示了其近似最优性、准确性、效率和鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 该工作为不确定动态系统的安全关键实时运动规划提供了有前景的应用前景。

authors_text: Sichao Li,Sean Banerjee,Natasha Kholgade Banerjee,Soumyabrata Dey
title: Simultaneous prediction of hand gestures, handedness, and hand keypoints using thermal images
title_zh: 使用热图像同时预测手势、惯用手和手部关键点
abstract: Hand gesture detection is a well-explored area in computer vision with applications in various forms of Human-Computer Interactions. In this work, we propose a technique for simultaneous hand gesture classification, handedness detection, and hand keypoints localization using thermal data captured by an infrared camera. Our method uses a novel deep multi-task learning architecture that includes shared encoderdecoder layers followed by three branches dedicated for each mentioned task. We performed extensive experimental validation of our model on an in-house dataset consisting of 24 users data. The results confirm higher than 98 percent accuracy for gesture classification, handedness detection, and fingertips localization, and more than 91 percent accuracy for wrist points localization.
summary: Hand gesture detection is a well-explored area in computer vision with applications in various forms of Human-Computer Interactions. In this work, we propose a technique for simultaneous hand gesture classification, handedness detection, and hand keypoints localization using thermal data captured by an infrared camera. Our method uses a novel deep multi-task learning architecture that includes shared encoderdecoder layers followed by three branches dedicated for each mentioned task. We performed extensive experimental validation of our model on an in-house dataset consisting of 24 users data. The results confirm higher than 98 percent accuracy for gesture classification, handedness detection, and fingertips localization, and more than 91 percent accuracy for wrist points localization.
abstract_zh: 手势检测是计算机视觉中一个被充分探索的领域，在各种形式的人机交互中都有应用。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种使用红外相机捕获的热数据同时进行手势分类、利手检测和手部关键点定位的技术。我们的方法使用一种新颖的深度多任务学习架构，其中包括共享编码器解码器层，后面是专门用于每个提到的任务的三个分支。我们在包含 24 个用户数据的内部数据集上对我们的模型进行了广泛的实验验证。结果证实，手势分类、惯用手检测和指尖定位的准确率高于 98%，手腕点定位的准确率高于 91%。

AI.tldr: 本论文提出了一种利用热成像数据同时预测手势、左右手识别及手部关键点定位的方法。
AI.motivation: 传统可见光摄像头易受光照影响，而红外热成像在复杂光照下更具鲁棒性，且多任务联合学习可提升模型效率。
AI.method: 设计多任务学习框架，包含共享的编码器-解码器层与三个独立任务分支，分别处理手势分类、左右手检测和关键点定位。
AI.result: 在24用户自制数据集上，手势分类和左右手检测准确率超98%，指尖定位超98%，腕部定位超91%。
AI.conclusion: 热成像多任务模型在手部行为分析中表现优异，为实际人机交互场景提供高鲁棒性解决方案。

The results confirm higher than 98 percent accuracy for gesture classification, handedness detection, and fingertips localization, and more than 91 percent accuracy for wrist points localization. 手势检测是计算机视觉中一个被充分探索的领域，在各种形式的人机交互中都有应用。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种使用红外相机捕获的热数据同时进行手势分类、利手检测和手部关键点定位的技术。我们的方法使用一种新颖的深度多任务学习架构，其中包括共享编码器解码器层，后面是专门用于每个提到的任务的三个分支。我们在包含 24 个用户数据的内部数据集上对我们的模型进行了广泛的实验验证。结果证实，手势分类、惯用手检测和指尖定位的准确率高于 98%，手腕点定位的准确率高于 91%。 本论文提出了一种利用热成像数据同时预测手势、左右手识别及手部关键点定位的方法。 Sichao Li,Sean Banerjee,Natasha Kholgade Banerjee,Soumyabrata Dey cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Abhinanda R. Punnakkal,Suyog S Jadhav,Alexander Horsch,Krishna Agarwal,Dilip K. Prasad
title: MiShape: 3D Shape Modelling of Mitochondria in Microscopy
title_zh: MiShape：显微镜下线粒体的 3D 形状建模
abstract: Fluorescence microscopy is a quintessential tool for observing cells and understanding the underlying mechanisms of life-sustaining processes of all living organisms. The problem of extracting 3D shape of mitochondria from fluorescence microscopy images remains unsolved due to the complex and varied shapes expressed by mitochondria and the poor resolving capacity of these microscopes. We propose an approach to bridge this gap by learning a shape prior for mitochondria termed as MiShape, by leveraging high-resolution electron microscopy data. MiShape is a generative model learned using implicit representations of mitochondrial shapes. It provides a shape distribution that can be used to generate infinite realistic mitochondrial shapes. We demonstrate the representation power of MiShape and its utility for 3D shape reconstruction given a single 2D fluorescence image or a small 3D stack of 2D slices. We also showcase applications of our method by deriving simulated fluorescence microscope datasets that have realistic 3D ground truths for the problem of 2D segmentation and microscope-to-microscope transformation.
summary: Fluorescence microscopy is a quintessential tool for observing cells and understanding the underlying mechanisms of life-sustaining processes of all living organisms. The problem of extracting 3D shape of mitochondria from fluorescence microscopy images remains unsolved due to the complex and varied shapes expressed by mitochondria and the poor resolving capacity of these microscopes. We propose an approach to bridge this gap by learning a shape prior for mitochondria termed as MiShape, by leveraging high-resolution electron microscopy data. MiShape is a generative model learned using implicit representations of mitochondrial shapes. It provides a shape distribution that can be used to generate infinite realistic mitochondrial shapes. We demonstrate the representation power of MiShape and its utility for 3D shape reconstruction given a single 2D fluorescence image or a small 3D stack of 2D slices. We also showcase applications of our method by deriving simulated fluorescence microscope datasets that have realistic 3D ground truths for the problem of 2D segmentation and microscope-to-microscope transformation.
abstract_zh: 荧光显微镜是观察细胞和了解所有生物体生命维持过程的基本机制的典型工具。由于线粒体表达的形状复杂多变以及这些显微镜的分辨能力较差，从荧光显微镜图像中提取线粒体3D形状的问题仍未解决。我们提出了一种通过利用高分辨率电子显微镜数据来学习线粒体先验形状（称为 MiShape）来弥补这一差距的方法。 MiShape 是一种使用线粒体形状的隐式表示来学习的生成模型。它提供了一种形状分布，可用于生成无限逼真的线粒体形状。我们展示了 MiShape 的表示能力及其在给定单个 2D 荧光图像或小型 3D 切片堆栈的 3D 形状重建方面的实用性。我们还通过派生模拟荧光显微镜数据集来展示我们的方法的应用，这些数据集具有针对 2D 分割和显微镜到显微镜转换问题的真实 3D 基本事实。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MiShape方法，利用高分辨率电子显微镜数据学习线粒体形状先验，解决荧光显微镜下线粒体3D形状重建难题。
AI.motivation: 荧光显微镜因分辨率限制难以准确提取线粒体复杂多变的3D形状，现有方法存在重建精度不足的问题。
AI.method: 基于隐式表示构建生成模型，通过电子显微镜数据学习线粒体形状分布，实现从单张2D图像或小规模3D图像栈的3D形状重建。
AI.result: 验证了MiShape在形状生成与重建中的表现，生成具有真实3D结构的模拟数据集，支持2D分割与显微镜间转换任务。
AI.conclusion: MiShape通过形状先验有效弥补了荧光显微镜的局限性，为线粒体3D建模及显微图像分析提供了新方法。

authors_text: Guangyao Shi,Pratap Tokekar
title: Decision-Oriented Learning with Differentiable Submodular Maximization for Vehicle Routing Problem
title_zh: 车辆路径问题的可微子模最大化决策导向学习
abstract: We study the problem of learning a function that maps context observations (input) to parameters of a submodular function (output). Our motivating case study is a specific type of vehicle routing problem, in which a team of Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs) can serve as mobile charging stations to recharge a team of Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UAVs) that execute persistent monitoring tasks. {We want to learn the mapping from observations of UAV task routes and wind field to the parameters of a submodular objective function, which describes the distribution of landing positions of the UAVs .} Traditionally, such a learning problem is solved independently as a prediction phase without considering the downstream task optimization phase. However, the loss function used in prediction may be misaligned with our final goal, i.e., a good routing decision. Good performance in the isolated prediction phase does not necessarily lead to good decisions in the downstream routing task. In this paper, we propose a framework that incorporates task optimization as a differentiable layer in the prediction phase. Our framework allows end-to-end training of the prediction model without using engineered intermediate loss that is targeted only at the prediction performance. In the proposed framework, task optimization (submodular maximization) is made differentiable by introducing stochastic perturbations into deterministic algorithms (i.e., stochastic smoothing). We demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed framework using synthetic data. Experimental results of the mobile charging station routing problem show that the proposed framework can result in better routing decisions, e.g. the average number of UAVs recharged increases, compared to the prediction-optimization separate approach.
summary: We study the problem of learning a function that maps context observations (input) to parameters of a submodular function (output). Our motivating case study is a specific type of vehicle routing problem, in which a team of Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs) can serve as mobile charging stations to recharge a team of Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UAVs) that execute persistent monitoring tasks. {We want to learn the mapping from observations of UAV task routes and wind field to the parameters of a submodular objective function, which describes the distribution of landing positions of the UAVs .} Traditionally, such a learning problem is solved independently as a prediction phase without considering the downstream task optimization phase. However, the loss function used in prediction may be misaligned with our final goal, i.e., a good routing decision. Good performance in the isolated prediction phase does not necessarily lead to good decisions in the downstream routing task. In this paper, we propose a framework that incorporates task optimization as a differentiable layer in the prediction phase. Our framework allows end-to-end training of the prediction model without using engineered intermediate loss that is targeted only at the prediction performance. In the proposed framework, task optimization (submodular maximization) is made differentiable by introducing stochastic perturbations into deterministic algorithms (i.e., stochastic smoothing). We demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed framework using synthetic data. Experimental results of the mobile charging station routing problem show that the proposed framework can result in better routing decisions, e.g. the average number of UAVs recharged increases, compared to the prediction-optimization separate approach.
abstract_zh: 我们研究学习将上下文观察（输入）映射到子模函数（输出）参数的函数的问题。我们的激励性案例研究是一种特定类型的车辆路径问题，其中一组无人驾驶地面车辆（UGV）可以充当移动充电站，为执行持续监控任务的一组无人驾驶地面车辆（UAV）充电。 {我们想要学习从无人机任务路线和风场的观测到子模目标函数参数的映射，该目标函数描述了无人机着陆位置的分布。}传统上，这样的学习问题是作为预测阶段独立解决的，而不考虑下游任务优化阶段。然而，预测中使用的损失函数可能与我们的最终目标（即良好的路由决策）不一致。孤立预测阶段的良好性能并不一定会导致下游路由任务中的良好决策。在本文中，我们提出了一个框架，将任务优化作为预测阶段的可微层。我们的框架允许对预测模型进行端到端训练，而无需使用仅针对预测性能的工程中间损失。在所提出的框架中，通过将随机扰动引入确定性算法（即随机平滑），使任务优化（子模最大化）变得可微。我们使用合成数据证明了所提出的框架的有效性。移动充电站路由问题的实验结果表明，所提出的框架可以产生更好的路由决策，例如与预测优化单独方法相比，无人机充电的平均数量有所增加。

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何学习一个将上下文观测映射到子模函数参数的函数，以解决车辆路径规划问题，特别是在无人机（UAV）任务中利用无人地面车辆（UGV）作为移动充电站的场景。
AI.motivation: 传统方法将预测阶段与优化阶段分离，导致预测损失函数与最终决策目标不一致，从而影响路径规划效果。
AI.method: 提出一种将任务优化作为可微分层的框架，在预测阶段直接整合子模最大化优化，通过随机扰动使确定性算法可微（随机平滑），实现端到端训练。
AI.result: 实验表明，该框架在移动充电站路径规划问题中优于传统分离方法，例如平均充电的无人机数量增加。
AI.conclusion: 将优化过程嵌入学习框架能提升决策性能，证明了端到端联合训练在复杂任务中的有效性。
