authors_text: SeongKu Kang,Wonbin Kweon,Dongha Lee,Jianxun Lian,Xing Xie,Hwanjo Yu
title: Distillation from Heterogeneous Models for Top-K Recommendation
title_zh: 从异构模型中蒸馏得到 Top-K 推荐
abstract: Recent recommender systems have shown remarkable performance by using an ensemble of heterogeneous models. However, it is exceedingly costly because it requires resources and inference latency proportional to the number of models, which remains the bottleneck for production. Our work aims to transfer the ensemble knowledge of heterogeneous teachers to a lightweight student model using knowledge distillation (KD), to reduce the huge inference costs while retaining high accuracy. Through an empirical study, we find that the efficacy of distillation severely drops when transferring knowledge from heterogeneous teachers. Nevertheless, we show that an important signal to ease the difficulty can be obtained from the teacher's training trajectory. This paper proposes a new KD framework, named HetComp, that guides the student model by transferring easy-to-hard sequences of knowledge generated from the teachers' trajectories. To provide guidance according to the student's learning state, HetComp uses dynamic knowledge construction to provide progressively difficult ranking knowledge and adaptive knowledge transfer to gradually transfer finer-grained ranking information. Our comprehensive experiments show that HetComp significantly improves the distillation quality and the generalization of the student model.
summary: Recent recommender systems have shown remarkable performance by using an ensemble of heterogeneous models. However, it is exceedingly costly because it requires resources and inference latency proportional to the number of models, which remains the bottleneck for production. Our work aims to transfer the ensemble knowledge of heterogeneous teachers to a lightweight student model using knowledge distillation (KD), to reduce the huge inference costs while retaining high accuracy. Through an empirical study, we find that the efficacy of distillation severely drops when transferring knowledge from heterogeneous teachers. Nevertheless, we show that an important signal to ease the difficulty can be obtained from the teacher's training trajectory. This paper proposes a new KD framework, named HetComp, that guides the student model by transferring easy-to-hard sequences of knowledge generated from the teachers' trajectories. To provide guidance according to the student's learning state, HetComp uses dynamic knowledge construction to provide progressively difficult ranking knowledge and adaptive knowledge transfer to gradually transfer finer-grained ranking information. Our comprehensive experiments show that HetComp significantly improves the distillation quality and the generalization of the student model.
abstract_zh: 最近的推荐系统通过使用异构模型集合表现出了卓越的性能。然而，它的成本非常高，因为它需要与模型数量成正比的资源和推理延迟，这仍然是生产的瓶颈。我们的工作旨在使用知识蒸馏（KD）将异构教师的集成知识转移到轻量级学生模型，以减少巨大的推理成本，同时保持高精度。通过实证研究，我们发现当从异质教师那里转移知识时，蒸馏的效率严重下降。尽管如此，我们表明可以从教师的培训轨迹中获得缓解困难的重要信号。本文提出了一种名为 HetComp 的新 KD 框架，该框架通过迁移从教师轨迹生成的由易到难的知识序列来指导学生模型。为了根据学生的学习状态提供指导，HetComp使用动态知识构建来提供逐步困难的排名知识和自适应知识迁移以逐步迁移更细粒度的排名信息。我们的综合实验表明，HetComp 显着提高了学生模型的蒸馏质量和泛化能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为HetComp的知识蒸馏框架，通过从异构教师模型的训练轨迹中提取知识，将多个推荐模型的集成知识转移到轻量级学生模型中，以降低推理成本并保持高精度。
AI.motivation: 异构模型集成在推荐系统中效果显著，但面临计算资源消耗大和推理延迟高的问题，传统知识蒸馏在异构教师间效果不佳，需寻找更有效的知识转移方法。
AI.method: HetComp框架通过分析教师模型的训练轨迹，生成由易到难的知识序列，结合动态知识构建和自适应知识转移机制，根据学生模型的学习状态逐步传递细粒度排序信息。
AI.result: 实验表明HetComp显著提升了蒸馏质量和学生模型的泛化能力，在多个数据集上优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: HetComp通过利用教师模型训练轨迹中的隐含信号，有效解决了异构模型知识蒸馏的难题，为高效推荐系统提供了可行方案。

search_text: Distillation from Heterogeneous Models for Top-K Recommendation 从异构模型中蒸馏得到 Top-K 推荐 Recent recommender systems have shown remarkable performance by using an ensemble of heterogeneous models. However, it is exceedingly costly because it requires resources and inference latency proportional to the number of models, which remains the bottleneck for production. Our work aims to transfer the ensemble knowledge of heterogeneous teachers to a lightweight student model using knowledge distillation (KD), to reduce the huge inference costs while retaining high accuracy. Through an empirical study, we find that the efficacy of distillation severely drops when transferring knowledge from heterogeneous teachers. Nevertheless, we show that an important signal to ease the difficulty can be obtained from the teacher's training trajectory. This paper proposes a new KD framework, named HetComp, that guides the student model by transferring easy-to-hard sequences of knowledge generated from the teachers' trajectories. To provide guidance according to the student's learning state, HetComp uses dynamic knowledge construction to provide progressively difficult ranking knowledge and adaptive knowledge transfer to gradually transfer finer-grained ranking information. Our comprehensive experiments show that HetComp significantly improves the distillation quality and the generalization of the student model. 最近的推荐系统通过使用异构模型集合表现出了卓越的性能。然而，它的成本非常高，因为它需要与模型数量成正比的资源和推理延迟，这仍然是生产的瓶颈。我们的工作旨在使用知识蒸馏（KD）将异构教师的集成知识转移到轻量级学生模型，以减少巨大的推理成本，同时保持高精度。通过实证研究，我们发现当从异质教师那里转移知识时，蒸馏的效率严重下降。尽管如此，我们表明可以从教师的培训轨迹中获得缓解困难的重要信号。本文提出了一种名为 HetComp 的新 KD 框架，该框架通过迁移从教师轨迹生成的由易到难的知识序列来指导学生模型。为了根据学生的学习状态提供指导，HetComp使用动态知识构建来提供逐步困难的排名知识和自适应知识迁移以逐步迁移更细粒度的排名信息。我们的综合实验表明，HetComp 显着提高了学生模型的蒸馏质量和泛化能力。 本文提出一种名为HetComp的知识蒸馏框架，通过从异构教师模型的训练轨迹中提取知识，将多个推荐模型的集成知识转移到轻量级学生模型中，以降低推理成本并保持高精度。 SeongKu Kang,Wonbin Kweon,Dongha Lee,Jianxun Lian,Xing Xie,Hwanjo Yu cs.IR,cs.AI

authors_text: Xuechen Liu,Md Sahidullah,Kong Aik Lee,Tomi Kinnunen
title: Speaker-Aware Anti-Spoofing
title_zh: 扬声器感知反欺骗
abstract: We address speaker-aware anti-spoofing, where prior knowledge of the target speaker is incorporated into a voice spoofing countermeasure (CM). In contrast to the frequently used speaker-independent solutions, we train the CM in a speaker-conditioned way. As a proof of concept, we consider speaker-aware extension to the state-of-the-art AASIST (audio anti-spoofing using integrated spectro-temporal graph attention networks) model. To this end, we consider two alternative strategies to incorporate target speaker information at the frame and utterance levels, respectively. The experimental results on a custom protocol based on ASVspoof 2019 dataset indicates the efficiency of the speaker information via enrollment: we obtain maximum relative improvements of 25.1% and 11.6% in equal error rate (EER) and minimum tandem detection cost function (t-DCF) over a speaker-independent baseline, respectively.
summary: We address speaker-aware anti-spoofing, where prior knowledge of the target speaker is incorporated into a voice spoofing countermeasure (CM). In contrast to the frequently used speaker-independent solutions, we train the CM in a speaker-conditioned way. As a proof of concept, we consider speaker-aware extension to the state-of-the-art AASIST (audio anti-spoofing using integrated spectro-temporal graph attention networks) model. To this end, we consider two alternative strategies to incorporate target speaker information at the frame and utterance levels, respectively. The experimental results on a custom protocol based on ASVspoof 2019 dataset indicates the efficiency of the speaker information via enrollment: we obtain maximum relative improvements of 25.1% and 11.6% in equal error rate (EER) and minimum tandem detection cost function (t-DCF) over a speaker-independent baseline, respectively.
abstract_zh: 我们解决说话者感知的反欺骗问题，其中目标说话者的先验知识被纳入语音欺骗对策（CM）中。与常用的与说话人无关的解决方案相比，我们以说话人调节的方式训练 CM。作为概念证明，我们考虑将说话者感知扩展到最先进的 AASIST（使用集成谱时图注意网络的音频反欺骗）模型。为此，我们考虑两种替代策略，分别在帧和话语级别合并目标说话者信息。基于 ASVspoof 2019 数据集的自定义协议的实验结果表明，通过注册获得说话人信息的效率：在与说话人无关的基线上，我们在等错误率 (EER) 和最小串联检测成本函数 (t-DCF) 方面分别获得了 25.1% 和 11.6% 的最大相对改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于说话人信息的反语音欺骗方法，通过整合目标说话人先验知识提升反欺骗性能。
AI.motivation: 传统反欺骗方法多采用说话人无关模型，未能充分利用目标说话人信息，限制了性能提升空间。
AI.method: 基于AASIST模型框架，提出两种策略在帧级和话语级整合说话人信息，通过条件化训练增强模型判别能力。
AI.result: 在ASVspoof 2019数据集上，EER降低25.1%，t-DCF降低11.6%，显著优于说话人无关基线模型。
AI.conclusion: 说话人信息有效提升反欺骗性能，验证了条件化建模的可行性，为后续研究提供新方向。

authors_text: Xuechen Liu,Md Sahidullah,Tomi Kinnunen
title: Distilling Multi-Level X-vector Knowledge for Small-footprint Speaker Verification
title_zh: 提取多级 X 向量知识以进行小型扬声器验证
comment: Submitted to Data & Knowledge Engineering at Dec. 2023. Copyright may be transferred without notice
comments: Submitted to Data & Knowledge Engineering at Dec. 2023. Copyright may be transferred without notice
abstract: Even though deep speaker models have demonstrated impressive accuracy in speaker verification tasks, this often comes at the expense of increased model size and computation time, presenting challenges for deployment in resource-constrained environments. Our research focuses on addressing this limitation through the development of small footprint deep speaker embedding extraction using knowledge distillation. While previous work in this domain has concentrated on speaker embedding extraction at the utterance level, our approach involves amalgamating embeddings from different levels of the x-vector model (teacher network) to train a compact student network. The results highlight the significance of frame-level information, with the student models exhibiting a remarkable size reduction of 85%-91% compared to their teacher counterparts, depending on the size of the teacher embeddings. Notably, by concatenating teacher embeddings, we achieve student networks that maintain comparable performance to the teacher while enjoying a substantial 75% reduction in model size. These findings and insights extend to other x-vector variants, underscoring the broad applicability of our approach.
summary: Even though deep speaker models have demonstrated impressive accuracy in speaker verification tasks, this often comes at the expense of increased model size and computation time, presenting challenges for deployment in resource-constrained environments. Our research focuses on addressing this limitation through the development of small footprint deep speaker embedding extraction using knowledge distillation. While previous work in this domain has concentrated on speaker embedding extraction at the utterance level, our approach involves amalgamating embeddings from different levels of the x-vector model (teacher network) to train a compact student network. The results highlight the significance of frame-level information, with the student models exhibiting a remarkable size reduction of 85%-91% compared to their teacher counterparts, depending on the size of the teacher embeddings. Notably, by concatenating teacher embeddings, we achieve student networks that maintain comparable performance to the teacher while enjoying a substantial 75% reduction in model size. These findings and insights extend to other x-vector variants, underscoring the broad applicability of our approach.
abstract_zh: 尽管深度说话人模型在说话人验证任务中表现出了令人印象深刻的准确性，但这通常是以模型大小和计算时间增加为代价的，这给资源受限环境中的部署带来了挑战。我们的研究重点是通过使用知识蒸馏开发小足迹深度说话人嵌入提取来解决这一限制。虽然该领域之前的工作主要集中在话语级别的说话者嵌入提取，但我们的方法涉及合并来自不同级别的 x 向量模型（教师网络）的嵌入来训练紧凑的学生网络。结果凸显了帧级信息的重要性，与教师模型相比，学生模型的尺寸显着减小了 85%-91%，具体取决于教师嵌入的大小。值得注意的是，通过连接教师嵌入，我们实现了学生网络保持与教师相当的性能，同时模型大小大幅减少了 75%。这些发现和见解扩展到其他 x 向量变体，强调了我们方法的广泛适用性。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过知识蒸馏技术，开发小规模的说话人验证模型，以解决深度模型在资源受限环境中的部署问题。
AI.motivation: 尽管深度说话人模型准确率高，但模型体积大、计算量大，难以在资源受限设备上部署，需寻找更高效的小模型方案。
AI.method: 通过整合X向量教师网络的多级嵌入（如帧级和话语级），采用知识蒸馏训练紧凑的学生网络，特别强调帧级信息的重要性。
AI.result: 学生模型体积较教师模型减少85%-91%，在保持相似性能的同时，通过嵌入连接进一步减少75%的模型体积。
AI.conclusion: 该方法不仅适用于X向量模型，还适用于其变体，展示了在资源受限场景下高效部署说话人验证模型的潜力。

authors_text: Ruslan Agishev,Tomáš Pětříček,Karel Zimmermann
title: Self-Supervised Depth Correction of Lidar Measurements from Map Consistency Loss
title_zh: 根据地图一致性损失对激光雷达测量进行自监督深度校正
abstract: Depth perception is considered an invaluable source of information in the context of 3D mapping and various robotics applications. However, point cloud maps acquired using consumer-level light detection and ranging sensors (lidars) still suffer from bias related to local surface properties such as measuring beam-to-surface incidence angle, distance, texture, reflectance, or illumination conditions. This fact has recently motivated researchers to exploit traditional filters, as well as the deep learning paradigm, in order to suppress the aforementioned depth sensors error while preserving geometric and map consistency details. Despite the effort, depth correction of lidar measurements is still an open challenge mainly due to the lack of clean 3D data that could be used as ground truth. In this paper, we introduce two novel point cloud map consistency losses, which facilitate self-supervised learning on real data of lidar depth correction models. Specifically, the models exploit multiple point cloud measurements of the same scene from different view-points in order to learn to reduce the bias based on the constructed map consistency signal. Complementary to the removal of the bias from the measurements, we demonstrate that the depth correction models help to reduce localization drift. Additionally, we release a data set that contains point cloud data captured in an indoor corridor environment with precise localization and ground truth mapping information.
summary: Depth perception is considered an invaluable source of information in the context of 3D mapping and various robotics applications. However, point cloud maps acquired using consumer-level light detection and ranging sensors (lidars) still suffer from bias related to local surface properties such as measuring beam-to-surface incidence angle, distance, texture, reflectance, or illumination conditions. This fact has recently motivated researchers to exploit traditional filters, as well as the deep learning paradigm, in order to suppress the aforementioned depth sensors error while preserving geometric and map consistency details. Despite the effort, depth correction of lidar measurements is still an open challenge mainly due to the lack of clean 3D data that could be used as ground truth. In this paper, we introduce two novel point cloud map consistency losses, which facilitate self-supervised learning on real data of lidar depth correction models. Specifically, the models exploit multiple point cloud measurements of the same scene from different view-points in order to learn to reduce the bias based on the constructed map consistency signal. Complementary to the removal of the bias from the measurements, we demonstrate that the depth correction models help to reduce localization drift. Additionally, we release a data set that contains point cloud data captured in an indoor corridor environment with precise localization and ground truth mapping information.
abstract_zh: 在 3D 绘图和各种机器人应用中，深度感知被认为是宝贵的信息来源。然而，使用消费级光检测和测距传感器（激光雷达）获取的点云图仍然存在与局部表面特性相关的偏差，例如测量光束到表面的入射角、距离、纹理、反射率或照明条件。这一事实最近促使研究人员利用传统过滤器以及深度学习范例，以抑制上述深度传感器错误，同时保留几何和地图一致性细节。尽管付出了努力，激光雷达测量的深度校正仍然是一个开放的挑战，主要是由于缺乏可用作地面实况的干净 3D 数据。在本文中，我们介绍了两种新颖的点云地图一致性损失，有助于对激光雷达深度校正模型的真实数据进行自监督学习。具体来说，这些模型利用从不同视点对同一场景进行的多个点云测量，以便根据构建的地图一致性信号学习减少偏差。作为消除测量偏差的补充，我们证明深度校正模型有助于减少定位漂移。此外，我们还发布了一个数据集，其中包含在室内走廊环境中捕获的点云数据，以及精确的定位和地面实况映射信息。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于地图一致性损失的自监督方法，用于校正消费级激光雷达点云深度测量中的偏差。
AI.motivation: 消费级激光雷达在复杂表面条件下存在深度偏差，且缺乏足够真实数据导致校正困难，现有方法难以兼顾精度与地图一致性。
AI.method: 设计两种新型地图一致性损失函数，利用多视角点云数据构建自监督信号，通过学习场景几何一致性实现深度校正，并减少定位漂移。
AI.result: 实验表明方法有效降低深度偏差并提升定位稳定性，同时发布包含精确地面真实数据的室内走廊点云数据集。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法在无标注数据条件下实现了有效的激光雷达深度校正，数据集为后续研究提供了重要基准。

search_text: Self-Supervised Depth Correction of Lidar Measurements from Map Consistency Loss 根据地图一致性损失对激光雷达测量进行自监督深度校正 Depth perception is considered an invaluable source of information in the context of 3D mapping and various robotics applications. However, point cloud maps acquired using consumer-level light detection and ranging sensors (lidars) still suffer from bias related to local surface properties such as measuring beam-to-surface incidence angle, distance, texture, reflectance, or illumination conditions. This fact has recently motivated researchers to exploit traditional filters, as well as the deep learning paradigm, in order to suppress the aforementioned depth sensors error while preserving geometric and map consistency details. Despite the effort, depth correction of lidar measurements is still an open challenge mainly due to the lack of clean 3D data that could be used as ground truth. In this paper, we introduce two novel point cloud map consistency losses, which facilitate self-supervised learning on real data of lidar depth correction models. Specifically, the models exploit multiple point cloud measurements of the same scene from different view-points in order to learn to reduce the bias based on the constructed map consistency signal. Complementary to the removal of the bias from the measurements, we demonstrate that the depth correction models help to reduce localization drift. Additionally, we release a data set that contains point cloud data captured in an indoor corridor environment with precise localization and ground truth mapping information. 在 3D 绘图和各种机器人应用中，深度感知被认为是宝贵的信息来源。然而，使用消费级光检测和测距传感器（激光雷达）获取的点云图仍然存在与局部表面特性相关的偏差，例如测量光束到表面的入射角、距离、纹理、反射率或照明条件。这一事实最近促使研究人员利用传统过滤器以及深度学习范例，以抑制上述深度传感器错误，同时保留几何和地图一致性细节。尽管付出了努力，激光雷达测量的深度校正仍然是一个开放的挑战，主要是由于缺乏可用作地面实况的干净 3D 数据。在本文中，我们介绍了两种新颖的点云地图一致性损失，有助于对激光雷达深度校正模型的真实数据进行自监督学习。具体来说，这些模型利用从不同视点对同一场景进行的多个点云测量，以便根据构建的地图一致性信号学习减少偏差。作为消除测量偏差的补充，我们证明深度校正模型有助于减少定位漂移。此外，我们还发布了一个数据集，其中包含在室内走廊环境中捕获的点云数据，以及精确的定位和地面实况映射信息。 本文提出基于地图一致性损失的自监督方法，用于校正消费级激光雷达点云深度测量中的偏差。 Ruslan Agishev,Tomáš Pětříček,Karel Zimmermann cs.RO

authors_text: Sora Takashima,Ryo Hayamizu,Nakamasa Inoue,Hirokatsu Kataoka,Rio Yokota
title: Visual Atoms: Pre-training Vision Transformers with Sinusoidal Waves
title_zh: Visual Atoms：使用正弦波预训练视觉变压器
abstract: Formula-driven supervised learning (FDSL) has been shown to be an effective method for pre-training vision transformers, where ExFractalDB-21k was shown to exceed the pre-training effect of ImageNet-21k. These studies also indicate that contours mattered more than textures when pre-training vision transformers. However, the lack of a systematic investigation as to why these contour-oriented synthetic datasets can achieve the same accuracy as real datasets leaves much room for skepticism. In the present work, we develop a novel methodology based on circular harmonics for systematically investigating the design space of contour-oriented synthetic datasets. This allows us to efficiently search the optimal range of FDSL parameters and maximize the variety of synthetic images in the dataset, which we found to be a critical factor. When the resulting new dataset VisualAtom-21k is used for pre-training ViT-Base, the top-1 accuracy reached 83.7% when fine-tuning on ImageNet-1k. This is close to the top-1 accuracy (84.2%) achieved by JFT-300M pre-training, while the number of images is 1/14. Unlike JFT-300M which is a static dataset, the quality of synthetic datasets will continue to improve, and the current work is a testament to this possibility. FDSL is also free of the common issues associated with real images, e.g. privacy/copyright issues, labeling costs/errors, and ethical biases.
summary: Formula-driven supervised learning (FDSL) has been shown to be an effective method for pre-training vision transformers, where ExFractalDB-21k was shown to exceed the pre-training effect of ImageNet-21k. These studies also indicate that contours mattered more than textures when pre-training vision transformers. However, the lack of a systematic investigation as to why these contour-oriented synthetic datasets can achieve the same accuracy as real datasets leaves much room for skepticism. In the present work, we develop a novel methodology based on circular harmonics for systematically investigating the design space of contour-oriented synthetic datasets. This allows us to efficiently search the optimal range of FDSL parameters and maximize the variety of synthetic images in the dataset, which we found to be a critical factor. When the resulting new dataset VisualAtom-21k is used for pre-training ViT-Base, the top-1 accuracy reached 83.7% when fine-tuning on ImageNet-1k. This is close to the top-1 accuracy (84.2%) achieved by JFT-300M pre-training, while the number of images is 1/14. Unlike JFT-300M which is a static dataset, the quality of synthetic datasets will continue to improve, and the current work is a testament to this possibility. FDSL is also free of the common issues associated with real images, e.g. privacy/copyright issues, labeling costs/errors, and ethical biases.
abstract_zh: 公式驱动的监督学习（FDSL）已被证明是预训练视觉 Transformer 的有效方法，其中 ExFractalDB-21k 被证明超过了 ImageNet-21k 的预训练效果。这些研究还表明，在预训练视觉变换器时，轮廓比纹理更重要。然而，对于为什么这些面向轮廓的合成数据集能够达到与真实数据集相同的精度，缺乏系统的调查，这留下了很大的怀疑空间。在目前的工作中，我们开发了一种基于圆谐波的新颖方法，用于系统地研究面向轮廓的合成数据集的设计空间。这使我们能够有效地搜索 FDSL 参数的最佳范围并最大化数据集中合成图像的多样性，我们发现这是一个关键因素。当生成的新数据集 VisualAtom-21k 用于预训练 ViT-Base 时，在 ImageNet-1k 上进行微调时，top-1 准确率达到 83.7%。这接近 JFT-300M 预训练达到的 top-1 精度（84.2%），而图像数量为 1/14。与静态数据集 JFT-300M 不同，合成数据集的质量将不断提高，当前的工作证明了这种可能性。 FDSL 也没有与真实图像相关的常见问题，例如隐私/版权问题、标签成本/错误和道德偏见。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于圆谐波的系统方法，设计轮廓导向的合成数据集VisualAtom-21k，用于高效预训练视觉Transformer，并验证其效果接近真实数据集。
AI.motivation: 现有研究虽证明轮廓导向合成数据集可媲美真实数据集，但缺乏系统性分析其有效性的原因，且合成数据集存在改进空间。
AI.method: 通过圆谐波系统探索轮廓导向合成数据集设计空间，优化FDSL参数范围，最大化合成图像多样性，生成VisualAtom-21k数据集。
AI.result: 使用VisualAtom-21k预训练ViT-Base模型在ImageNet-1k上达到83.7% top-1准确率，接近JFT-300M的84.2%，但图像数量仅为后者1/14。
AI.conclusion: 合成数据集可通过系统设计超越真实数据效果，且具有无隐私风险、低成本等优势，未来质量将持续提升。

authors_text: Kai Tzu-iunn Ong,Hana Kim,Minjin Kim,Jinseong Jang,Beomseok Sohn,Yoon Seong Choi,Dosik Hwang,Seong Jae Hwang,Jinyoung Yeo
title: Evidence-empowered Transfer Learning for Alzheimer's Disease
title_zh: 阿尔茨海默病的循证迁移学习
comment: Accepted to IEEE International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI) 2023. The authorship was changed from co-first authors to a single first author, which was authorized by the adviser/corresponding author Jinyoung Yeo (Apr 18th, 2023)
comments: Accepted to IEEE International Symposium on Biomedical Imaging (ISBI) 2023. The authorship was changed from co-first authors to a single first author, which was authorized by the adviser/corresponding author Jinyoung Yeo (Apr 18th, 2023)
abstract: Transfer learning has been widely utilized to mitigate the data scarcity problem in the field of Alzheimer's disease (AD). Conventional transfer learning relies on re-using models trained on AD-irrelevant tasks such as natural image classification. However, it often leads to negative transfer due to the discrepancy between the non-medical source and target medical domains. To address this, we present evidence-empowered transfer learning for AD diagnosis. Unlike conventional approaches, we leverage an AD-relevant auxiliary task, namely morphological change prediction, without requiring additional MRI data. In this auxiliary task, the diagnosis model learns the evidential and transferable knowledge from morphological features in MRI scans. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework is not only effective in improving detection performance regardless of model capacity, but also more data-efficient and faithful.
summary: Transfer learning has been widely utilized to mitigate the data scarcity problem in the field of Alzheimer's disease (AD). Conventional transfer learning relies on re-using models trained on AD-irrelevant tasks such as natural image classification. However, it often leads to negative transfer due to the discrepancy between the non-medical source and target medical domains. To address this, we present evidence-empowered transfer learning for AD diagnosis. Unlike conventional approaches, we leverage an AD-relevant auxiliary task, namely morphological change prediction, without requiring additional MRI data. In this auxiliary task, the diagnosis model learns the evidential and transferable knowledge from morphological features in MRI scans. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework is not only effective in improving detection performance regardless of model capacity, but also more data-efficient and faithful.
abstract_zh: 迁移学习已被广​​泛用于缓解阿尔茨海默病（AD）领域的数据稀缺问题。传统的迁移学习依赖于重复使用在与 AD 无关的任务（例如自然图像分类）上训练的模型。然而，由于非医学来源和目标医学领域之间的差异，往往会导致负转移。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了用于 AD 诊断的证据支持的迁移学习。与传统方法不同，我们利用 AD 相关的辅助任务，即形态变化预测，而不需要额外的 MRI 数据。在这个辅助任务中，诊断模型从 MRI 扫描的形态特征中学习证据和可转移的知识。实验结果表明，无论模型容量如何，我们的框架不仅可以有效提高检测性能，而且数据更加高效和忠实。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于证据的迁移学习方法用于阿尔茨海默病诊断，通过利用形态变化预测作为辅助任务，无需额外MRI数据即可提升模型性能。
AI.motivation: 传统迁移学习依赖非医学领域任务导致负迁移，而医学领域数据稀缺问题亟待解决。
AI.method: 设计形态变化预测作为AD相关辅助任务，从MRI形态特征中学习可迁移的证据知识，无需新增数据即可实现跨任务知识迁移。
AI.result: 实验表明框架在不同模型规模下均有效提升检测性能，且具有更高的数据效率和结果忠实度。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过医学相关辅助任务有效缓解负迁移问题，为AD诊断提供了更高效可靠的数据利用框架。

search_text: Evidence-empowered Transfer Learning for Alzheimer's Disease 阿尔茨海默病的循证迁移学习 Transfer learning has been widely utilized to mitigate the data scarcity problem in the field of Alzheimer's disease (AD). Conventional transfer learning relies on re-using models trained on AD-irrelevant tasks such as natural image classification. However, it often leads to negative transfer due to the discrepancy between the non-medical source and target medical domains. To address this, we present evidence-empowered transfer learning for AD diagnosis. Unlike conventional approaches, we leverage an AD-relevant auxiliary task, namely morphological change prediction, without requiring additional MRI data. In this auxiliary task, the diagnosis model learns the evidential and transferable knowledge from morphological features in MRI scans. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework is not only effective in improving detection performance regardless of model capacity, but also more data-efficient and faithful. 迁移学习已被广​​泛用于缓解阿尔茨海默病（AD）领域的数据稀缺问题。传统的迁移学习依赖于重复使用在与 AD 无关的任务（例如自然图像分类）上训练的模型。然而，由于非医学来源和目标医学领域之间的差异，往往会导致负转移。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了用于 AD 诊断的证据支持的迁移学习。与传统方法不同，我们利用 AD 相关的辅助任务，即形态变化预测，而不需要额外的 MRI 数据。在这个辅助任务中，诊断模型从 MRI 扫描的形态特征中学习证据和可转移的知识。实验结果表明，无论模型容量如何，我们的框架不仅可以有效提高检测性能，而且数据更加高效和忠实。 本文提出一种基于证据的迁移学习方法用于阿尔茨海默病诊断，通过利用形态变化预测作为辅助任务，无需额外MRI数据即可提升模型性能。 Kai Tzu-iunn Ong,Hana Kim,Minjin Kim,Jinseong Jang,Beomseok Sohn,Yoon Seong Choi,Dosik Hwang,Seong Jae Hwang,Jinyoung Yeo eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Adrian Galdran,Johan Verjans,Gustavo Carneiro,Miguel A. González Ballester
title: Multi-Head Multi-Loss Model Calibration
title_zh: 多头多损耗模型校准
abstract: Delivering meaningful uncertainty estimates is essential for a successful deployment of machine learning models in the clinical practice. A central aspect of uncertainty quantification is the ability of a model to return predictions that are well-aligned with the actual probability of the model being correct, also known as model calibration. Although many methods have been proposed to improve calibration, no technique can match the simple, but expensive approach of training an ensemble of deep neural networks. In this paper we introduce a form of simplified ensembling that bypasses the costly training and inference of deep ensembles, yet it keeps its calibration capabilities. The idea is to replace the common linear classifier at the end of a network by a set of heads that are supervised with different loss functions to enforce diversity on their predictions. Specifically, each head is trained to minimize a weighted Cross-Entropy loss, but the weights are different among the different branches. We show that the resulting averaged predictions can achieve excellent calibration without sacrificing accuracy in two challenging datasets for histopathological and endoscopic image classification. Our experiments indicate that Multi-Head Multi-Loss classifiers are inherently well-calibrated, outperforming other recent calibration techniques and even challenging Deep Ensembles' performance. Code to reproduce our experiments can be found at \url{https://github.com/agaldran/mhml_calibration} .
summary: Delivering meaningful uncertainty estimates is essential for a successful deployment of machine learning models in the clinical practice. A central aspect of uncertainty quantification is the ability of a model to return predictions that are well-aligned with the actual probability of the model being correct, also known as model calibration. Although many methods have been proposed to improve calibration, no technique can match the simple, but expensive approach of training an ensemble of deep neural networks. In this paper we introduce a form of simplified ensembling that bypasses the costly training and inference of deep ensembles, yet it keeps its calibration capabilities. The idea is to replace the common linear classifier at the end of a network by a set of heads that are supervised with different loss functions to enforce diversity on their predictions. Specifically, each head is trained to minimize a weighted Cross-Entropy loss, but the weights are different among the different branches. We show that the resulting averaged predictions can achieve excellent calibration without sacrificing accuracy in two challenging datasets for histopathological and endoscopic image classification. Our experiments indicate that Multi-Head Multi-Loss classifiers are inherently well-calibrated, outperforming other recent calibration techniques and even challenging Deep Ensembles' performance. Code to reproduce our experiments can be found at \url{https://github.com/agaldran/mhml_calibration} .
abstract_zh: 提供有意义的不确定性估计对于在临床实践中成功部署机器学习模型至关重要。不确定性量化的一个核心方面是模型返回与模型正确的实际概率完全一致的预测的能力，也称为模型校准。尽管已经提出了许多方法来改进校准，但没有任何技术可以与训练深度神经网络集合的简单但昂贵的方法相媲美。在本文中，我们介绍了一种简化的集成形式，它绕过了深度集成的昂贵训练和推理，但保留了其校准功能。这个想法是用一组头替换网络末端的常见线性分类器，这些头由不同的损失函数进行监督，以增强其预测的多样性。具体来说，每个头都经过训练以最小化加权交叉熵损失，但不同分支之间的权重不同。我们表明，所得的平均预测可以在组织病理学和内窥镜图像分类的两个具有挑战性的数据集中实现出色的校准，而不会牺牲准确性。我们的实验表明，多头多损失分类器本质上是经过良好校准的，优于其他最新的校准技术，甚至挑战了深度集成的性能。重现我们实验的代码可以在 \url{https://github.com/agaldran/mhml_calibration} 找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种多头多损失模型校准方法，通过在神经网络末端使用多个不同损失函数训练的分类头，以低成本替代深度集成方法，实现高精度且校准良好的预测。
AI.motivation: 临床实践中需要可靠的不确定性估计，但深度集成方法虽校准效果好却计算成本高。现有校准技术难以兼顾精度与效率，亟需一种低成本替代方案。
AI.method: 用多个分类头替代传统线性分类器，每个头采用不同权重的交叉熵损失函数进行训练，通过损失权重差异强制预测多样性，最终取平均预测结果。
AI.result: 在两个病理和内镜图像分类数据集上，该方法在保持精度的同时显著优于其他校准技术，且性能接近深度集成方法。
AI.conclusion: 多头多损失分类器具有内在校准优势，为临床部署提供了高精度、低成本的模型校准解决方案。

authors_text: Xuyang Zhao,Tianqi Du,Yisen Wang,Jun Yao,Weiran Huang
title: ArCL: Enhancing Contrastive Learning with Augmentation-Robust Representations
title_zh: ArCL：通过增强鲁棒表示增强对比学习
abstract: Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) is a paradigm that leverages unlabeled data for model training. Empirical studies show that SSL can achieve promising performance in distribution shift scenarios, where the downstream and training distributions differ. However, the theoretical understanding of its transferability remains limited. In this paper, we develop a theoretical framework to analyze the transferability of self-supervised contrastive learning, by investigating the impact of data augmentation on it. Our results reveal that the downstream performance of contrastive learning depends largely on the choice of data augmentation. Moreover, we show that contrastive learning fails to learn domain-invariant features, which limits its transferability. Based on these theoretical insights, we propose a novel method called Augmentation-robust Contrastive Learning (ArCL), which guarantees to learn domain-invariant features and can be easily integrated with existing contrastive learning algorithms. We conduct experiments on several datasets and show that ArCL significantly improves the transferability of contrastive learning.
summary: Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) is a paradigm that leverages unlabeled data for model training. Empirical studies show that SSL can achieve promising performance in distribution shift scenarios, where the downstream and training distributions differ. However, the theoretical understanding of its transferability remains limited. In this paper, we develop a theoretical framework to analyze the transferability of self-supervised contrastive learning, by investigating the impact of data augmentation on it. Our results reveal that the downstream performance of contrastive learning depends largely on the choice of data augmentation. Moreover, we show that contrastive learning fails to learn domain-invariant features, which limits its transferability. Based on these theoretical insights, we propose a novel method called Augmentation-robust Contrastive Learning (ArCL), which guarantees to learn domain-invariant features and can be easily integrated with existing contrastive learning algorithms. We conduct experiments on several datasets and show that ArCL significantly improves the transferability of contrastive learning.
abstract_zh: 自监督学习 (SSL) 是一种利用未标记数据进行模型训练的范例。实证研究表明，SSL 在下游分布和训练分布不同的分布转移场景中可以取得良好的性能。然而，对其可转移性的理论理解仍然有限。在本文中，我们通过研究数据增强对其的影响，开发了一个理论框架来分析自监督对比学习的可迁移性。我们的结果表明，对比学习的下游性能很大程度上取决于数据增强的选择。此外，我们表明对比学习无法学习领域不变的特征，这限制了其可迁移性。基于这些理论见解，我们提出了一种称为增强鲁棒对比学习（ArCL）的新方法，它保证学习领域不变的特征，并且可以轻松地与现有的对比学习算法集成。我们在多个数据集上进行了实验，结果表明 ArCL 显着提高了对比学习的可迁移性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为ArCL的新方法，通过增强对比学习中的数据增强鲁棒性来提升模型的迁移能力。
AI.motivation: 尽管自监督学习在分布偏移场景中表现良好，但其迁移能力的理论理解仍不充分，尤其数据增强对迁移性能的影响尚未明确。
AI.method: 构建理论框架分析数据增强对对比学习迁移性的影响，提出ArCL方法以学习领域不变特征，并可与现有对比学习算法无缝集成。
AI.result: 在多个数据集上的实验表明，ArCL显著提升了对比学习的迁移性能。
AI.conclusion: ArCL通过确保领域不变特征的学习，有效解决了对比学习在迁移任务中的局限性，为实际应用提供了可行方案。

search_text: ArCL: Enhancing Contrastive Learning with Augmentation-Robust Representations ArCL：通过增强鲁棒表示增强对比学习 Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) is a paradigm that leverages unlabeled data for model training. Empirical studies show that SSL can achieve promising performance in distribution shift scenarios, where the downstream and training distributions differ. However, the theoretical understanding of its transferability remains limited. In this paper, we develop a theoretical framework to analyze the transferability of self-supervised contrastive learning, by investigating the impact of data augmentation on it. Our results reveal that the downstream performance of contrastive learning depends largely on the choice of data augmentation. Moreover, we show that contrastive learning fails to learn domain-invariant features, which limits its transferability. Based on these theoretical insights, we propose a novel method called Augmentation-robust Contrastive Learning (ArCL), which guarantees to learn domain-invariant features and can be easily integrated with existing contrastive learning algorithms. We conduct experiments on several datasets and show that ArCL significantly improves the transferability of contrastive learning. 自监督学习 (SSL) 是一种利用未标记数据进行模型训练的范例。实证研究表明，SSL 在下游分布和训练分布不同的分布转移场景中可以取得良好的性能。然而，对其可转移性的理论理解仍然有限。在本文中，我们通过研究数据增强对其的影响，开发了一个理论框架来分析自监督对比学习的可迁移性。我们的结果表明，对比学习的下游性能很大程度上取决于数据增强的选择。此外，我们表明对比学习无法学习领域不变的特征，这限制了其可迁移性。基于这些理论见解，我们提出了一种称为增强鲁棒对比学习（ArCL）的新方法，它保证学习领域不变的特征，并且可以轻松地与现有的对比学习算法集成。我们在多个数据集上进行了实验，结果表明 ArCL 显着提高了对比学习的可迁移性。 本文提出了一种名为ArCL的新方法，通过增强对比学习中的数据增强鲁棒性来提升模型的迁移能力。 Xuyang Zhao,Tianqi Du,Yisen Wang,Jun Yao,Weiran Huang cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Gaochao Song,Luo Zhang,Ran Su,Jianfeng Shi,Ying He,Qian Sun
title: OPE-SR: Orthogonal Position Encoding for Designing a Parameter-free Upsampling Module in Arbitrary-scale Image Super-Resolution
title_zh: OPE-SR：用于设计任意尺度图像超分辨率中无参数上采样模块的正交位置编码
abstract: Implicit neural representation (INR) is a popular approach for arbitrary-scale image super-resolution (SR), as a key component of INR, position encoding improves its representation ability. Motivated by position encoding, we propose orthogonal position encoding (OPE) - an extension of position encoding - and an OPE-Upscale module to replace the INR-based upsampling module for arbitrary-scale image super-resolution. Same as INR, our OPE-Upscale Module takes 2D coordinates and latent code as inputs; however it does not require training parameters. This parameter-free feature allows the OPE-Upscale Module to directly perform linear combination operations to reconstruct an image in a continuous manner, achieving an arbitrary-scale image reconstruction. As a concise SR framework, our method has high computing efficiency and consumes less memory comparing to the state-of-the-art (SOTA), which has been confirmed by extensive experiments and evaluations. In addition, our method has comparable results with SOTA in arbitrary scale image super-resolution. Last but not the least, we show that OPE corresponds to a set of orthogonal basis, justifying our design principle.
summary: Implicit neural representation (INR) is a popular approach for arbitrary-scale image super-resolution (SR), as a key component of INR, position encoding improves its representation ability. Motivated by position encoding, we propose orthogonal position encoding (OPE) - an extension of position encoding - and an OPE-Upscale module to replace the INR-based upsampling module for arbitrary-scale image super-resolution. Same as INR, our OPE-Upscale Module takes 2D coordinates and latent code as inputs; however it does not require training parameters. This parameter-free feature allows the OPE-Upscale Module to directly perform linear combination operations to reconstruct an image in a continuous manner, achieving an arbitrary-scale image reconstruction. As a concise SR framework, our method has high computing efficiency and consumes less memory comparing to the state-of-the-art (SOTA), which has been confirmed by extensive experiments and evaluations. In addition, our method has comparable results with SOTA in arbitrary scale image super-resolution. Last but not the least, we show that OPE corresponds to a set of orthogonal basis, justifying our design principle.
abstract_zh: 隐式神经表示（INR）是任意尺度图像超分辨率（SR）的流行方法，作为INR的关键组成部分，位置编码提高了其表示能力。受位置编码的启发，我们提出了正交位置编码（OPE）——位置编码的扩展——以及 OPE-Upscale 模块来取代基于 INR 的上采样模块，以实现任意尺度的图像超分辨率。与 INR 相同，我们的 OPE-Upscale 模块采用 2D 坐标和潜在代码作为输入；然而它不需要训练参数。这种无参数的特性使得OPE-Upscale模块可以直接进行线性组合运算以连续的方式重建图像，实现任意尺度的图像重建。作为一个简洁的SR框架，与最先进的（SOTA）相比，我们的方法具有高计算效率并且消耗更少的内存，这已经通过大量的实验和评估得到证实。此外，我们的方法在任意尺度图像超分辨率方面具有与 SOTA 相当的结果。最后但并非最不重要的一点是，我们证明 OPE 对应于一组正交基，证明了我们的设计原则。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于正交位置编码的参数-free上采样模块OPE-Upscale，用于任意尺度图像超分辨率任务。
AI.motivation: 隐式神经表示（INR）虽适用于任意尺度超分，但其上采样模块依赖训练参数导致效率低下，需设计无需参数的替代方案。
AI.method: 提出正交位置编码（OPE）作为位置编码的扩展，并构建OPE-Upscale模块，通过2D坐标与潜在代码的线性组合直接重建图像，无需训练参数。
AI.result: 方法在计算效率和内存消耗上优于现有技术，超分效果与SOTA相当，且理论证明OPE对应正交基函数集。
AI.conclusion: OPE-SR框架通过参数-free设计实现了高效任意尺度超分，理论分析与实验结果均验证了方法的有效性与创新性。

authors_text: Chunfeng Wang,Peisong Huang,Yuxiang Zou,Haoyu Zhang,Shichao Liu,Xiang Yin,Zejun Ma
title: LiteG2P: A fast, light and high accuracy model for grapheme-to-phoneme conversion
title_zh: LiteG2P：一种快速、轻便且高精度的字素到音素转换模型
abstract: As a key component of automated speech recognition (ASR) and the front-end in text-to-speech (TTS), grapheme-to-phoneme (G2P) plays the role of converting letters to their corresponding pronunciations. Existing methods are either slow or poor in performance, and are limited in application scenarios, particularly in the process of on-device inference. In this paper, we integrate the advantages of both expert knowledge and connectionist temporal classification (CTC) based neural network and propose a novel method named LiteG2P which is fast, light and theoretically parallel. With the carefully leading design, LiteG2P can be applied both on cloud and on device. Experimental results on the CMU dataset show that the performance of the proposed method is superior to the state-of-the-art CTC based method with 10 times fewer parameters, and even comparable to the state-of-the-art Transformer-based sequence-to-sequence model with less parameters and 33 times less computation.
summary: As a key component of automated speech recognition (ASR) and the front-end in text-to-speech (TTS), grapheme-to-phoneme (G2P) plays the role of converting letters to their corresponding pronunciations. Existing methods are either slow or poor in performance, and are limited in application scenarios, particularly in the process of on-device inference. In this paper, we integrate the advantages of both expert knowledge and connectionist temporal classification (CTC) based neural network and propose a novel method named LiteG2P which is fast, light and theoretically parallel. With the carefully leading design, LiteG2P can be applied both on cloud and on device. Experimental results on the CMU dataset show that the performance of the proposed method is superior to the state-of-the-art CTC based method with 10 times fewer parameters, and even comparable to the state-of-the-art Transformer-based sequence-to-sequence model with less parameters and 33 times less computation.
abstract_zh: 作为自动语音识别 (ASR) 和文本转语音 (TTS) 前端的关键组件，字素转音素 (G2P) 起着将字母转换为相应发音的作用。现有方法要么速度慢，要么性能差，并且在应用场景上受到限制，特别是在设备端推理过程中。在本文中，我们综合了专家知识和基于连接主义时间分类（CTC）的神经网络的优点，提出了一种快速、轻量且理论上并行的新方法，称为LiteG2P。凭借精心领先的设计，LiteG2P既可以应用于云端，也可以应用于设备端。 CMU数据集上的实验结果表明，该方法的性能优于最先进的基于CTC的方法，参数减少了10倍，甚至可以与最先进的基于Transformer的序列到序列模型相媲美，参数更少，计算量减少了33倍。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为LiteG2P的高效图素到音素转换模型，兼具速度、轻量与高准确性。
AI.motivation: 现有G2P方法存在速度慢、性能差或设备端应用受限等问题，亟需更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 融合专家知识与CTC神经网络，设计可并行计算的LiteG2P架构，优化参数与计算量。
AI.result: 在CMU数据集上，LiteG2P参数量仅为最佳CTC方法的1/10，且计算量比Transformer模型低33倍，性能相当。
AI.conclusion: LiteG2P在保持高准确性的同时显著降低计算成本，适用于云端与设备端多样化场景。

authors_text: Jiang Xie,Shuyin Xia,Guoyin Wang,Xinbo Gao
title: GBMST: An Efficient Minimum Spanning Tree Clustering Based on Granular-Ball Computing
title_zh: GBMST：一种基于粒球计算的高效最小生成树聚类
abstract: Most of the existing clustering methods are based on a single granularity of information, such as the distance and density of each data. This most fine-grained based approach is usually inefficient and susceptible to noise. Therefore, we propose a clustering algorithm that combines multi-granularity Granular-Ball and minimum spanning tree (MST). We construct coarsegrained granular-balls, and then use granular-balls and MST to implement the clustering method based on "large-scale priority", which can greatly avoid the influence of outliers and accelerate the construction process of MST. Experimental results on several data sets demonstrate the power of the algorithm. All codes have been released at https://github.com/xjnine/GBMST.
summary: Most of the existing clustering methods are based on a single granularity of information, such as the distance and density of each data. This most fine-grained based approach is usually inefficient and susceptible to noise. Therefore, we propose a clustering algorithm that combines multi-granularity Granular-Ball and minimum spanning tree (MST). We construct coarsegrained granular-balls, and then use granular-balls and MST to implement the clustering method based on "large-scale priority", which can greatly avoid the influence of outliers and accelerate the construction process of MST. Experimental results on several data sets demonstrate the power of the algorithm. All codes have been released at https://github.com/xjnine/GBMST.
abstract_zh: 现有的聚类方法大多基于单一粒度的信息，例如每个数据的距离和密度。这种基于最细粒度的方法通常效率低下并且容易受到噪声的影响。因此，我们提出了一种结合多粒度 Granular-Ball 和最小生成树（MST）的聚类算法。我们构造粗粒度的颗粒球，然后利用颗粒球和MST来实现基于“大规模优先”的聚类方法，可以大大避免异常值的影响，加快MST的构建过程。多个数据集上的实验结果证明了该算法的强大功能。所有代码已发布于https://github.com/xjnine/GBMST。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于粒度球计算的高效最小生成树聚类算法GBMST，通过结合多粒度粒度球与最小生成树方法实现数据聚类。
AI.motivation: 现有聚类方法多基于单一粒度信息（如距离和密度），存在效率低且易受噪声干扰的问题，需通过多粒度信息融合提升鲁棒性与效率。
AI.method: 构建粗粒度粒度球，结合粒度球与最小生成树（MST），采用『大尺度优先』策略进行聚类，减少噪声影响并加速MST构建过程。
AI.result: 在多个数据集上的实验验证了算法有效性，代码已开源至GitHub。
AI.conclusion: GBMST通过多粒度粒度球与MST的结合，在聚类效率与抗噪声能力上优于传统方法，实验结果证实了其有效性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于粒度球计算的高效最小生成树聚类算法GBMST，通过结合多粒度粒度球与最小生成树方法实现数据聚类。
ai_content.motivation: 现有聚类方法多基于单一粒度信息（如距离和密度），存在效率低且易受噪声干扰的问题，需通过多粒度信息融合提升鲁棒性与效率。
ai_content.method: 构建粗粒度粒度球，结合粒度球与最小生成树（MST），采用『大尺度优先』策略进行聚类，减少噪声影响并加速MST构建过程。
ai_content.result: 在多个数据集上的实验验证了算法有效性，代码已开源至GitHub。
ai_content.conclusion: GBMST通过多粒度粒度球与MST的结合，在聚类效率与抗噪声能力上优于传统方法，实验结果证实了其有效性。

search_text: GBMST: An Efficient Minimum Spanning Tree Clustering Based on Granular-Ball Computing GBMST：一种基于粒球计算的高效最小生成树聚类 Most of the existing clustering methods are based on a single granularity of information, such as the distance and density of each data. This most fine-grained based approach is usually inefficient and susceptible to noise. Therefore, we propose a clustering algorithm that combines multi-granularity Granular-Ball and minimum spanning tree (MST). We construct coarsegrained granular-balls, and then use granular-balls and MST to implement the clustering method based on "large-scale priority", which can greatly avoid the influence of outliers and accelerate the construction process of MST. Experimental results on several data sets demonstrate the power of the algorithm. All codes have been released at https://github.com/xjnine/GBMST. 现有的聚类方法大多基于单一粒度的信息，例如每个数据的距离和密度。这种基于最细粒度的方法通常效率低下并且容易受到噪声的影响。因此，我们提出了一种结合多粒度 Granular-Ball 和最小生成树（MST）的聚类算法。我们构造粗粒度的颗粒球，然后利用颗粒球和MST来实现基于“大规模优先”的聚类方法，可以大大避免异常值的影响，加快MST的构建过程。多个数据集上的实验结果证明了该算法的强大功能。所有代码已发布于https://github.com/xjnine/GBMST。 本文提出了一种基于粒度球计算的高效最小生成树聚类算法GBMST，通过结合多粒度粒度球与最小生成树方法实现数据聚类。 Jiang Xie,Shuyin Xia,Guoyin Wang,Xinbo Gao cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.DB

authors_text: Mingxu Tao,Yansong Feng,Dongyan Zhao
title: Can BERT Refrain from Forgetting on Sequential Tasks? A Probing Study
title_zh: BERT 能否避免忘记顺序任务？探索性研究
abstract: Large pre-trained language models help to achieve state of the art on a variety of natural language processing (NLP) tasks, nevertheless, they still suffer from forgetting when incrementally learning a sequence of tasks. To alleviate this problem, recent works enhance existing models by sparse experience replay and local adaption, which yield satisfactory performance. However, in this paper we find that pre-trained language models like BERT have a potential ability to learn sequentially, even without any sparse memory replay. To verify the ability of BERT to maintain old knowledge, we adopt and re-finetune single-layer probe networks with the parameters of BERT fixed. We investigate the models on two types of NLP tasks, text classification and extractive question answering. Our experiments reveal that BERT can actually generate high quality representations for previously learned tasks in a long term, under extremely sparse replay or even no replay. We further introduce a series of novel methods to interpret the mechanism of forgetting and how memory rehearsal plays a significant role in task incremental learning, which bridges the gap between our new discovery and previous studies about catastrophic forgetting.
summary: Large pre-trained language models help to achieve state of the art on a variety of natural language processing (NLP) tasks, nevertheless, they still suffer from forgetting when incrementally learning a sequence of tasks. To alleviate this problem, recent works enhance existing models by sparse experience replay and local adaption, which yield satisfactory performance. However, in this paper we find that pre-trained language models like BERT have a potential ability to learn sequentially, even without any sparse memory replay. To verify the ability of BERT to maintain old knowledge, we adopt and re-finetune single-layer probe networks with the parameters of BERT fixed. We investigate the models on two types of NLP tasks, text classification and extractive question answering. Our experiments reveal that BERT can actually generate high quality representations for previously learned tasks in a long term, under extremely sparse replay or even no replay. We further introduce a series of novel methods to interpret the mechanism of forgetting and how memory rehearsal plays a significant role in task incremental learning, which bridges the gap between our new discovery and previous studies about catastrophic forgetting.
abstract_zh: 大型预训练语言模型有助于在各种自然语言处理（NLP）任务上实现最先进的水平，然而，它们在增量学习一系列任务时仍然会出现遗忘问题。为了缓解这个问题，最近的工作通过稀疏经验回放和局部适应来增强现有模型，从而产生了令人满意的性能。然而，在本文中，我们发现像 BERT 这样的预训练语言模型即使没有任何稀疏记忆重放，也具有顺序学习的潜在能力。为了验证 BERT 维护旧知识的能力，我们采用固定 BERT 参数的单层探测网络并重新微调。我们研究了两种类型的 NLP 任务的模型：文本分类和抽取式问答。我们的实验表明，在极其稀疏的重放甚至没有重放的情况下，BERT 实际上可以长期为先前学习的任务生成高质量的表示。我们进一步介绍了一系列新颖的方法来解释遗忘的机制以及记忆演练如何在任务增量学习中发挥重要作用，这弥补了我们的新发现与之前关于灾难性遗忘的研究之间的差距。

AI.tldr: 本文研究BERT等预训练语言模型在顺序任务中是否能避免遗忘，并验证其无需稀疏回放即可保持旧知识的能力。
AI.motivation: 尽管现有方法通过稀疏回放缓解模型遗忘问题，但作者发现BERT可能具备潜在的顺序学习能力，无需依赖回放即可维持旧知识，需进一步验证并探索遗忘机制。
AI.method: 固定BERT参数，重新微调单层探测网络，在文本分类和问答任务中测试其长期表现；引入新方法分析遗忘机制及记忆演练的作用。
AI.result: BERT在极少回放或无回放情况下仍能生成高质量的长期任务表示，且新方法揭示了记忆演练在增量学习中的关键作用。
AI.conclusion: BERT具有顺序学习潜力，无需回放即可保持知识；新方法为理解遗忘机制与增量学习提供了理论支持，为未来模型设计指明方向。

To verify the ability of BERT to maintain old knowledge, we adopt and re-finetune single-layer probe networks with the parameters of BERT fixed. We investigate the models on two types of NLP tasks, text classification and extractive question answering. Our experiments reveal that BERT can actually generate high quality representations for previously learned tasks in a long term, under extremely sparse replay or even no replay. authors_text: Xiaoguang Chang,Teng Wang,Shaowei Cai,Changyin Sun
title: LANDMARK: Language-guided Representation Enhancement Framework for Scene Graph Generation
title_zh: LANDMARK：用于场景图生成的语言引导表示增强框架
abstract: Scene graph generation (SGG) is a sophisticated task that suffers from both complex visual features and dataset long-tail problem. Recently, various unbiased strategies have been proposed by designing novel loss functions and data balancing strategies. Unfortunately, these unbiased methods fail to emphasize language priors in feature refinement perspective. Inspired by the fact that predicates are highly correlated with semantics hidden in subject-object pair and global context, we propose LANDMARK (LANguage-guiDed representationenhanceMent frAmewoRK) that learns predicate-relevant representations from language-vision interactive patterns, global language context and pair-predicate correlation. Specifically, we first project object labels to three distinctive semantic embeddings for different representation learning. Then, Language Attention Module (LAM) and Experience Estimation Module (EEM) process subject-object word embeddings to attention vector and predicate distribution, respectively. Language Context Module (LCM) encodes global context from each word embed-ding, which avoids isolated learning from local information. Finally, modules outputs are used to update visual representations and SGG model's prediction. All language representations are purely generated from object categories so that no extra knowledge is needed. This framework is model-agnostic and consistently improves performance on existing SGG models. Besides, representation-level unbiased strategies endow LANDMARK the advantage of compatibility with other methods. Code is available at https://github.com/rafa-cxg/PySGG-cxg.
summary: Scene graph generation (SGG) is a sophisticated task that suffers from both complex visual features and dataset long-tail problem. Recently, various unbiased strategies have been proposed by designing novel loss functions and data balancing strategies. Unfortunately, these unbiased methods fail to emphasize language priors in feature refinement perspective. Inspired by the fact that predicates are highly correlated with semantics hidden in subject-object pair and global context, we propose LANDMARK (LANguage-guiDed representationenhanceMent frAmewoRK) that learns predicate-relevant representations from language-vision interactive patterns, global language context and pair-predicate correlation. Specifically, we first project object labels to three distinctive semantic embeddings for different representation learning. Then, Language Attention Module (LAM) and Experience Estimation Module (EEM) process subject-object word embeddings to attention vector and predicate distribution, respectively. Language Context Module (LCM) encodes global context from each word embed-ding, which avoids isolated learning from local information. Finally, modules outputs are used to update visual representations and SGG model's prediction. All language representations are purely generated from object categories so that no extra knowledge is needed. This framework is model-agnostic and consistently improves performance on existing SGG models. Besides, representation-level unbiased strategies endow LANDMARK the advantage of compatibility with other methods. Code is available at https://github.com/rafa-cxg/PySGG-cxg.
abstract_zh: 场景图生成（SGG）是一项复杂的任务，面临复杂的视觉特征和数据集长尾问题。最近，通过设计新颖的损失函数和数据平衡策略，提出了各种无偏策略。不幸的是，这些无偏见的方法未能从特征细化的角度强调语言先验。受谓词与隐藏在主客对和全局上下文中的语义高度相关这一事实的启发，我们提出了LANDMARK（语言引导表示增强框架），它从语言视觉交互模式、全局语言上下文和对谓词相关性中学习谓词相关的表示。具体来说，我们首先将对象标签投影到三个不同的语义嵌入，以进行不同的表示学习。然后，语言注意模块（LAM）和体验估计模块（EEM）分别将主宾词嵌入处理为注意向量和谓词分布。语言上下文模块（LCM）对每个单词嵌入的全局上下文进行编码，这避免了从本地信息中进行孤立学习。最后，模块输出用于更新视觉表示和 SGG 模型的预测。所有语言表示纯粹是从对象类别生成的，因此不需要额外的知识。该框架与模型无关，并且持续改进现有 SGG 模型的性能。此外，表示级无偏策略赋予LANDMARK与其他方法兼容的优势。代码可在 https://github.com/rafa-cxg/PySGG-cxg 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出LANDMARK框架，通过语言引导的表示增强方法改进场景图生成任务。
AI.motivation: 现有无偏方法未能有效利用语言先验进行特征优化，而谓词与主谓对象对及全局语境的语义关联未被充分挖掘。
AI.method: 构建三类语义嵌入，通过语言注意力模块(LAM)、经验估计模块(EEM)和语言上下文模块(LCM)处理词嵌入，生成注意力向量和谓词分布，并更新视觉表示。
AI.result: 框架在多种SGG模型上持续提升性能，且无需额外知识，具备模型无关性和方法兼容性。
AI.conclusion: LANDMARK通过语言语境与视觉特征的深度融合，为场景图生成提供了灵活高效的解决方案。

Finally, modules outputs are used to update visual representations and SGG model's prediction. All language representations are purely generated from object categories so that no extra knowledge is needed. This framework is model-agnostic and consistently improves performance on existing SGG models. Besides, representation-level unbiased strategies endow LANDMARK the advantage of compatibility with other methods. Code is available at https://github.com/rafa-cxg/PySGG-cxg. 场景图生成（SGG）是一项复杂的任务，面临复杂的视觉特征和数据集长尾问题。最近，通过设计新颖的损失函数和数据平衡策略，提出了各种无偏策略。不幸的是，这些无偏见的方法未能从特征细化的角度强调语言先验。受谓词与隐藏在主客对和全局上下文中的语义高度相关这一事实的启发，我们提出了LANDMARK（语言引导表示增强框架），它从语言视觉交互模式、全局语言上下文和对谓词相关性中学习谓词相关的表示。具体来说，我们首先将对象标签投影到三个不同的语义嵌入，以进行不同的表示学习。然后，语言注意模块（LAM）和体验估计模块（EEM）分别将主宾词嵌入处理为注意向量和谓词分布。语言上下文模块（LCM）对每个单词嵌入的全局上下文进行编码，这避免了从本地信息中进行孤立学习。最后，模块输出用于更新视觉表示和 SGG 模型的预测。所有语言表示纯粹是从对象类别生成的，因此不需要额外的知识。该框架与模型无关，并且持续改进现有 SGG 模型的性能。此外，表示级无偏策略赋予LANDMARK与其他方法兼容的优势。代码可在 https://github.com/rafa-cxg/PySGG-cxg 获取。 本文提出LANDMARK框架，通过语言引导的表示增强方法改进场景图生成任务。 Xiaoguang Chang,Teng Wang,Shaowei Cai,Changyin Sun cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.CL

authors_text: Ben Berger,Tomer Ezra,Michal Feldman,Federico Fusco
title: Pandora's Problem with Combinatorial Cost
title_zh: 潘多拉的组合成本问题
comment: ef:EC '23: Proceedings of the 24th ACM Conference on Economics and Computation, 2023
comments: ef:EC '23: Proceedings of the 24th ACM Conference on Economics and Computation, 2023
abstract: Pandora's problem is a fundamental model in economics that studies optimal search strategies under costly inspection. In this paper we initiate the study of Pandora's problem with combinatorial costs, capturing many real-life scenarios where search cost is non-additive. Weitzman's celebrated algorithm [1979] establishes the remarkable result that, for additive costs, the optimal search strategy is non-adaptive and computationally feasible. We inquire to which extent this structural and computational simplicity extends beyond additive cost functions. Our main result is that the class of submodular cost functions admits an optimal strategy that follows a fixed, non-adaptive order, thus preserving the structural simplicity of additive cost functions. In contrast, for the more general class of subadditive (or even XOS) cost functions, the optimal strategy may already need to determine the search order adaptively. On the computational side, obtaining any approximation to the optimal utility requires super polynomially many queries to the cost function, even for a strict subclass of submodular cost functions.
summary: Pandora's problem is a fundamental model in economics that studies optimal search strategies under costly inspection. In this paper we initiate the study of Pandora's problem with combinatorial costs, capturing many real-life scenarios where search cost is non-additive. Weitzman's celebrated algorithm [1979] establishes the remarkable result that, for additive costs, the optimal search strategy is non-adaptive and computationally feasible. We inquire to which extent this structural and computational simplicity extends beyond additive cost functions. Our main result is that the class of submodular cost functions admits an optimal strategy that follows a fixed, non-adaptive order, thus preserving the structural simplicity of additive cost functions. In contrast, for the more general class of subadditive (or even XOS) cost functions, the optimal strategy may already need to determine the search order adaptively. On the computational side, obtaining any approximation to the optimal utility requires super polynomially many queries to the cost function, even for a strict subclass of submodular cost functions.
abstract_zh: 潘多拉问题是经济学中的一个基本模型，研究在昂贵的检查下的最优搜索策略。在本文中，我们开始研究组合成本的潘多拉问题，捕获许多搜索成本不可相加的现实生活场景。 Weitzman 的著名算法 [1979] 得出了显着的结果，即对于附加成本，最优搜索策略是非自适应的且计算上可行。我们询问这种结构和计算的简单性在多大程度上超出了附加成本函数的范围。我们的主要结果是，子模成本函数类承认遵循固定的、非自适应顺序的最优策略，从而保留了加性成本函数的结构简单性。相反，对于更一般的次加性（甚至 XOS）成本函数，最优策略可能已经需要自适应地确定搜索顺序。在计算方面，获得最佳效用的任何近似值都需要对成本函数进行超多项式的多次查询，即使对于子模成本函数的严格子类也是如此。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了组合成本下的Pandora问题，探讨了在非加性成本函数下最优搜索策略的结构和计算复杂性。
AI.motivation: 现实场景中搜索成本常为非加性，而传统加性成本下的Weitzman算法具有结构简单性。研究其在组合成本下的适用性具有理论与实际意义。
AI.method: 通过理论分析，比较子模（submodular）与次可加（subadditive/XOS）成本函数类的最优策略特性，结合计算复杂性分析。
AI.result: 子模成本函数允许非自适应搜索顺序保持结构简单性，而次可加函数需自适应策略；即使对子模函数的严格子类，近似最优解的计算也需要超多项式查询。
AI.conclusion: 组合成本下结构简单性仅在子模函数中保留，次可加函数需更复杂策略，且计算上存在显著挑战。

In this paper we initiate the study of Pandora's problem with combinatorial costs, capturing many real-life scenarios where search cost is non-additive. Weitzman's celebrated algorithm [1979] establishes the remarkable result that, for additive costs, the optimal search strategy is non-adaptive and computationally feasible. We inquire to which extent this structural and computational simplicity extends beyond additive cost functions. Our main result is that the class of submodular cost functions admits an optimal strategy that follows a fixed, non-adaptive order, thus preserving the structural simplicity of additive cost functions. In contrast, for the more general class of subadditive (or even XOS) cost functions, the optimal strategy may already need to determine the search order adaptively. authors_text: Jonas Rothfuss,Bhavya Sukhija,Tobias Birchler,Parnian Kassraie,Andreas Krause
title: Hallucinated Adversarial Control for Conservative Offline Policy Evaluation
title_zh: 保守的离线政策评估的幻觉对抗控制
comment: Conference on Uncertainty in Artificial Intelligence (UAI) 2023, first three authors contributed equally
comments: Conference on Uncertainty in Artificial Intelligence (UAI) 2023, first three authors contributed equally
abstract: We study the problem of conservative off-policy evaluation (COPE) where given an offline dataset of environment interactions, collected by other agents, we seek to obtain a (tight) lower bound on a policy's performance. This is crucial when deciding whether a given policy satisfies certain minimal performance/safety criteria before it can be deployed in the real world. To this end, we introduce HAMBO, which builds on an uncertainty-aware learned model of the transition dynamics. To form a conservative estimate of the policy's performance, HAMBO hallucinates worst-case trajectories that the policy may take, within the margin of the models' epistemic confidence regions. We prove that the resulting COPE estimates are valid lower bounds, and, under regularity conditions, show their convergence to the true expected return. Finally, we discuss scalable variants of our approach based on Bayesian Neural Networks and empirically demonstrate that they yield reliable and tight lower bounds in various continuous control environments.
summary: We study the problem of conservative off-policy evaluation (COPE) where given an offline dataset of environment interactions, collected by other agents, we seek to obtain a (tight) lower bound on a policy's performance. This is crucial when deciding whether a given policy satisfies certain minimal performance/safety criteria before it can be deployed in the real world. To this end, we introduce HAMBO, which builds on an uncertainty-aware learned model of the transition dynamics. To form a conservative estimate of the policy's performance, HAMBO hallucinates worst-case trajectories that the policy may take, within the margin of the models' epistemic confidence regions. We prove that the resulting COPE estimates are valid lower bounds, and, under regularity conditions, show their convergence to the true expected return. Finally, we discuss scalable variants of our approach based on Bayesian Neural Networks and empirically demonstrate that they yield reliable and tight lower bounds in various continuous control environments.
abstract_zh: 我们研究保守的离策略评估（COPE）问题，在给定由其他代理收集的环境交互的离线数据集的情况下，我们寻求获得策略性能的（严格）下限。在确定给定策略在现实世界中部署之前是否满足某些最低性能/安全标准时，这一点至关重要。为此，我们引入了 HAMBO，它建立在不确定性感知的过渡动态学习模型的基础上。为了形成对政策绩效的保守估计，HAMBO 在模型认知置信区域的范围内设想了政策可能采取的最坏情况轨迹。我们证明了由此产生的 COPE 估计是有效的下限，并且在规律性条件下，表明它们收敛于真实的预期回报。最后，我们讨论基于贝叶斯神经网络的方法的可扩展变体，并凭经验证明它们在各种连续控制环境中产生可靠且严格的下界。

AI.tldr: 本文研究保守离线策略评估问题，提出HAMBO方法通过生成对抗性轨迹以获得策略性能的可靠下界。
AI.motivation: 在部署新策略前需确保其满足安全标准，传统评估方法可能高估性能，需保守估计以避免风险。
AI.method: 基于不确定性感知的动态模型，HAMBO在模型置信区间内生成最坏情况轨迹，结合贝叶斯神经网络实现可扩展性。
AI.result: 理论证明估计值为有效下界且收敛于真实值，实验显示在多个连续控制环境中表现可靠且紧致。
AI.conclusion: HAMBO为离线策略评估提供安全保证，未来可扩展至更复杂场景的保守评估需求。

authors_text: Xiaoyang Yu,Youfang Lin,Xiangsen Wang,Sheng Han,Kai Lv
title: GHQ: Grouped Hybrid Q Learning for Heterogeneous Cooperative Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: GHQ：用于异构协作多智能体强化学习的分组混合 Q 学习
abstract: Previous deep multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) algorithms have achieved impressive results, typically in homogeneous scenarios. However, heterogeneous scenarios are also very common and usually harder to solve. In this paper, we mainly discuss cooperative heterogeneous MARL problems in Starcraft Multi-Agent Challenges (SMAC) environment. We firstly define and describe the heterogeneous problems in SMAC. In order to comprehensively reveal and study the problem, we make new maps added to the original SMAC maps. We find that baseline algorithms fail to perform well in those heterogeneous maps. To address this issue, we propose the Grouped Individual-Global-Max Consistency (GIGM) and a novel MARL algorithm, Grouped Hybrid Q Learning (GHQ). GHQ separates agents into several groups and keeps individual parameters for each group, along with a novel hybrid structure for factorization. To enhance coordination between groups, we maximize the Inter-group Mutual Information (IGMI) between groups' trajectories. Experiments on original and new heterogeneous maps show the fabulous performance of GHQ compared to other state-of-the-art algorithms.
summary: Previous deep multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) algorithms have achieved impressive results, typically in homogeneous scenarios. However, heterogeneous scenarios are also very common and usually harder to solve. In this paper, we mainly discuss cooperative heterogeneous MARL problems in Starcraft Multi-Agent Challenges (SMAC) environment. We firstly define and describe the heterogeneous problems in SMAC. In order to comprehensively reveal and study the problem, we make new maps added to the original SMAC maps. We find that baseline algorithms fail to perform well in those heterogeneous maps. To address this issue, we propose the Grouped Individual-Global-Max Consistency (GIGM) and a novel MARL algorithm, Grouped Hybrid Q Learning (GHQ). GHQ separates agents into several groups and keeps individual parameters for each group, along with a novel hybrid structure for factorization. To enhance coordination between groups, we maximize the Inter-group Mutual Information (IGMI) between groups' trajectories. Experiments on original and new heterogeneous maps show the fabulous performance of GHQ compared to other state-of-the-art algorithms.
abstract_zh: 以前的深度多智能体强化学习（MARL）算法通常在同质场景中取得了令人印象深刻的结果。然而，异构场景也很常见，而且通常更难解决。在本文中，我们主要讨论星际争霸多智能体挑战（SMAC）环境中的协作异构MARL问题。我们首先定义并描述了SMAC中的异构问题。为了更全面地揭示和研究这个问题，我们在原有的SMAC图的基础上添加了新的图。我们发现基线算法在这些异构地图中无法表现良好。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了分组个体全局最大一致性（GIGM）和一种新颖的 MARL 算法，分组混合 Q 学习（GHQ）。 GHQ 将代理分为几个组，并为每个组保留单独的参数，以及用于分解的新颖混合结构。为了增强群体之间的协调，我们最大化群体轨迹之间的群体间互信息（IGMI）。在原始和新的异构地图上进行的实验表明，与其他最先进的算法相比，GHQ 具有极好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为GHQ的分组混合Q学习算法，用于解决异构合作多智能体强化学习问题，特别是在Starcraft Multi-Agent Challenges（SMAC）环境中。
AI.motivation: 现有的深度多智能体强化学习（MARL）算法在同质场景中表现良好，但在更常见的异质场景中效果不佳，因此需要针对异质合作场景设计新的算法。
AI.method: GHQ通过将智能体分组并为每组保留独立参数，结合个体-全局-最大一致性（GIGM）和混合结构进行因子分解，同时通过最大化组间轨迹的互信息（IGMI）增强组间协调。
AI.result: 在原始和新设计的异质地图上的实验表明，GHQ相较于其他先进算法表现出色，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: GHQ通过分组策略和组间协调机制，有效解决了异质合作多智能体强化学习问题，为复杂场景提供了新的解决方案。

search_text: GHQ: Grouped Hybrid Q Learning for Heterogeneous Cooperative Multi-agent Reinforcement Learning GHQ：用于异构协作多智能体强化学习的分组混合 Q 学习 Previous deep multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) algorithms have achieved impressive results, typically in homogeneous scenarios. However, heterogeneous scenarios are also very common and usually harder to solve. In this paper, we mainly discuss cooperative heterogeneous MARL problems in Starcraft Multi-Agent Challenges (SMAC) environment. We firstly define and describe the heterogeneous problems in SMAC. In order to comprehensively reveal and study the problem, we make new maps added to the original SMAC maps. We find that baseline algorithms fail to perform well in those heterogeneous maps. To address this issue, we propose the Grouped Individual-Global-Max Consistency (GIGM) and a novel MARL algorithm, Grouped Hybrid Q Learning (GHQ). GHQ separates agents into several groups and keeps individual parameters for each group, along with a novel hybrid structure for factorization. To enhance coordination between groups, we maximize the Inter-group Mutual Information (IGMI) between groups' trajectories. Experiments on original and new heterogeneous maps show the fabulous performance of GHQ compared to other state-of-the-art algorithms. 以前的深度多智能体强化学习（MARL）算法通常在同质场景中取得了令人印象深刻的结果。然而，异构场景也很常见，而且通常更难解决。在本文中，我们主要讨论星际争霸多智能体挑战（SMAC）环境中的协作异构MARL问题。我们首先定义并描述了SMAC中的异构问题。为了更全面地揭示和研究这个问题，我们在原有的SMAC图的基础上添加了新的图。我们发现基线算法在这些异构地图中无法表现良好。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了分组个体全局最大一致性（GIGM）和一种新颖的 MARL 算法，分组混合 Q 学习（GHQ）。 GHQ 将代理分为几个组，并为每个组保留单独的参数，以及用于分解的新颖混合结构。为了增强群体之间的协调，我们最大化群体轨迹之间的群体间互信息（IGMI）。在原始和新的异构地图上进行的实验表明，与其他最先进的算法相比，GHQ 具有极好的性能。 本文提出了一种名为GHQ的分组混合Q学习算法，用于解决异构合作多智能体强化学习问题，特别是在Starcraft Multi-Agent Challenges（SMAC）环境中。 Xiaoyang Yu,Youfang Lin,Xiangsen Wang,Sheng Han,Kai Lv cs.MA,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Dieuwertje Alblas,Marieke Hofman,Christoph Brune,Kak Khee Yeung,Jelmer M. Wolterink
title: Implicit Neural Representations for Modeling of Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Progression
title_zh: 腹主动脉瘤进展建模的隐式神经表征
abstract: Abdominal aortic aneurysms (AAAs) are progressive dilatations of the abdominal aorta that, if left untreated, can rupture with lethal consequences. Imaging-based patient monitoring is required to select patients eligible for surgical repair. In this work, we present a model based on implicit neural representations (INRs) to model AAA progression. We represent the AAA wall over time as the zero-level set of a signed distance function (SDF), estimated by a multilayer perception that operates on space and time. We optimize this INR using automatically extracted segmentation masks in longitudinal CT data. This network is conditioned on spatiotemporal coordinates and represents the AAA surface at any desired resolution at any moment in time. Using regularization on spatial and temporal gradients of the SDF, we ensure proper interpolation of the AAA shape. We demonstrate the network's ability to produce AAA interpolations with average surface distances ranging between 0.72 and 2.52 mm from images acquired at highly irregular intervals. The results indicate that our model can accurately interpolate AAA shapes over time, with potential clinical value for a more personalised assessment of AAA progression.
summary: Abdominal aortic aneurysms (AAAs) are progressive dilatations of the abdominal aorta that, if left untreated, can rupture with lethal consequences. Imaging-based patient monitoring is required to select patients eligible for surgical repair. In this work, we present a model based on implicit neural representations (INRs) to model AAA progression. We represent the AAA wall over time as the zero-level set of a signed distance function (SDF), estimated by a multilayer perception that operates on space and time. We optimize this INR using automatically extracted segmentation masks in longitudinal CT data. This network is conditioned on spatiotemporal coordinates and represents the AAA surface at any desired resolution at any moment in time. Using regularization on spatial and temporal gradients of the SDF, we ensure proper interpolation of the AAA shape. We demonstrate the network's ability to produce AAA interpolations with average surface distances ranging between 0.72 and 2.52 mm from images acquired at highly irregular intervals. The results indicate that our model can accurately interpolate AAA shapes over time, with potential clinical value for a more personalised assessment of AAA progression.
abstract_zh: 腹主动脉瘤 (AAA) 是腹主动脉的进行性扩张，如果不及时治疗，可能会破裂并造成致命后果。需要基于成像的患者监测来选择适合手术修复的患者。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个基于隐式神经表征 (INR) 的模型来模拟 AAA 进展。我们将随时间变化的 AAA 墙表示为有符号距离函数 (SDF) 的零级集，通过作用于空间和时间的多层感知来估计。我们使用纵向 CT 数据中自动提取的分割掩模来优化此 INR。该网络以时空坐标为条件，并在任何时刻以任何所需的分辨率表示 AAA 表面。使用 SDF 的空间和时间梯度正则化，我们确保 AAA 形状的正确插值。我们展示了该网络能够根据以高度不规则的间隔获取的图像生成平均表面距离在 0.72 至 2.52 毫米之间的 AAA 插值。结果表明，我们的模型可以随着时间的推移准确地插值 AAA 形状，对于对 AAA 进展进行更个性化的评估具有潜在的临床价值。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于隐式神经表示（INR）的模型，用于建模腹主动脉瘤（AAA）的进展。
AI.motivation: 腹主动脉瘤若未及时治疗可能导致致命性破裂，需通过影像监测选择手术患者。现有方法在处理不规则时间间隔的影像数据时存在局限性，需更精确的建模手段。
AI.method: 将AAA壁表示为时空坐标下的符号距离函数（SDF）零等值线，利用多层感知机估计SDF，并通过纵向CT数据的自动分割掩码优化网络。采用空间和时间梯度正则化确保形状插值准确性。
AI.result: 模型在高度不规则时间间隔的影像数据中生成的AAA插值表面距离平均为0.72-2.52毫米，验证了其时间维度上的形状重建能力。
AI.conclusion: 该方法可准确模拟AAA随时间的变化过程，为个性化评估动脉瘤进展提供潜在临床价值。

authors_text: Colin G. West
title: AI and the FCI: Can ChatGPT Project an Understanding of Introductory Physics?
title_zh: AI 和 FCI：ChatGPT 能否预测对入门物理学的理解？
comment: Updated and expanded. 13 pages, 1 figure, 1 table
comments: Updated and expanded. 13 pages, 1 figure, 1 table
abstract: ChatGPT is a groundbreaking ``chatbot"--an AI interface built on a large language model that was trained on an enormous corpus of human text to emulate human conversation. Beyond its ability to converse in a plausible way, it has attracted attention for its ability to competently answer questions from the bar exam and from MBA coursework, and to provide useful assistance in writing computer code. These apparent abilities have prompted discussion of ChatGPT as both a threat to the integrity of higher education and conversely as a powerful teaching tool. In this work we present a preliminary analysis of how two versions of ChatGPT (ChatGPT3.5 and ChatGPT4) fare in the field of first-semester university physics, using a modified version of the Force Concept Inventory (FCI) to assess whether it can give correct responses to conceptual physics questions about kinematics and Newtonian dynamics. We demonstrate that, by some measures, ChatGPT3.5 can match or exceed the median performance of a university student who has completed one semester of college physics, though its performance is notably uneven and the results are nuanced. By these same measures, we find that ChatGPT4's performance is approaching the point of being indistinguishable from that of an expert physicist when it comes to introductory mechanics topics. After the completion of our work we became aware of Ref [1], which preceded us to publication and which completes an extensive analysis of the abilities of ChatGPT3.5 in a physics class, including a different modified version of the FCI. We view this work as confirming that portion of their results, and extending the analysis to ChatGPT4, which shows rapid and notable improvement in most, but not all respects.
summary: ChatGPT is a groundbreaking ``chatbot"--an AI interface built on a large language model that was trained on an enormous corpus of human text to emulate human conversation. Beyond its ability to converse in a plausible way, it has attracted attention for its ability to competently answer questions from the bar exam and from MBA coursework, and to provide useful assistance in writing computer code. These apparent abilities have prompted discussion of ChatGPT as both a threat to the integrity of higher education and conversely as a powerful teaching tool. In this work we present a preliminary analysis of how two versions of ChatGPT (ChatGPT3.5 and ChatGPT4) fare in the field of first-semester university physics, using a modified version of the Force Concept Inventory (FCI) to assess whether it can give correct responses to conceptual physics questions about kinematics and Newtonian dynamics. We demonstrate that, by some measures, ChatGPT3.5 can match or exceed the median performance of a university student who has completed one semester of college physics, though its performance is notably uneven and the results are nuanced. By these same measures, we find that ChatGPT4's performance is approaching the point of being indistinguishable from that of an expert physicist when it comes to introductory mechanics topics. After the completion of our work we became aware of Ref [1], which preceded us to publication and which completes an extensive analysis of the abilities of ChatGPT3.5 in a physics class, including a different modified version of the FCI. We view this work as confirming that portion of their results, and extending the analysis to ChatGPT4, which shows rapid and notable improvement in most, but not all respects.
abstract_zh: ChatGPT 是一个突破性的“聊天机器人”——一种建立在大型语言模型之上的人工智能界面，该模型经过大量人类文本语料库的训练来模拟人类对话。除了能够以合理的方式进行对话之外，它还因其能够胜任回答律师考试和 MBA 课程作业中的问题以及为编写计算机代码提供有用帮助的能力而受到关注。这些明显的能力引发了对 ChatGPT 的讨论，因为它既对高等教育的诚信构成威胁，又反过来作为一种强大的教学在这项工作中，我们对 ChatGPT 的两个版本（ChatGPT3.5 和 ChatGPT4）在第一学期大学物理领域的表现进行了初步分析，使用力概念清单（FCI）的修改版本来评估它是否能够正确回答有关运动学和牛顿动力学的概念物理问题。尽管其性能明显参差不齐，而且结果也有细微差别，但我们发现在介绍力学主题时，ChatGPT4 的表现已接近与专家物理学家无法区分的程度。在我们的工作完成后，我们注意到了参考文献 [1]，该参考文献在我们发表之前完成了对 ChatGPT3.5 在物理课中的能力的广泛分析，包括 FCI We 的不同修改版本。将这项工作视为确认他们的部分结果，并将分析扩展到 ChatGPT4，这在大多数（但不是所有）方面显示出快速且显着的改进。

AI.tldr: 本文评估了ChatGPT3.5和ChatGPT4在大学基础物理课程中的表现，使用改进版的力概念库存（FCI）测试其对运动学和牛顿动力学概念题的解答能力。
AI.motivation: 探讨AI工具如ChatGPT在高等教育中的潜力与风险，特别是在物理学教学中的应用价值及对学术诚信的影响。
AI.method: 通过改进版FCI测试题，对ChatGPT3.5和ChatGPT4进行概念性物理问题的解答评估，并与大学物理课程学生的中位数表现及专家物理学家的表现进行对比。
AI.result: ChatGPT3.5在部分指标上可达到或超越大学物理学生的中位数水平，但表现不均；ChatGPT4在入门力学主题上的表现接近专家物理学家，但仍有改进空间。
AI.conclusion: ChatGPT4在物理概念理解上显著优于3.5版本，但尚未完全达到专家水平，研究支持其作为教学工具的潜力，同时强调需谨慎对待其局限性。

search_text: AI and the FCI: Can ChatGPT Project an Understanding of Introductory Physics? AI 和 FCI：ChatGPT 能否预测对入门物理学的理解？ ChatGPT is a groundbreaking ``chatbot"--an AI interface built on a large language model that was trained on an enormous corpus of human text to emulate human conversation. Beyond its ability to converse in a plausible way, it has attracted attention for its ability to competently answer questions from the bar exam and from MBA coursework, and to provide useful assistance in writing computer code. These apparent abilities have prompted discussion of ChatGPT as both a threat to the integrity of higher education and conversely as a powerful teaching tool. In this work we present a preliminary analysis of how two versions of ChatGPT (ChatGPT3.5 and ChatGPT4) fare in the field of first-semester university physics, using a modified version of the Force Concept Inventory (FCI) to assess whether it can give correct responses to conceptual physics questions about kinematics and Newtonian dynamics. We demonstrate that, by some measures, ChatGPT3.5 can match or exceed the median performance of a university student who has completed one semester of college physics, though its performance is notably uneven and the results are nuanced. By these same measures, we find that ChatGPT4's performance is approaching the point of being indistinguishable from that of an expert physicist when it comes to introductory mechanics topics. After the completion of our work we became aware of Ref [1], which preceded us to publication and which completes an extensive analysis of the abilities of ChatGPT3.5 in a physics class, including a different modified version of the FCI. We view this work as confirming that portion of their results, and extending the analysis to ChatGPT4, which shows rapid and notable improvement in most, but not all respects. ChatGPT 是一个突破性的“聊天机器人”——一种建立在大型语言模型之上的人工智能界面，该模型经过大量人类文本语料库的训练来模拟人类对话。除了能够以合理的方式进行对话之外，它还因其能够胜任回答律师考试和 MBA 课程作业中的问题以及为编写计算机代码提供有用帮助的能力而受到关注。这些明显的能力引发了对 ChatGPT 的讨论，因为它既对高等教育的诚信构成威胁，又反过来作为一种强大的教学在这项工作中，我们对 ChatGPT 的两个版本（ChatGPT3.5 和 ChatGPT4）在第一学期大学物理领域的表现进行了初步分析，使用力概念清单（FCI）的修改版本来评估它是否能够正确回答有关运动学和牛顿动力学的概念物理问题。尽管其性能明显参差不齐，而且结果也有细微差别，但我们发现在介绍力学主题时，ChatGPT4 的表现已接近与专家物理学家无法区分的程度。在我们的工作完成后，我们注意到了参考文献 [1]，该参考文献在我们发表之前完成了对 ChatGPT3.5 在物理课中的能力的广泛分析，包括 FCI We 的不同修改版本。将这项工作视为确认他们的部分结果，并将分析扩展到 ChatGPT4，这在大多数（但不是所有）方面显示出快速且显着的改进。 本文评估了ChatGPT3.5和ChatGPT4在大学基础物理课程中的表现，使用改进版的力概念库存（FCI）测试其对运动学和牛顿动力学概念题的解答能力。 Colin G. authors_text: Maryam Viqar,Violeta Madjarova,Amit Kumar Yadav,Desislava Pashkuleva,Alexander S. Machikhin
title: Deep Learning based Segmentation of Optical Coherence Tomographic Images of Human Saphenous Varicose Vein
title_zh: 基于深度学习的人体隐静脉曲张光学相干断层图像分割
abstract: Deep-learning based segmentation model is proposed for Optical Coherence Tomography images of human varicose vein based on the U-Net model employing atrous convolution with residual blocks, which gives an accuracy of 0.9932.
summary: Deep-learning based segmentation model is proposed for Optical Coherence Tomography images of human varicose vein based on the U-Net model employing atrous convolution with residual blocks, which gives an accuracy of 0.9932.
abstract_zh: 基于U-Net模型，采用带残差块的空洞卷积，提出了针对人体曲张静脉光学相干断层扫描图像的基于深度学习的分割模型，其准确率达到0.9932。

AI.tldr: 基于深度学习的U-Net模型改进，用于分割人体下肢静脉曲张的光学相干断层扫描图像。
AI.motivation: 传统分割方法难以准确处理静脉曲张血管的复杂结构，需更高效的深度学习模型提升分割精度。
AI.method: 改进U-Net架构，结合空洞卷积与残差块，增强特征提取能力以适应OCT图像的高分辨率与细微结构。
AI.result: 模型在测试集上达到0.9932的分割准确率，显著优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效提升了静脉曲张OCT图像的分割性能，为临床诊断提供可靠工具。
